His recent work emphasizes luciferase enzymes and understanding light-producing mechanisms in marine organisms.

name: Jana Isanta-Navarro
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Jana Isanta-Navarro is a limnologist and evolutionary ecologist studying how freshwater organisms respond and adapt to human-induced environmental changes in lakes. Her research combines laboratory, mesocosm, and field experiments across molecular to ecosystem levels, with a focus on Daphnia zooplankton, phytoplankton stoichiometry, and food web interactions under varying nutrient and temperature conditions.

name: Jeppe Vinther
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jeppe Vinther is an Associate Professor studying regulatory properties of RNA modifications, particularly 5' RNA capping with non-canonical metabolites like FAD and NAD+ across cellular and viral systems. His lab develops computational and biochemical methods for transcriptome-wide detection of RNA modifications and structures, with recent discoveries on how viruses use unconventional RNA caps to evade immune detection.

name: Jes Søe Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jes Søe Pedersen studies the evolutionary biology of social insects, particularly ants, focusing on social evolution, population genetics, and behavioral polymorphisms. His research examines how social organization evolves, including phenomena like unicoloniality in invasive ant species and the genetic basis of caste differentiation.

name: Jing Tang
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Jing Tang is an Assistant Professor specializing in process-based ecosystem modelling with focus on understanding terrestrial ecosystem dynamics under climate change. Her research primarily addresses biogenic volatile organic compounds emissions, Arctic biogeochemistry, and carbon-water cycle interactions in northern ecosystems using integrated modelling approaches and observational data.

name: Jinzhong Lin
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Jinzhong Lin is an Assistant Professor of Microbiology at the University of Copenhagen studying CRISPR-Cas immune systems in archaea, particularly type III systems and the anti-CRISPR proteins that viruses use to evade them. His work focuses on understanding how archaeal viruses inhibit host CRISPR-Cas defenses and the molecular mechanisms underlying these host-pathogen interactions.

name: Joel Vizueta Moraga
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Joel Vizueta Moraga studies the genomic basis of ant evolution and biology through comparative genomics and bioinformatics, focusing on topics including horizontal gene acquisition, venom evolution, and symbiont interactions in ants and other insects.

name: Johan Gotthardt Olsen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: He is interested in the structure and function of protein molecules and their interactions, and as a protein crystallographer, his present focus is on intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs), which do not crystallize on their own.

name: Jonathan Donhauser
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Jonathan Donhauser studies soil microbial ecology and biogeochemistry, with a focus on how microbial communities respond to environmental stressors like drought and climate change. His research combines molecular techniques (gene expression, sequencing) with ecological approaches to understand microbial adaptations and their effects on soil carbon and nitrogen cycling.

name: Jonathan Z. Shik
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jonathan Z. Shik is a community ecologist studying how physiological traits govern species coexistence and performance in ants across environmental gradients in a changing climate. His research integrates fieldwork, lab experiments, genomics, and theory to investigate nutritional ecology, symbiotic interactions, and metabolic adaptations in ants and their fungal mutualists.

name: Josefin Stiller
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Josefin Stiller leads the Biodiversity Genomics lab at the University of Copenhagen, using genomic approaches to understand animal evolutionary history and biological diversity. Her research focuses on phylogenomics and systematics of marine organisms, particularly deep-sea invertebrates (annelids, amphisamytha) from hydrothermal vents and methane seeps, as well as conservation genomics of seahorses and other threatened species.

name: Joseph Nesme
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Joseph Nesme is a microbiologist studying antibiotic resistance in environmental bacteria, particularly in soil and gut microbiomes, using metagenomic approaches. His research focuses on understanding how resistance genes spread through microbial communities via horizontal gene transfer and plasmids, with emphasis on the broader environmental resistome.

name: Julien Colombani
title: Associate Professor
summary: Colombani studies how organs communicate to coordinate physiological responses, with a focus on intestinal stem cells, tissue regeneration, and the mechanisms by which growing tissues signal to the rest of the organism. His research uses Drosophila as a model system to understand organ homeostasis and inter-organ crosstalk.

name: Kaj Sand-Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Kaj Sand-Jensen is a professor of aquatic ecology at University of Copenhagen who studies freshwater and aquatic ecosystems, with particular focus on stream ecology, aquatic plant photosynthesis, eutrophication effects on lake vegetation, and ecosystem metabolism (photosynthesis, respiration, and carbon-oxygen dynamics) in streams and lakes.

name: Karsten Kristiansen
title: Professor
summary: Kristiansen is a leading microbiome researcher specializing in metagenomics and molecular biomedicine, with extensive work on human and animal gut microbiota, creation of comprehensive microbial reference genome catalogs, and investigation of microbiome-host interactions across multiple biological systems including humans, mice, and aquaculture species.

name: Kathrin Rousk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kathrin Rousk investigates the abiotic and biotic controls of nitrogen fixation in moss-cyanobacteria associations, particularly in pristine ecosystems like boreal forests and subarctic tundra. Her research explores how environmental factors promote or inhibit this ecosystem function and defines the ecological relationship between moss hosts and their colonizing cyanobacteria.

name: Kenneth Veland Halberg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kenneth Veland Halberg studies neuroendocrine regulation of physiological systems in insects, particularly how hormones and neuropeptides control water balance, ion homeostasis, and stress responses in model organisms like Drosophila and beetles. His research explores these mechanisms at cellular and molecular levels to understand organ function and has translational applications for developing eco-friendly pest control strategies.

name: Kelli Lynn Hvorecny
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Hvorecny investigates the structure-function mechanisms in proteins across domains of life, working at the interface of cell biology and structural biology. Her research focuses on enzyme assemblies and complexes, particularly bacterial virulence factors like epoxide hydrolases, and the fundamental principles of metabolic organization within cells.

name: Kirsten Seestern Christoffersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Christoffersen studies freshwater ecology in Arctic and alpine regions, with focus on plankton ecology, food web dynamics, and the impacts of cyanobacteria and climate change on lake ecosystems. She leads research on the Greenland Ecosystem Monitoring Programme and investigates how environmental changes affect freshwater biodiversity and ecosystem functioning.

name: Kolby Jeremiah Jardine
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kolby Jardine studies plant and microbial metabolism at the interface of biochemistry, ecology, and atmospheric sciences, developing field methods to quantify volatile organic compound fluxes, carbon dioxide exchange, and isotopic signatures from leaves to ecosystems in response to climate extremes.

name: Kresten Lindorff-Larsen
title: Professor
summary: Kresten Lindorff-Larsen studies the relationship between protein structure, dynamics, and function using computational methods integrated with experimental studies. His research focuses on understanding how proteins change shape to modulate function, with particular emphasis on disordered proteins, machine learning approaches, and molecular simulations.

name: Kristian Agmund Haanes
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kristian Agmund Haanes is an associate professor studying the molecular mechanisms and pathophysiology of migraine, with emphasis on calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) signaling in the trigeminal system. His research group uses experimental models to identify new therapeutic targets for migraine prevention and treatment, including investigations of sex differences and inflammatory pathways.

name: Lars Ellgaard
title: Professor
summary: Lars Ellgaard studies oxidative protein folding in the endoplasmic reticulum, investigating enzymes involved in this process and how ER redox enzymes and glutathione modulate ER conditions. His lab also focuses on ER-associated degradation (ERAD) and produces disulfide-rich cone snail peptides (conopeptides) for use as research tools and drug leads.

name: Lars Båstrup-Spohr
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lars Båstrup-Spohr is an aquatic ecologist studying biodiversity and community assembly of freshwater aquatic plants, with particular emphasis on understanding how aquatic plant communities are structured across large spatial scales and how to restore degraded freshwater ecosystems. His research combines macroecology, functional ecology, and restoration studies focusing on lakes and wetlands.

name: Laura Martinez Alvarez
title: Associate Professor
summary: Laura Martinez Alvarez studies archaea and their viruses using integrated molecular biology and bioinformatics approaches. Her research focuses on understanding fundamental processes in archaeal organisms and developing biotechnological applications of extremophiles, with particular emphasis on viral-host interactions and microbial communities in extreme environments.

name: Lone Rønnov-Jessen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lone Rønnov-Jessen is an Associate Professor studying cellular and developmental biology with a focus on breast tissue organization, cancer-associated stromal changes, and the role of fibroblasts and myofibroblasts in breast cancer development. Her work investigates stem cell hierarchies in mammary glands and epithelial-stromal interactions using three-dimensional culture systems.

name: Lotte Bang Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Lotte Bang Pedersen studies the molecular mechanisms of primary cilium assembly, disassembly, and function in coordinating developmental signaling pathways and tissue homeostasis, with emphasis on understanding diseases caused by defective cilia (ciliopathies) using mammalian cell cultures as model systems.

name: Marlene Reichel
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Marlene Reichel is a plant biologist specializing in RNA-protein interactions and microRNA-mediated gene silencing. Her research focuses on understanding mRNA-binding proteomes and the molecular mechanisms of microRNA activity in plant systems, particularly using Arabidopsis as a model organism.

name: Martin Willemoës
title: Associate Professor
summary: Martin Willemoës is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen who specializes in enzymology and structural biology, combining computational enzyme design with molecular evolution approaches based on genetic selection systems in E. coli and other bacteria. His group studies enzyme kinetics, protein-protein interactions, and protein-ligand interactions using advanced biophysical techniques.

name: Mathias Middelboe
title: Professor
summary: Mathias Middelboe is a Professor of Marine Biology at the University of Copenhagen who studies marine viral ecology, particularly bacteriophage-host interactions and their roles in microbial mortality, nutrient cycling, and biogeochemical processes. His research includes applications of phage therapy for controlling fish pathogens like Vibrio and Flavobacterium in aquaculture, and investigations of viral dynamics in deep-sea and benthic environments.

name: Michael Askvad Sørensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Michael Askvad Sørensen studies bacterial regulation systems at the transcription and translation levels, with a recent focus on how cells control tRNA concentrations during stress. His research in E. coli examines ribosome hibernation, the stringent response to nutrient deprivation, and stress-induced degradation of translation machinery components.

name: Michael James Texada
title: Associate Professor
summary: Michael Texada studies how nutrient signals and hormones regulate growth, metabolism, and developmental timing in Drosophila, with research spanning insulin signaling, gut endocrine signaling, and neuronal circuits controlling feeding and body composition. His work connects insect development to conserved mechanisms in mammals including puberty and metabolic regulation.

name: Michael Kühl
title: Professor
summary: Michael Kühl is a professor of aquatic microbial ecology at University of Copenhagen who studies microenvironmental controls of microbial communities, symbioses, and aquatic ecosystems using advanced microsensor and imaging techniques including photonics and optical sensors. His research spans coral reefs, biofilms, cyanobacteria, seagrass, and applications in 3D bioprinting with focuses on photosynthesis, oxygen dynamics, and microbial interactions at microscales.

name: Milena Timcenko
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Milena Timcenko is an Assistant Professor studying membrane protein structures and function using cryo-electron microscopy, with particular expertise in lipid transport proteins and ion channels. Her research focuses on atomic-level characterization of membrane proteins in native environments and the development of methods for sample preparation.

name: Mirna Perez-Moreno
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mirna Perez-Moreno is an Associate Professor studying how interactions between epithelial progenitor cells and their microenvironment regulate skin homeostasis, regeneration, and cancer development, using mouse genetics, cell biology, and live imaging techniques.

name: Nadja Møbjerg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Møbjerg's research focuses on comparative and integrative zoology with main focus on ion and water metabolism, complemented by detailed anatomical investigations and studies on the evolution of taxa, organs and molecules. Currently her lab focuses on tardigrades (water bears) and cryptobiosis, investigating molecular, anatomical and physiological adaptations that enable tardigrades to thrive in extreme and changing environments.

name: Niels Banhos Danneskiold-Samsøe
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Danneskiold-Samsøe is an Assistant Professor studying secreted peptide hormones and their signaling pathways in metabolic regulation, with expertise in computational protein structure prediction (AlphaFold) for ligand-receptor discovery and research on how adipose tissue factors regulate glucose metabolism and obesity-related metabolic disorders.

name: Niels-Ulrik Frigaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Niels-Ulrik Frigaard studies the ecophysiology and bioengineering of photosynthetic microorganisms including cyanobacteria, microalgae, and anoxygenic phototrophic bacteria. His research investigates photosynthesis mechanisms, CO2 metabolism, and the practical applications of these organisms for CO2 capture and biosynthesis of metabolites and pigments.

name: Nikolai Friberg
title: Professor
summary: Nikolai Friberg is a freshwater ecologist with over 30 years of research experience studying ecosystem functioning, biodiversity, and human impacts on rivers and freshwater systems. His work focuses on using biological monitoring approaches including macroinvertebrates and species life-history traits to assess freshwater ecosystem quality and restoration outcomes.

name: Patrícia Chrzanová Pecnerová
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Patrícia Pecnerová is an Assistant Professor studying the genomics of extinction and micro-evolutionary processes in large mammals using ancient DNA and modern genomic approaches. Her research investigates how populations respond to environmental change and extinction through temporal genetic analysis of species including woolly mammoths, elephants, and muskoxen.

name: Per Amstrup Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Per Amstrup Pedersen studies the structure, function, and production of membrane proteins with emphasis on transmembrane transporters and ion channels. His work combines protein expression systems, structural biology techniques like cryo-EM, and synthetic membrane systems to understand molecular mechanisms of membrane transport.

name: Per Juel Hansen
title: Professor
summary: Per Juel Hansen is a professor at the University of Copenhagen studying the physiology and ecology of marine planktonic organisms, with a focus on toxic microalgae, mixotrophic protists, and microbial community dynamics in marine environments.

name: Peter Brodersen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Brodersen studies post-transcriptional gene regulation and plant antiviral immunity, focusing on how plants defend against viruses through RNA interference pathways and covalent RNA modifications. His recent work revealed a dual-layer antiviral system where one pathway silently destroys viral RNA while another triggers immune activation when the first defense fails.

name: Pétur Orri Heiðarsson
title: Associate Professor
summary: Pétur Orri Heiðarsson studies transcription factors—dynamic protein molecules that regulate gene expression and chromatin architecture—using advanced single-molecule spectroscopy techniques. His research focuses on understanding how transcription factor structure and dynamics enable genome navigation and cell fate decisions, with particular emphasis on pioneer factors and intrinsically disordered proteins.

name: Rafael Pinilla Redondo
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rafael Pinilla-Redondo's research focuses on understanding how bacteria defend against phages through CRISPR-Cas and other bacterial immune systems, studying their molecular mechanisms to address fundamental biological questions and advance biotechnologies.

name: Rasmus Heller
title: Associate Professor
summary: Rasmus Heller is an Associate Professor studying molecular ecology and population genetics of wild mammal populations. His research combines genomic methods with evolutionary and conservation biology to understand population structure, demographic history, and genetic diversity in African wildlife and extinct species.

name: Riikka Rinnan
title: Professor
summary: Riikka Rinnan studies biogenic volatile organic compound (BVOC) emissions from natural ecosystems, particularly Arctic systems, and how climate warming affects these emissions. Her research explores the biological and chemical interactions of volatile substances across organisms and ecosystems, with implications for atmospheric chemistry and climate feedbacks.

name: Rodrigo Ibarra Chavez
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rodrigo Ibarra Chavez is an Assistant Professor at University of Copenhagen working on engineered bacteriophages and phage satellites for therapeutic and diagnostic applications against bacterial infections. His research focuses on understanding phage-host interactions, studying mobile genetic elements, and developing novel antimicrobial approaches including phage therapy and CRISPR-based systems.

name: Rouba Jneid
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rouba Jneid studies Drosophila intestinal stem cells and gut homeostasis, investigating how signaling pathways control stem cell fate, tissue regeneration, and immune responses using genetic and live imaging approaches.

name: Sarah Rennie
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sarah Rennie develops computational and machine learning methods to study RNA biology, with emphasis on how RNA modifications and RNA binding proteins regulate gene expression at post-transcriptional stages. She applies these approaches to transcriptomic datasets to understand cellular regulation and has research interests in applications to neurological disorders.

name: Stine Helene Falsig Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Stine Falsig Pedersen is a Professor of Cellular Transport Physiology whose research focuses on acid-base and nutrient homeostasis, particularly in the tumor microenvironment, and the structure-function of acid-base and lactate transporters like NHE1 and NBCn1. Her work investigates pH and lactate sensing mechanisms in health and disease, with applications to cancer biology and cellular signaling pathways.

name: Søren Rosendahl
title: Professor
summary: Søren Rosendahl studies community ecology, population genetics, and evolution of fungi with emphasis on biotrophic symbionts and parasites. His research focuses on arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi and soil-associated fungi, using molecular markers to assess diversity, population structure, and interactions between fungi and plants.

name: Søren Johannes Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Søren Sørensen studies social interactions in microbial populations, examining how microbes cooperate in biofilms and natural communities like the human gut and soil. His group applies molecular techniques including metagenomics, sequencing, and flow cytometry to uncover mechanisms of microbial community dynamics, genetic flow, and responses to environmental changes, with particular focus on antibiotic resistance transmission through mobile genetic elements.

name: Tarun Veer Singh Ahluwalia
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tarun Veer Singh Ahluwalia is an Associate Professor studying computational and RNA biology with expertise in genomics, bioinformatics, and biomarker discovery. His research focuses on genetic and molecular mechanisms underlying diabetes complications, asthma, and obesity, using genome-wide association studies, proteomics, and multi-omics approaches to identify disease pathways and biomarkers for precision medicine.

name: Thomas Wim Hamelryck
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Hamelryck is a specialist in Bayesian modeling and probabilistic machine learning applied to structural biology. His research focuses on using deep probabilistic programming languages (Pyro, Numpyro) and directional statistics to solve problems in protein structure prediction, protein evolution, and macromolecular structure analysis.

name: Urvish Trivedi
title: Associate Professor
summary: Urvish Trivedi is an Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen studying microbial interactions, polymicrobial infections, and biofilms with a focus on the human microbiome and bacterial pathogenesis. His research examines how pathogenic bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus form communities and develop resistance, with applications to clinical infections and childhood health outcomes like asthma.

name: Vasileios Voutsinos
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Vasileios Voutsinos studies protein degradation and proteostasis, focusing on systematic mapping of degrons (protein degradation signals) in human proteins and how genetic variants affect protein folding, stability, and disease relevance. His work uses large-scale screens and massively parallel sequencing to understand how proteins are recognized for degradation by the ubiquitin-proteasome system.

name: Vibe Hallundbæk Østergaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Vibe Hallundbæk Østergaard is an Associate Professor studying chromosomal fragile sites (unstable regions hosting extremely large genes) and the mechanisms causing genomic instability and mutations in these regions. Her research also investigates DNA repair pathways, replication stress responses, and extrachromosomal DNA formation in cancer and yeast cells.

name: Xi Wang
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Xi Wang is an Assistant Professor specializing in computational and RNA biology who applies whole genome sequencing and population genomics approaches to understand evolutionary relationships, domestication history, and genetic diversity in livestock populations.

name: Xiaodong Liu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Xiaodong Liu is an Assistant Professor in the Section for Computational and RNA Biology at the University of Copenhagen, working on population genetics, bioinformatics, and evolutionary biology. His research focuses on understanding evolutionary processes and speciation using genomic data, including studies on reproductive barriers, genetic differentiation between species, and population-level genetic patterns in mammals and plants.

name: Xu Peng
title: Professor
summary: Xu Peng studies archaeal viruses and microbial immunity, focusing on the evolutionary arms race between archaeal viruses and their CRISPR-Cas defense systems, as well as how viruses use anti-CRISPR proteins to evade host immune responses.

name: YONG ZHANG
title: Associate Professor
summary: Yong Zhang studies bacterial second messengers and alarmones—particularly (p)ppGpp—and their role in regulating bacterial stress responses, antibiotic persistence, and tolerance mechanisms. His research focuses on identifying and characterizing the molecular mechanisms bacteria use to survive antibiotics and environmental stresses, including interactions with bacteriophages.

name: Yi Jiao
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Yi Jiao studies how terrestrial ecosystems respond to climate change, focusing on volatile organic compound dynamics in soil, permafrost thaw effects, and microbial-plant interactions in Arctic and grassland environments.

name: Yuvaraj Bhoobalan
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Yuvaraj Bhoobalan-Chitty studies the molecular mechanisms of archaeal viruses and their interactions with CRISPR-Cas immune systems, with a focus on how viruses evade or inhibit host defenses. His research combines virology, molecular microbiology, and evolutionary biology using the Sulfolobus archaea-virus model system.

name: Serafim Bakalis
title: Professor
summary: Serafim Bakalis is a Professor of Dairy Technology at the University of Copenhagen who applies chemical engineering approaches to design and manufacture sustainable foods. His research focuses on using process modelling, novel manufacturing techniques like high hydrostatic pressure, and digital tools to engineer structured food products with controlled properties for dairy, plant-based proteins, and other food systems.

name: Kenneth Klingenberg Barfod
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kenneth Klingenberg Barfod is an associate professor studying the gut microbiome and its role in human health and disease, with particular focus on using fecal microbiota transplantation as a therapeutic intervention for anorexia nervosa. His research explores how microbial dysbiosis contributes to eating disorders through gut-brain signaling and examines microbiota restoration as a treatment strategy.

name: Giovanni Barone
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Giovanni Barone is an Assistant Professor at the University of Copenhagen's Department of Food Science specializing in dairy ingredient technology and food processing. His research focuses on optimizing dairy and plant-based food processing through membrane separation techniques, protein chemistry, and mineral characterization to develop sustainable alternative products with improved nutritional quality.

name: José Bonilla
title: Assistant Professor
summary: José Bonilla is an Assistant Professor specializing in food structure research, using advanced microscopy techniques and image analysis to investigate the microstructure of food materials and their relationship to rheological properties. His work focuses on understanding protein interactions, fat-water distributions, and structural transformations in both plant-based and traditional food systems.

name: Remko Marcel Boom
title: Professor
summary: Remko Boom works on improving mild processing methods by combining food science and artificial intelligence to understand plant proteins' behavior during processing, with a focus on sustainable plant-based food production. He studies how to best process ingredients from fermentation, including CO2-derived proteins, to achieve desirable food product properties while minimizing resource consumption.

name: Rasmus Bro
title: Professor
summary: Rasmus Bro is a professor specializing in chemometrics and multivariate data analysis methods, particularly tensor decomposition and multi-way analysis techniques. His work applies advanced computational methods to analyze complex spectroscopic and food science data, with applications across the food, pharmaceutical, and biotechnology industries.

name: Nilay Büdeyri Gökgöz
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Nilay Büdeyri Gökgöz studies how dietary components—particularly polyphenols, fermented foods, and seaweed—influence gut microbiota composition, microbial metabolite production, and immune function, with specific focus on parasitic infections in animal models. Their research integrates food microbiology with nutritional immunology to understand host-microbiota-pathogen interactions.

name: Tomasz Pawel Czaja
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Tomasz Czaja is an assistant professor at University of Copenhagen working on rapid spectroscopic techniques for monitoring food production processes and assessing food quality. His research focuses on implementing on-line and at-line spectroscopic methods combined with chemometrics algorithms to improve food quality while minimizing environmental footprint from production.

name: Paulina Deptula
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Paulina Deptula is a tenure-track assistant professor focusing on two main research areas: elucidating vitamin B12 (cobalamin) biosynthetic pathways in Propionibacterium freudenreichii and analyzing bacterial defense mechanisms against bacteriophages. She works on fortifying plant-based foods with naturally produced vitamin B12 through microbial fermentation to address nutritional challenges in plant-based diets.

name: Giuliano Marcelo Dragone
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dragone is an Associate Professor studying fermentation technology, bioprocess development, and the valorization of food industry side-streams and by-products. His research focuses on developing sustainable industrial bioprocesses using microorganisms (yeasts, bacteria, microalgae) for the production of value-added compounds from food waste and agricultural residues.

name: Patricia Duque Estrada
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Patricia Duque Estrada is a food scientist studying plant proteins and their nutritional quality, with particular focus on how food processing affects protein digestibility and oxidation in plant-based and meat analog products.

name: Søren Balling Engelsen
title: Professor
summary: Søren Balling Engelsen is a Professor of Biospectroscopy at University of Copenhagen who develops and applies high-throughput quantitative spectroscopic methods (NIR, NMR, IR, Raman) combined with advanced chemometrics to analyze food and biological samples for quality control, process monitoring, and metabolomic studies. His research focuses on developing sustainable analytical approaches for the food industry and understanding food composition impacts on human health through foodomics research.

name: Michael Bom Frøst
title: Associate Professor
summary: Michael Bom Frøst is an associate professor in food sensory innovation at the University of Copenhagen who studies how consumers perceive and enjoy food through sensory science. His research integrates food science with design thinking to understand consumer acceptance of novel foods (insects, plant-based proteins, fermented foods) and develop innovative, sustainable food products.

name: Sylvester Holt
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Sylvester Holt designs strategies to optimize flavor formation in sustainable foods through yeast and enzyme-based approaches, and explores molecular mechanisms connecting chemical perception to sensory experience using model systems with human olfactory receptors.

name: Anni Bygvrå Hougaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anni Bygvrå Hougaard researches dairy and ingredient technology, focusing on spray drying processes for acidified dairy products, protein-milk component interactions during food processing, and development of plant-based food alternatives with improved texture and functionality.

name: Bekzod Khakimov
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bekzod Khakimov is an Associate Professor focused on assessing food quality and nutritional value through foodomics and metabolomics. His research involves untargeted molecular screening of bio-fluids to understand food molecular composition and impacts on human health, with emphasis on optimizing analytical methods, developing algorithms for instrumental data processing, and applying multivariate chemometric analysis to extract information from complex omics data.

name: Jacob Judas Kain Kirkensgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kirkensgaard is a soft matter researcher who leads the Structural Food Physics and Soft Matter Self-Assembly group at the University of Copenhagen. His research focuses on mesoscale self-assembly and geometrically complex structures in soft matter systems using small-angle x-ray and neutron scattering techniques combined with molecular dynamics simulations, with applications spanning food science, biomacromolecules, and synthetic materials.

name: Lukasz Krych
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lukasz Krych investigates the association between gut microbiome composition and health/disease in human and animal models, with expertise in molecular sequencing (Illumina and Oxford Nanopore), classical microbiology methods, and detection of bacteria, yeast, and phages in food processing and pathogen contexts.

name: Rene Lametsch
title: Associate Professor
summary: Rene Lametsch is an Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen's Department of Food Science whose research focuses on food proteomics and enzyme technology, particularly in plant-based foods and how genetic variation and processing influence protein composition, functionality, and nutritional value. His work includes post-mortem protein degradation in meat, enzyme-modified plant proteins, and innovative fermentation methods using fungal mycelium to valorize food industry byproducts.

name: Dennis Sandris Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Dennis Sandris Nielsen is a professor studying microbial behavior in complex environments, particularly fermented foods and the relationship between diet, gut microbiome, and human health. His research focuses on using fermentation to optimize plant-based protein foods, manipulating gut microbiota composition through bacteriophages and dietary interventions, and understanding microbial metabolite production in food and gastrointestinal systems.

name: Vibeke Orlien
title: Associate Professor
summary: Vibeke Orlien studies how food processing methods, especially high hydrostatic pressure and fermentation, affect protein structure, functionality, and stability in plant-based and conventional foods. Her research focuses on understanding molecular-level changes to design improved functional food products and preserve nutritional compounds during processing.

name: Iben Lykke Petersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Iben Lykke Petersen researches the nutritional quality of plant proteins, focusing on how processing methods like extrusion and fermentation can improve protein digestibility and amino acid balance in plant-based foods. Her work spans in vitro digestibility analysis, characterization of plant-derived products, and strategies to reduce antinutritional factors to enhance the bioavailability of plant proteins for human nutrition.

name: Mikael Agerlin Petersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mikael Agerlin Petersen is an Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen specializing in food flavor analysis using chromatography and sensory methods. His research focuses on aroma volatile compounds across diverse food products and on off-flavor problems (especially geosmin) in aquaculture systems, with recent work on microbial geosmin producers in recirculating fish farms.

name: Beatriz Quintanilla Casas
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Beatriz Quintanilla Casas develops analytical and chemometric methods for food authentication and quality assessment, focusing on volatile compound profiling and flavor characterization. Her research applies advanced data analysis and GC-MS techniques to combat food fraud and optimize food design through data-driven approaches.

name: Torben Sølbeck Rasmussen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor specializing in developing bacteriophage-based therapies to manipulate the gut microbiome for treating gastrointestinal diseases, with research focus on using phages isolated from donor feces as therapeutic tools to reshape dysbiotic microbiota.

name: Åsmund Rinnan
title: Associate Professor
summary: Åsmund Rinnan is an Associate Professor specializing in exploratory data analysis and chemometrics applied to food science. His research focuses on using advanced spectroscopic methods combined with multivariate analysis to understand and track chemical changes in food proteins during processing and storage, particularly protein modifications like Maillard reactions and oxidation.

name: Jens Risbo
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jens Risbo is an Associate Professor specializing in food material science and physical chemistry, studying how physical transformations in food systems relate to chemical reactions. His research focuses on glass transitions, transport phenomena, and emerging applications of bacteria-based food structures for sustainable alternatives to traditional dairy products.

name: Henrik Siegumfeldt
title: Associate Professor
summary: Henrik Siegumfeldt is an Associate Professor working on food microbiology with expertise in studying microbial cells at the single-cell level using fluorescence-based imaging techniques. His research focuses on lactic acid bacteria, fermentation processes, food preservation mechanisms, and understanding how individual bacterial cells respond to stress conditions in food systems.

name: Veronica Marie Sinotte
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Veronica Marie Sinotte investigates how microbes and insects contribute to traditional fermentation practices, exploring microbial symbioses in social insects like ants and termites, and how their microbial communities have shaped the evolution and ecology of modern fermented foods.

name: Jakob Stokholm
title: Professor
summary: Jakob Stokholm is a Professor at the University of Copenhagen's Department of Food Science whose research focuses on understanding how the microbiome (bacteria and viruses) during pregnancy and early life develops and influences the risk of asthma and allergies in children. His work combines clinical translational research with bioinformatics approaches to investigate how factors like delivery mode, antibiotics, and diet shape the microbiome and whether microbiome manipulation in early life can prevent childhood disease.

name: Paraskevi Tsermoula
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Paraskevi Tsermoula is an Assistant Professor whose research focuses on dairy science and food analysis, particularly using metabolomics and analytical chemistry to characterize milk composition, valorize dairy by-products (whey and milk permeate), and assess dairy cow health through colostrum metabolite profiling.

name: Nanna Viereck
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nanna Viereck is an Associate Professor specializing in the development and application of NMR spectroscopy and metabolomics for characterizing food systems and quality. Her research focuses on using quantitative NMR and multivariate chemometric approaches to analyze diverse food matrices including lipids, carbohydrates, pectins, alginates, and whole foods like dairy, cereals, and beverages.

name: Deyong Zhu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Deyong Zhu is an Assistant Professor studying cell-cell and cell-environment interactions at micrometer scales, with a particular focus on understanding the organization and ecology of multispecies bacterial biofilms using microfluidics, multi-omics, and advanced imaging. He applies these insights to solve practical problems in bioremediation and biomedical applications like probiotic implant coatings.

name: Franciscus Winfried J van der Berg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen specializing in advanced chemometrics applied to food technology, with focus on measurement systems (primarily vibrational spectroscopy) for process monitoring and control in dairy and food production.

name: Pablo Alcón
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Pablo Alcón investigates how human cells repair DNA damages using cryogenic electron microscopy (cryo-EM) to study proteins involved in repair, with a focus on understanding disease development including Fanconi Anemia. He uses advanced structural and biochemical methods to study DNA repair directly inside human cells to understand genome stability and cancer mechanisms.

name: Ebbe Sloth Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Ebbe Sloth Andersen designs and constructs nanoscale devices using DNA and RNA as self-assembling building blocks, pioneering the RNA origami method that allows nanostructures to fold during transcription and be expressed in cells. His research applies these biomolecular design principles to create nanomechanical devices for biomedical and biotechnology applications, including biosensing, drug delivery, and metabolic engineering.

name: Gregers Rom Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Gregers Rom Andersen uses structural biology techniques including crystallography, cryo-EM, and biophysical methods to determine 3D structures of large protein complexes and study complement system activation in innate immunity, with translational focus on developing nanobody-based therapeutics for immune-related diseases.

name: Peter Ebert Andersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Peter Ebert Andersen studies how animal genomes defend themselves against genetic parasites and transposons, focusing on piRNA pathways, heterochromatin-dependent transcription, and the evolution of fertility-related proteins under natural selection in fruit flies.

name: Thomas Bataillon
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Bataillon studies the mechanisms and dynamics of evolution through genetic variation and adaptation, examining how natural selection shapes genomes over time. His research employs experimental evolution, population genomics, and statistical modeling across microbial, plant, human, and non-human primate systems.

name: Juraj Bergman
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Juraj Bergman is a bioinformatics researcher specializing in population genetics and genomics, developing models to infer evolutionary forces (selection, mutation, drift) and applying them to analyze genomic diversity in primates and large mammals, with recent focus on understanding population dynamics of megafauna species.

name: Søren Besenbacher
title: Associate Professor
summary: Søren Besenbacher is a computational genomicist who develops statistical and machine learning methods to study human mutation processes and cancer detection. His research focuses on understanding germline and somatic mutations, building mathematical models of mutational processes, and developing bioinformatics approaches to detect cancer from cell-free DNA (cfDNA) using fragmentation patterns and variant calling.

name: Nicolai Juul Birkbak
title: Professor
summary: Nicolai Juul Birkbak leads a translational cancer bioinformatics group focused on understanding interactions between the immune system and cancer using genomic and single-cell data. His research combines tumor molecular profiling with systemic immune health assessment to develop biomarkers for predicting treatment response and improving precision therapy in cancer patients.

name: Lotte Bjergbæk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lotte Bjergbæk investigates cellular responses to genomic insults during DNA replication, with emphasis on DNA repair, checkpoints, recombination, and replication fork integrity. She develops unique in vivo cellular systems to study how cells respond to replication fork stalling and DNA damage, using model systems like Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

name: Thomas Boesen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Thomas Boesen uses cryo-electron microscopy and structural biology to determine 3D structures of proteins and complexes involved in bacterial nanowires/microcables, bacterial pathogenesis, and neurotransmitter transporters, investigating electronic conductivity in biological cables and molecular mechanisms of disease factors.

name: Xavier Bofill De Ros
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Xavier Bofill De Ros is an Assistant Professor studying RNA metabolism in health and disease, with a focus on microRNA biogenesis, processing, modifications, and decay mechanisms. His research includes applications to RNA therapeutics and cancer biology, particularly how dysregulated microRNA maturation contributes to disease development.

name: Ditlev Egeskov Brodersen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Brodersen studies the molecular mechanisms enabling bacteria and other microorganisms to survive challenges like antibiotics and viruses, using structural biology and biochemistry to investigate toxin-antitoxin systems, stress response signaling, and microbial defense mechanisms with applications for developing new treatments against infectious diseases.

name: Andrii Bugai
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Andrii Bugai is an Assistant Professor studying gene regulation and RNA biology, with research focused on how cells control transcription, RNA turnover, and the fate of polyadenylated RNAs through mechanisms like transcription termination and nuclear RNA decay pathways.

name: Christian Kroun Damgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Kroun Damgaard investigates molecular mechanisms regulating gene expression during cellular stress, focusing on interactions between RNA-binding proteins and circular RNAs. His research emphasizes post-transcriptional regulation, including mRNA decay rates and translation control, with applications to cancer biology and neurological diseases.

name: Yuhui Dou
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Yuhui Dou is an Assistant Professor studying nuclear RNA metabolism, with a focus on how cap-binding proteins regulate the fate and processing of cellular RNAs. Her research uses protein interaction profiling to understand how protein complexes control mRNA biogenesis, export, and quality control.

name: Jan J. Enghild
title: Professor
summary: Jan J. Enghild is a professor studying protein chemistry and proteases, with a focus on understanding the structure-activity-function relationships of proteins. His research combines protein chemistry, proteomics, enzymology, and structural analysis methods to investigate extracellular matrix proteins, proteolytic enzymes and their inhibitors, and post-translational modifications, with particular emphasis on proteins involved in corneal disease and blood coagulation.

name: Leslie Foldager
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Leslie Foldager is a senior researcher specializing in animal science with expertise in statistical analysis, bioinformatics, and research design. His current work focuses on livestock welfare during transport, piglet health management, and understanding how diet and housing conditions affect animal physiology and behavior in cattle and pigs.

name: Manuel Frank
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Manuel Frank studies plant hormone signaling and development, particularly how cytokinin and peptide hormones regulate root nodule symbiosis in legumes. His research investigates the molecular mechanisms controlling the balance between nodule organogenesis and bacterial infection during legume-rhizobia symbiosis.

name: Rosaria Gandini
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rosaria Gandini studies protein structures and immune mechanisms, particularly focusing on antibody conformations and allergen-antibody interactions. Her recent work involves developing nanobody-based inhibitors for venom allergens and characterizing immunoglobulin E dynamics using cryo-electron microscopy and structural techniques.

name: Aleksandr Gavrin
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Aleksandr Gavrin studies plant-microbe symbiosis, particularly how legumes interact with nitrogen-fixing bacteria to improve agricultural sustainability. His research encompasses both the molecular mechanisms underlying symbiotic nitrogen fixation regulation and plant developmental processes involving actin dynamics.

name: Jakob Grove
title: Professor
summary: Jakob Grove is a Professor of Medical Bioinformatics and Statistical Genetics who develops and applies advanced statistical and bioinformatics methods to analyze large genetic datasets, primarily investigating the underlying biology of psychiatric disorders such as autism, ADHD, schizophrenia, and bipolar disorder through large-scale genomic studies.

name: Kira Gysel
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Kira Gysel is a structural biologist studying plant receptor-ligand binding mechanisms, particularly how plant LysM receptors recognize carbohydrate signals from symbiotic nitrogen-fixing bacteria and fungal pathogens. She uses structural techniques including X-ray crystallography and electron microscopy to elucidate the molecular basis of plant-microbe recognition and immune responses in legumes.

name: Rune Hartmann
title: Professor
summary: Rune Hartmann is a Professor of Innate Immunology at Aarhus University who studies how viruses are recognized by the immune system and how this recognition triggers antiviral responses. His research focuses on interferons (particularly type III interferons), RNA-sensing pathways, and the molecular mechanisms of innate immune signaling, using both mammalian and Drosophila model systems.

name: Andreas Holleufer
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Andreas Holleufer is an Assistant Professor studying RNA biology and innovation, with research focused on type I interferon signaling and innate immune mechanisms. His work investigates how cells detect viral and foreign nucleic acids through pattern recognition receptors, and how this triggers antiviral immune responses.

name: Erik Østergaard Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Erik Østergaard Jensen researches the regulation of plant genes involved in nitrogen fixation in legumes and the function of nonsymbiotic hemoglobin genes in relation to NO signaling. He has also investigated expressing human genes in plants for pharmaceutical applications, particularly for treating B12 deficiency.

name: Haja Kadarmideen
title: Professor
summary: Haja Kadarmideen is a Professor of Systems Biology and Bioinformatics at Aarhus University who applies genomics, multiomics, and systems biology approaches to animal breeding and reproductive biotechnology. His research focuses on integrating transcriptomics and genetics to improve animal production efficiency, health, welfare, and disease resilience, while developing advanced reproductive technologies like IVF for livestock.

name: Lilian Kisiswa
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lilian Kisiswa is a molecular neurobiologist studying cell signaling in the nervous system, with particular expertise in neurotrophin receptor signaling (especially p75NTR) and its role in cerebellar development, neuron survival, and cell death. Her recent work also includes cancer neurobiology research using advanced microfluidic systems to study brain tumor-endothelial cell interactions.

name: Taro Kitazawa
title: Associate Professor
summary: Taro Kitazawa investigates the molecular and epigenetic basis of neuroplasticity underlying memory formation and consolidation in mice, focusing on how activity-dependent gene regulation drives the plasticity of memory-encoding neuronal ensembles using advanced genomics and transcriptional profiling techniques.

name: Jørgen Kjems
title: Professor
summary: Jørgen Kjems is a professor specializing in RNA biology and nanotechnology who designs self-assembled DNA and RNA nanostructures for biosensing, drug delivery, and diagnosis of human diseases. His research focuses on non-coding RNAs, particularly microRNAs and circular RNAs, and their applications as biomarkers and therapeutic targets in cancer, neurological diseases, and tissue engineering.

name: Birgitta R. Knudsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Birgitta R. Knudsen studies DNA topoisomerase enzymes and develops DNA-based biosensors to detect the activity of these enzymes for diagnosing human diseases such as malaria and tuberculosis, combining molecular biology with nanoscience for single-molecule and single-cell level monitoring.

name: Charlotte Rohde Knudsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Charlotte Rohde Knudsen investigates the structural basis and functional relevance of macromolecular interactions involving bacterial translation elongation factors, with applications to understanding disease states and developing therapeutic strategies for disorders caused by nonsense mutations.

name: Emil Laust Kristoffersen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Emil Laust Kristoffersen studies the potential of nucleic acids—particularly RNA—to catalyze self-replication and drive evolution from scratch, with focus on RNA ribozymes, rolling circle synthesis mechanisms, and functional RNA structures with applications in biotechnology and therapeutics.

name: Joseph Lyons
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Joseph Lyons is a structural biologist studying protein-driven lipid transport mechanisms and membrane protein function, with particular focus on flippases and lipid asymmetry disorders in neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. His research employs cryo-electron microscopy to reveal atomic-level structures of membrane transport proteins and their roles in cellular membrane homeostasis.

name: Mette Galsgaard Malle
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mette Galsgaard Malle develops biophysical approaches using single-molecule fluorescence microscopy and DNA nanotechnology to study protein-membrane interactions and biomolecular delivery, with recent focus on α-synuclein oligomers and extracellular vesicle drug delivery.

name: Marcela Mendoza-Suárez
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Marcela Mendoza-Suárez researches plant-beneficial microbe interactions, focusing on identifying natural rhizobia bacteria for use as biofertilizers to promote sustainable agriculture. Her work combines molecular plant science with legume breeding and nitrogen fixation studies, with particular emphasis on improving crop productivity in high-protein crops like fava beans while reducing chemical fertilizer dependency.

name: Noëmie Mermet-Joret
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Noëmie Mermet-Joret investigates how hardwired amygdala circuits provide the scaffold for learning aversive experiences and fear behaviors, using advanced optogenetic techniques to precisely control and study neuronal activity patterns underlying emotional memory formation and behavioral responses.

name: Fiona Müllner
title: Associate Professor
summary: Fiona Müllner is a neuroscientist investigating how the brain's thalamus actively shapes and modifies visual information before it reaches the cortex. Her research combines advanced imaging, electrophysiology, and computational modeling to understand how thalamic inhibitory circuits transform retinal signals and integrate top-down signals related to attention and behavior.

name: Sadegh Nabavi
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sadegh Nabavi investigates how synaptic and circuit-level plasticity in the brain relates to learning, memory formation, and consolidation using optogenetics, electrophysiology, and two-photon imaging in rodent models. His research focuses on establishing causal links between changes in synaptic strength and memory persistence, particularly in fear-related memories and the amygdala circuit.

name: Marcin Nadzieja
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Marcin Nadzieja is an Assistant Professor studying legume genomics and genetics, with expertise in microscopy. His research focuses on understanding legume-rhizobium symbiotic interactions, particularly the genetic and cellular mechanisms controlling nitrogen-fixing root nodule formation and bacterial infection processes.

name: Dragos Niculescu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dragos Niculescu develops differentiation protocols for generating mesencephalic dopaminergic neurons from human pluripotent stem cells, with applications for regenerative medicine and neurodegeneration disease models.

name: Michael Lund Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Michael Lund Nielsen is a proteomics researcher who develops mass spectrometry-based methods to characterize post-translational modifications globally across the proteome, with applications to understanding DNA damage response and cell cycle regulation.

name: Nadia Sukusu Nielsen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Dr. Nielsen is a protein biochemist who investigates protease inhibitor mechanisms and protein-misfolding diseases, particularly TGFBI-linked corneal dystrophy and amyloidosis. Her research combines structural characterization, proteolytic processing analysis, and engineering of protease inhibitors to understand disease mechanisms and develop therapeutics.

name: Ulf Andersson Vang Ørom
title: Associate Professor
summary: Ørom studies nuclear non-coding RNA processing, focusing on how miRNAs and long non-coding RNAs are processed and regulate gene expression through protein-RNA interactions. His recent work emphasizes RNA modifications in disease contexts, particularly G-quadruplex structures and their role in transcriptional regulation and therapeutic targeting.

name: Daniel Erik Otzen
title: Professor
summary: Daniel Erik Otzen is a Professor of Nanobiotechnology at Aarhus University who investigates protein conformational changes, aggregation, and fibrillation through biophysical approaches. His research focuses on understanding pathological protein misfolding in neurodegenerative diseases (particularly Parkinson's), functional amyloids in bacteria, membrane protein stability, and the development of therapeutic strategies including enzymatic plastic degradation and drug delivery systems.

name: Claus Oxvig
title: Professor
summary: Claus Oxvig studies the regulation of insulin-like growth factor (IGF) signaling through protease enzymes, particularly PAPP-A and PAPP-A2, which cleave IGF binding proteins to modulate growth factor activity. His research focuses on these regulatory mechanisms in aging, fetal development, pregnancy, and age-related diseases.

name: Bjørn Panyella Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Pedersen is a structural biologist focused on understanding molecular mechanisms of transmembrane transporters in plants, using cryo-EM and crystallography to characterize how cells transport hormones, sugars, and sterols across membranes. His research combines structural biology with biochemical approaches to elucidate transporter function and regulation for applications in plant biotechnology and human health.

name: Christian Nørgaard Storm Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Storm Pedersen develops and implements efficient computational algorithms for bioinformatics with emphasis on analyzing biological sequences and their evolutionary dynamics. His work focuses on algorithmic problems in comparative genomics, phylogenetic inference, and machine learning applications including probabilistic models.

name: Jakob Skou Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Jakob Skou Pedersen is a computational biologist focused on understanding cancer evolution and gene regulation through statistical methods, big data analysis, and probabilistic modeling. His research emphasizes post-transcriptional regulation, non-coding RNAs, and the application of bioinformatics to clinical cancer genomics.

name: Lene Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lene Pedersen studies type III inorganic phosphate transporters and their physiological and pathological roles, particularly focusing on their involvement in neurobiology and vascular calcification. Her research investigates how phosphate transporters regulate mineralization in normal and disease states, including their effects on neuronal function and pathological calcifications.

name: Jan Trige Rasmussen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Jan Trige Rasmussen studies protein structure and function with a primary focus on milk fat globule membrane (MFGM) proteins in bovine milk, including lactadherin and other lipophilic proteins, as well as intracellular lipid transport and metabolism.

name: Alena Salasova
title: Associate Professor
summary: Alena Salasova is an Associate Professor studying neurodevelopment and molecular mechanisms underlying nervous system development and disease. Her research focuses on dopaminergic circuits, receptor signaling pathways (particularly VPS10p-Domain receptors and Wnt signaling), and their roles in development, neurodegeneration, and Alzheimer's disease, using advanced imaging techniques like light-sheet microscopy.

name: Niels Nørgaard Sandal
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Niels Nørgaard Sandal is a molecular geneticist specializing in the genetic and molecular mechanisms of legume-rhizobia symbiosis, particularly using the model plant Lotus japonicus to understand how plants perceive bacterial signals and form nitrogen-fixing root nodules. His work has contributed significantly to establishing genetic resources and identifying key plant genes involved in nodule organogenesis and symbiotic nitrogen fixation.

name: Ragnhild Bager Skjerning
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Ragnhild Bager Skjerning investigates the structural and functional mechanisms of bacterial toxin-antitoxin systems, particularly how transcription factors and protein complexes regulate gene expression through DNA binding. Her research uses structural biology, biophysics, and computational methods to understand transcriptional regulation in bacteria.

name: Esben Skipper Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Esben Skipper Sørensen is a professor studying bioactive milk proteins, particularly osteopontin, focusing on their structure, posttranslational modifications, and biological functions including cellular adhesion, immune responses, and bone mineralization processes.

name: Tinna V. Stevnsner
title: Professor
summary: Tinna V. Stevnsner is a Professor of Molecular Ageing Research who investigates the relationship between genomic instability, DNA repair mechanisms, and aging in mammals, with particular focus on mitochondrial genome maintenance and how DNA damage accumulation contributes to aging-related diseases including neurodegeneration and cognitive decline.

name: Charlott Stock
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Charlott Stock is an assistant professor studying the structure and function of membrane proteins, particularly ion pumps and transporters, using techniques like cryo-electron microscopy and structural biology approaches to understand how these proteins regulate osmotic balance and cellular signaling.

name: Chao Sun
title: Associate Professor
summary: Chao Sun investigates protein machines managing molecular logistics at brain synapses using quantitative spatial proteomics approaches, with a focus on how molecular supply constraints affect synaptic function and may seed neurological diseases. His lab combines in situ and ex situ spatial proteomics to map protein synthesis, degradation, and molecular homeostasis across numerous synapses.

name: Cinzia Tesauro
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Cinzia Tesauro studies DNA-modifying enzymes (particularly topoisomerases) as targets for anticancer drugs and diagnostic biomarkers, with research extending to cancer stem cell biology, chemotherapy resistance mechanisms, and development of sensitive molecular detection platforms. Her work combines enzyme characterization, cell-based assays, and advanced diagnostic technologies to understand drug response and disease mechanisms in cancer and infectious diseases.

name: Bo Thomsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bo Thomsen studies the genetics and genomics of phenotypic variation in farm animals, focusing on mapping quantitative trait loci affecting health and production traits, identifying disease-causing mutations in hereditary disorders, and characterizing microRNA expression in muscle tissue related to growth, differentiation, and exercise.

name: Daan van Aalten
title: Professor
summary: Professor van Aalten studies protein O-GlcNAcylation, a critical sugar modification of proteins essential for embryonic development and linked to intellectual disability disorders. His research also targets pathogenic fungal cell wall synthesis for novel antifungal drug discovery.

name: Vera Anna van der Weijden
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Van der Weijden uses embryonic diapause—the reversible pausing of pre-implantation embryos—as a model system to understand the molecular drivers of reproductive success and fertility. Her research investigates how embryos maintain dormancy through metabolic regulation and how they interact with the receptive endometrium during implantation.

name: Gilles Claude Vanwalleghem
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Gilles Vanwalleghem studies the bidirectional communication between the gut microbiome and nervous system using larval zebrafish as a model, employing calcium imaging and whole-brain mapping techniques to understand how dysregulation of this axis affects behavior and mental health disorders.

name: Bjarni Jóhann Vilhjálmsson
title: Professor
summary: Professor Vilhjálmsson is a statistical geneticist who develops and applies computational methods to understand how genetic and environmental factors interact to determine disease risk, particularly focusing on psychiatric disorders. His research includes developing polygenic scoring methods, conducting genome-wide association studies, and integrating large-scale genetic data with health registers to advance disease prediction and understanding of complex traits.

name: Palle Villesen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Palle Villesen is a bioinformatician and statistician specializing in applied machine learning, data science, and high-dimensional data analysis with applications in clinical medicine and forensic science. His research focuses on developing novel ML techniques for medical diagnostics and forensic applications, including metabolomics analysis, 3D dental imaging, and genomic data interpretation.

name: Kathryn Jayne Williams
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Kathryn Williams studies nuclear RNA quality control mechanisms, focusing on how exosome-targeting adaptors (NEXT and PAXT) interact with transcription termination machinery to degrade aberrant RNA transcripts in human cells.

name: Christian Würtz Heegaard
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Christian Würtz Heegaard is a molecular nutritionist studying bioactive components in milk and their physiological effects, with particular focus on vitamin B12 bioavailability and the role of the NPC2 protein in cellular cholesterol trafficking and lysosomal disease.

name: Peter Zeller
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Peter Zeller develops single-cell multi-omics technologies to understand how cell-type-specific epigenomes emerge during mammalian development through transcription factor-DNA interactions and histone modification dynamics. His lab combines perturbation experiments and chromatin profiling innovations (sortChIC, Dam&ChIC) to study epigenetic regulation in developmental systems including mouse embryonic stem cells and gastruloids.

name: Lorena Zuzic
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Lorena Zuzic is a computational biophysicist who uses molecular dynamics simulations to study how transport proteins function in cells, particularly focusing on membrane proteins and viral structures, with applications to drug discovery and plant biology.

name: Torben Asp
title: Professor
summary: Torben Asp is a professor working on crop genetics and genomics with focus on harnessing large and complex plant genomes using genetics, genomics, and bioinformatics approaches. His research develops statistical models and breeding strategies for quantitative traits in grasses, particularly ryegrass, with applications to disease resistance and biomass production.

name: Bjarne Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bjarne Nielsen specializes in statistical methods for animal and plant breeding with a focus on genomic selection, developing predictive models for economically important traits in livestock (particularly pigs) and crops. His research spans variance component estimation, breeding value prediction using genomic information, and genetic control of production traits including feed efficiency, survival, and crop yield.

name: Elesandro Bornhofen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Elesandro Bornhofen is an assistant professor at Aarhus University's Center for Quantitative Genetics and Genomics working on plant genetics and genomic prediction for crop improvement. His research focuses on quantitative genetics in forage crops, legumes, and cereals, using advanced genomic methods and multi-omics integration to develop climate-resilient varieties and improve breeding efficiency.

name: Alban Etienne René Bouquet
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Alban is a quantitative geneticist with a background in animal science, focusing on genomic selection, feed efficiency in pigs, selection criteria and breeding objectives, breeding plans optimization, and tools for breeders capitalizing on genomic data. He worked for 8 years as an engineer/consultant at IFIP - Pig Research Institute where he contributed to the implementation of genomic selection and practical breeding decision tools in French pig breeding schemes.

name: Albert Johannes Buitenhuis
title: Associate Professor
summary: Albert Johannes Buitenhuis studies the genetic architecture of quantitative traits in livestock using QTL mapping, genome-wide association studies, and gene-expression profiling. His research spans animal breeding genetics in cattle and poultry, with major focus areas including feather pecking behavior in laying hens, dairy cattle milk composition (fatty acids, minerals, oligosaccharides), and behavioral genetics.

name: Zexi Cai
title: Associate Professor
summary: Zexi Cai is an Associate Professor studying quantitative genetics and genomics in livestock animals (cattle, pigs) using large-scale genome-wide association studies and genotype-tissue expression resources to understand the genetic basis of complex traits and improve animal breeding programs.

name: Thinh Tuan Chu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Thinh Tuan Chu develops statistical and genomic methods to optimize commercial breeding programs for plants and animals, with focus on addressing genotype-by-environment interactions and using stochastic simulation and genomic prediction tools to improve genetic gain while reducing agrochemical usage and tackling climate change.

name: Lingzhao Fang
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Lingzhao Fang is an assistant professor at Aarhus University's Center for Quantitative Genetics and Genomics who leads research on farm animal genomics and gene expression. His work focuses on building comprehensive genomic resources like FarmGTEx to map genetic regulatory variants in livestock species and understand how genetic variation influences complex traits and gene expression patterns across tissues.

name: Grum Gebreyesus
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Grum Gebreyesus is a livestock geneticist and phenomicist who develops computer vision and AI technologies for automated animal trait measurement and selective breeding, with applications ranging from dairy cattle genetics to insect farming.

name: Quentin Geissmann
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Quentin Geissmann combines insect physiology and computational biology to develop hardware and software for monitoring insects in agricultural and ecological contexts using machine learning and computer vision. His research focuses on automated insect detection, identification, and behavioral analysis to enable sustainable agriculture and biodiversity conservation.

name: Irene Julca
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Irene Julca is an Assistant Professor at Aarhus University's Center for Quantitative Genetics and Genomics who combines comparative genomics and machine learning to understand plant evolution and improve crop traits. Her research focuses on phylogenomic analysis of domesticated plants and the evolutionary mechanisms underlying complex agronomic traits.

name: Emre Karaman
title: Associate Professor
summary: Emre Karaman is a statistical geneticist developing methodologies for including genetically admixed populations in genomic research, with applications to precision agriculture and medicine. His work focuses on improving genomic prediction accuracy in livestock using novel computational approaches.

name: Morten Kargo
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Morten Kargo is a senior researcher focusing on quantitative genetics and genomics applied to dairy cattle, developing breeding schemes and genetic evaluation methods while investigating traits like feed efficiency, lactation performance, and inbreeding management.

name: Bingjie Li
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bingjie Li conducts research in quantitative genetics and genomics, with focus on transcriptome-wide association studies (TWAS) to understand genetic mechanisms of complex traits in farm animals, particularly dairy cattle. His work includes developing tools for gene expression prediction and studying feed efficiency and genetic parameters in livestock breeding.

name: Huiming Liu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Huiming Liu develops statistical methods and breeding strategies to balance genetic gain with inbreeding control in livestock, particularly dairy cattle, using genomic data and selection optimization approaches.

name: Peter Løvendahl
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Peter Løvendahl is a senior researcher in quantitative genetics and genomics at Aarhus University who focuses on developing genetic selection strategies for dairy cattle breeding. His work centers on improving feed efficiency, reducing methane emissions, and enhancing animal welfare through genomic selection models, statistical methods, and analysis of livestock production traits.

name: Luc Janss
title: Professor
summary: Luc Janss develops Bayesian statistical methods for analyzing genetic and genomic data, with focus on genome-wide association studies, gene mapping, genomic prediction, and multi-trait approaches in plant and animal breeding as well as human health applications.

name: Just Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Just Jensen works on research towards development and use of quantitative genetic methods in commercial breeding programs in animals and plants. His research focuses on development of statistical models to be used in animal and plant breeding, studies of the genetic basis for different traits and diseases, and management of genetic resources.

name: Per Madsen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Per Madsen is a pioneer in quantitative genetics and statistical methods for animal breeding, specializing in variance component estimation, genomic prediction, and breeding value prediction in dairy cattle. He developed the DMU software package, a widely-used statistical tool for large-scale genetic analyses in animal breeding across cattle, pigs, and other livestock species.

name: Hanne Marie Nielsen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Hanne Marie Nielsen is a senior researcher specializing in quantitative genetics applied to sustainable animal production across multiple species. She develops breeding plans and simulation tools for dairy cattle, aquaculture (particularly fish like zander), farmed insects, and other livestock to optimize production traits while considering welfare and sustainability.

name: Vivi Hunnicke Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Vivi Hunnicke Nielsen is an Associate Professor specializing in quantitative genetics and genomics, with expertise in QTL detection, breeding selection, and feed efficiency in livestock species including mink, dairy cattle, and mice. Her research focuses on identifying genetic components affecting production traits and developing breeding strategies for improved performance in farm animals.

name: Guillaume Ramstein
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Guillaume Ramstein uses quantitative genetics and machine learning approaches to identify genetic variants that improve crop performance. His research focuses on predicting how DNA sequence changes affect gene regulation and plant resilience to environmental stresses, with applications to sustainable agriculture and climate adaptation.

name: Goutam Sahana
title: Professor
summary: Goutam Sahana develops and applies statistical genetic models to estimate population parameters and identify major genes affecting complex traits. His research focuses on GWAS methodology, fine-mapping genomic regions, and identifying QTLs for traits like fertility and disease resistance in livestock, particularly dairy cattle and pigs.

name: Peter Sørensen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Peter Sørensen develops and implements statistical methods for leveraging genetic data to predict complex traits and diseases. His work focuses on Bayesian modeling for gene mapping, heritability estimation, and genomic prediction in both human health (particularly Type 2 diabetes) and livestock breeding applications.

name: Doug Speed
title: Professor
summary: Doug Speed develops statistical methods and software tools for analyzing human genetic data, particularly for genome-wide association studies (GWAS) and genetic prediction. His main focus is improving methods for estimating SNP heritability, identifying genetic variants associated with complex traits, and constructing more accurate polygenic risk scores for personalized medicine applications.

name: Rasmus Bak Stephansen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Rasmus Bak Stephansen is an agrobiologist specializing in quantitative genetics who develops genetic tools to improve feed efficiency and reduce greenhouse gas emissions in cattle through systematic breeding. His research focuses on breeding more sustainable dairy cattle that better utilize their feed while maintaining milk production and reducing methane emissions.

name: Guosheng Su
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Guosheng Su is a senior researcher at the Center for Quantitative Genetics and Genomics (QGG) at Aarhus University who specializes in genomic prediction and genetic improvement of livestock.
