His work focuses on integrating genome-wide association studies (GWAS) and transcriptomic resources like PigGTEx to understand the genetic basis of complex traits in pigs and cattle, particularly for breeding applications.

name: Viktor Milkevych
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Viktor Milkevych develops statistical methods and computational tools for quantitative genetics and genomics, with applications to animal breeding, dairy cattle health monitoring through sensor systems and machine learning, and mechanistic modeling of complex trait formation.

name: Roos Marina Zaalberg
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Zaalberg is an Assistant Professor at the Center for Quantitative Genetics and Genomics focusing on genetic analysis and breeding programs for livestock, particularly pigs and dairy cattle. Her research applies quantitative genetic methods to improve breeding outcomes in organic farming systems and estimate heritability of traits like piglet survival and milk composition.

name: Dan Stærk
title: Professor
summary: Dan Stærk is a professor who develops analytical techniques for discovering bioactive peptides and small molecules from natural sources (plants, marine organisms, fungi) as drug leads for treating type 2 diabetes, infectious diseases, and cancer, with expertise in NMR spectroscopy and advanced bioanalytical technologies for natural products research.

name: Flemming Steen Jørgensen
title: Professor
summary: Flemming Steen Jørgensen is a professor specializing in computational chemistry and molecular modeling for drug design. His research focuses on using molecular dynamics simulations and computational methods to understand drug metabolism through cytochrome P450 enzymes, predict structure-activity relationships, and enable structure-based drug design through protein modeling and chemoinformatics approaches.

name: Jesper Langgaard Kristensen
title: Professor
summary: Kristensen develops synthetic methodology and chemical probes to study serotonin receptors and other pharmacological targets, with a major focus on 5-HT2A receptor agonists derived from psychedelics for psychiatric applications including depression treatment. His work spans from basic medicinal chemistry to preclinical pharmacology and radiopharmaceutical imaging.

name: Jette Sandholm Jensen Kastrup
title: Professor
summary: Professor Kastrup leads a major research group in structural biology of ionotropic glutamate receptors and G protein-coupled receptors, with over 100 determined structures. The group focuses on crystal structure determination and structural studies of receptor-ligand interactions to inform drug design, with applications to neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer's and epilepsy.

name: Lasse Kristoffer Bak
title: Professor
summary: Professor Bak conducts translational research on biomarker biology for disease diagnosis and treatment monitoring, with particular focus on bone-related diseases and brain metabolism. He develops clinical pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic models using therapeutic drug monitoring data to enable personalized model-based dosing of drugs.

name: Matthias Manfred Herth
title: Professor
summary: Herth develops radiopharmaceuticals from bench to clinical use, specializing in radioactive labeling strategies (particularly PET nuclides carbon-11 and fluorine-18) and targeted radionuclide therapies for imaging brain diseases and treating cancers. His research applies bioorthogonal chemistry and creates imaging agents for CNS-related targets like GPCRs, ion channels, and enzymes.

name: Miriam Kolko
title: Professor
summary: Professor Kolko is a clinician-scientist specializing in translational eye research and glaucoma management, bridging clinical practice with basic and translational research to understand glaucomatous neurodegeneration and develop new therapeutic approaches including neuroprotective agents.

name: Morten Lindow
title: Professor
summary: Morten Lindow is a computational drug discovery pioneer specializing in oligonucleotide therapeutics and microRNA-targeted drugs, with 17 years of experience leading RNA drug discovery projects. His current focus integrates data science and bioinformatics into pharmaceutical research and education, particularly through managing the Center for Pharmaceutical Data Science Education at the University of Copenhagen.

name: Morten Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Morten Andersen is an expert in pharmacoepidemiology and pharmacovigilance with over 25 years of experience using national healthcare registers and real-world data to study drug utilization patterns, safety surveillance, and medication adherence across populations. His research includes proactive drug safety monitoring, signal detection, multi-country database network studies, and applications of machine learning to healthcare data analytics.

name: Nicholas M I Taylor
title: Professor
summary: Nicholas M I Taylor studies the structural biology of molecular machines, focusing on how membrane transport is achieved and regulated. His research uses cryo-electron microscopy to investigate systems including ABC transporters, bacteriophage injection mechanisms, and contractile tail systems, with applications to drug design and understanding viral-bacterial interactions.

name: Stefan Vogel
title: Professor
summary: Stefan Vogel develops biomolecular drug delivery technologies focusing on DNA, RNA, and peptide-based therapeutics. His research emphasizes synthesizing modified nucleic acids and peptides for targeted cell and tissue recognition to create personalized medicines with reduced side effects.

name: Trond Ulven
title: Professor
summary: Trond Ulven is a medicinal chemist focusing on G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) drug discovery, with particular emphasis on lipid-activated free fatty acid receptors for metabolic and inflammatory diseases like type 2 diabetes. His research integrates ligand design and synthesis with pharmacological evaluation to identify novel therapeutic compounds.

name: Alexander Sebastian Hauser
title: Associate Professor
summary: Alexander Hauser integrates large-scale biomedical data from genomics, structural biology, and pharmacology using computational methods to understand G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) and develop personalized medicine approaches. His research focuses on discovering physiological ligands for orphan receptors, understanding GPCR pharmacogenomics to predict drug responses, and using machine learning to identify bioactive peptides and molecular interactions.

name: Anders Skov Kristensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anders Skov Kristensen is an associate professor studying molecular pharmacology of neurotransmitter receptors, particularly glutamate-gated ion channels (NMDA and AMPA receptors). His research integrates structural biology, computational modeling, and pharmacological characterization to understand receptor mechanisms and design therapeutic modulators.

name: Annette Eva Langkilde
title: Associate Professor
summary: Annette Eva Langkilde is an Associate Professor specializing in structural analysis of proteins in solution, with a particular focus on understanding protein fibrillation processes that lead to amyloid and amyloid-like structures. She directs the BioSAXS group at University of Copenhagen, using small-angle X-ray scattering as the primary method to determine protein structures, complemented by additional biophysical techniques including transmission electron microscopy and fiber diffraction.

name: Anne Byriel Walls
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anne Byriel Walls is an Associate Professor in Translational and Clinical Pharmacology at the University of Copenhagen. Her research spans from fundamental neuroscience studies of brain energy metabolism and neurotransmitter synthesis to clinical pharmacology investigations of medication use patterns and therapeutic outcomes in hospital and patient populations.

name: Azadeh Shahsavar
title: Associate Professor
summary: Azadeh Shahsavar is a structural biologist who uses cryo-electron microscopy to elucidate the atomic structures of neurotransmitter transporters and their complexes with drug molecules, with a focus on understanding inhibition mechanisms for therapeutic applications in neurological disorders.

name: Céline Galvagnion-Büll
title: Associate Professor
summary: Céline Galvagnion-Büll investigates the role of lipid homeostasis disruptions in Parkinson's disease pathogenesis, specifically how abnormal lipid changes promote alpha-synuclein protein aggregation and neuronal death. She uses integrated approaches combining stem cell models, post-mortem brain tissue analysis, and molecular biophysics to understand protein-membrane interactions in neurodegeneration.

name: Elisabeth Rexen Ulven
title: Associate Professor
summary: Elisabeth Rexen Ulven is an associate professor of medicinal chemistry who designs and characterizes ligands for free fatty acid receptors and other GPCRs as therapeutic targets for metabolic and inflammatory diseases. Her research involves structure-activity relationship studies, lead compound optimization, and functional characterization of receptor agonists and antagonists.

name: Jesper M. Mathiesen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jesper M. Mathiesen is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen studying molecular pharmacology of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), with particular focus on opioid receptors and their allosteric modulation, calcium-sensing receptors, and development of nanobody-based pharmacological tools for therapeutic applications.

name: Joseph Matthew Rogers
title: Associate Professor
summary: Joseph Matthew Rogers develops novel cyclic peptides and proteins as drug candidates through computational design and ribosomal display screening. His research focuses on creating peptides that can bind disease-related proteins and overcome cellular entry barriers, combining chemistry, molecular biology, and deep learning methods.

name: Karla Andrea Frydenvang
title: Associate Professor
summary: Karla Andrea Frydenvang is an Associate Professor specializing in structural biology of membrane-bound ionotropic glutamate receptors. Her research involves crystallizing and determining structures of glutamate receptor ligand-binding domains in complex with agonists, antagonists, and positive allosteric modulators to understand binding affinity, selectivity, and specificity differences.

name: Kristi Anne Kohlmeier
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kohlmeier investigates the neurobiology underlying arousal states and sleep, with focus on how neuroactive chemicals regulate sleep-wake cycles and arousal disorders. Her research examines the mechanisms by which pharmacological agents and drugs of abuse modulate neuronal activity in arousal-related brain regions, using electrophysiological techniques and animal models to understand drug effects and develop therapeutic targets for addiction, sleep disorders, and stress-related conditions.

name: Maurizio Sessa
title: Associate Professor
summary: Maurizio Sessa leads research integrating pharmacoepidemiology, pharmacovigilance, and data science to identify individuals at higher risk of adverse drug reactions or insufficient drug benefit using post-marketing administrative and spontaneous reporting databases. His work combines translational perspectives with real-world data analysis and artificial intelligence to improve patient safety in pharmacological treatment.

name: Morten Baltzer Houlind
title: Associate Professor
summary: Houlind conducts translational and clinical pharmacology research focused on drug safety, medication management, and biomarkers in older hospitalized patients, with recent work on biological aging markers, appropriate medication dosing for kidney disease, and clinical interventions to improve patient outcomes.

name: Niels Skotte
title: Associate Professor
summary: The Skotte Group explores protein alterations in Huntington's disease to discover biomarkers using human biofluids and define novel drug targets using human induced pluripotent stem cells and murine single cell populations including neurons, astrocytes, microglia, and oligodendrocytes from the central nervous system. He has worked extensively on Huntington's disease and played a central role in developing allele-selective antisense oligonucleotides for Huntington's disease.

name: Patrick Mac Donald Fisher
title: Associate Professor
summary: Patrick Fisher is a neuroscientist who applies multimodal brain imaging (fMRI and PET) to understand neurobiological mechanisms underlying neuropsychiatric disorders, particularly depression and psychedelic drug effects. His research focuses on how serotonergic systems and psychedelics like psilocybin affect brain connectivity, neural networks, and treatment outcomes.

name: Paul Robert Hansen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Paul Robert Hansen is an Associate Professor specializing in the design, synthesis, and development of peptide-based antibiotics and antimicrobial agents to combat multidrug-resistant bacteria. His research focuses on peptides, peptoids, and hybrid peptidomimetics as novel therapeutic approaches, with applications in immunology and cancer detection.

name: Tri Hien Viet Huynh
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tri Hien Viet Huynh develops and manufactures PET radiopharmaceuticals for clinical diagnosis of cancers and neurological diseases using fluorine-18 and carbon-11 nuclides. His research focuses on creating diagnostic imaging tracers for Alzheimer's disease, prostate cancer, and other conditions, with emphasis on novel PET chemistry methodologies and good manufacturing practice production.

name: Trine Meldgaard Lund
title: Associate Professor
summary: Trine Meldgaard Lund leads the Pharmacometrics Research Group at University of Copenhagen, focusing on population pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic modeling and simulations to characterize drug interactions and optimize individualized patient dosing. Her current research includes studies on opioid exposure and postoperative pain management, dose-finding for drug combinations, and development of fixed-dose combination drugs.

name: Tuula Anneli Kallunki
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tuula Kallunki studies cancer invasion and metastasis, particularly in HER2/ErbB2-positive breast cancers. She uses phenotypic screening and drug discovery approaches to identify inhibitors of invasion-promoting mechanisms involving lysosomes, signal transduction, and transcription factors.

name: Uffe Kristiansen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Uffe Kristiansen investigates ionotropic receptors for inhibitory amino acids (GABA and glycine) with a focus on developing selective ligands for extrasynaptic GABAA receptors as potential antiepileptic drugs. His research combines structure-activity relationship studies, patch-clamp electrophysiology, and animal models of epilepsy.

name: Charlotte Vermehren
title: Associate Professor
summary: Charlotte Vermehren is an Associate Professor in Translational and Clinical Pharmacology who focuses on reducing inappropriate polypharmacy and promoting personalized medication use through pharmacogenetic testing, computational drug metabolism prediction, medication reviews in primary care, and deprescribing interventions, particularly in older populations and patients with dementia.

name: Christian Kofoed
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Christian Kofoed develops methods for engineering customizable protein functions, particularly through programmable protein ligation on cell surfaces. His research integrates chemical biology, synthetic biology, and protein engineering approaches to create novel biopharmaceutical tools.

name: Christian Bernard Matthijs Poulie
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Poulie is a medicinal chemist specializing in the design and radiolabeling of serotonergic ligands, particularly 5-HT receptor agonists for positron emission tomography (PET) imaging and psychedelic drug research. His work focuses on developing novel synthesis routes for radiofluorinated compounds and understanding biased signaling mechanisms of psychedelic compounds at serotonin receptors.

name: Erika Bianca Torres Villanueva
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Erika Bianca Torres Villanueva is a researcher studying neuroinflammation and neurodegenerative diseases, with a particular focus on understanding glial-neuronal communication in Huntington's disease and the role of interferon signaling pathways in neurodegeneration using mouse models and mass spectrometry approaches.

name: Francesco Bavo
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Francesco Bavo is a medicinal chemist developing PROTACs and targeted protein degraders for CNS diseases, with expertise in designing selective inhibitors for brain-relevant ion channels and transporters like GABA transporters and nicotinic acetylcholine receptors. His work combines chemical synthesis, structural characterization, and fluorescent probe development to overcome limitations of traditional small-molecule inhibitors.

name: Haidai Hu
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Haidai Hu studies the structural biology of molecular machines using cryo-electron microscopy, with a focus on understanding bacteriophage-host interactions, bacterial defense mechanisms against phage invasion, and the structure and function of molecular motor complexes.

name: Janina Soermann
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Janina Sörmann is a researcher at the Department of Drug Design and Pharmacology who studies ion channels and membrane proteins, with expertise in electrophysiology, nanobodies, and protein engineering for drug discovery. Her recent work includes using deep learning to design miniprotein modulators for ion channels implicated in diseases like stroke and sleep apnea.

name: Jane Knöchel
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Jane Knöchel applies mathematical techniques and computational modeling to inform drug development and clinical pharmacology, with expertise in quantitative systems pharmacology and model reduction for understanding complex biological processes relevant to cardiovascular, renal, and metabolic diseases.

name: Jonas Sigurd Mortensen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Jonas Sigurd Mortensen studies the structural biology of membrane proteins, particularly neurotransmitter transporters and bacterial secretion systems, using advanced techniques including cryo-electron microscopy and NMR spectroscopy to understand protein function and substrate binding mechanisms.

name: Markus Staudt
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Markus Staudt is an assistant professor of medicinal chemistry at University of Copenhagen focused on the design and synthesis of bioactive small molecules, particularly glutamate receptor ligands and bioorthogonal probes for imaging applications. His research combines synthetic organic chemistry with pharmacological evaluation to develop selective receptor agonists/antagonists and radiopharmaceutical tools for biomedical research.

name: Trinh Trung Duong Nguyen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Trinh Trung Duong Nguyen applies machine learning and deep learning techniques to computational drug design and pharmacology, focusing on protein-target interactions, drug repurposing for neurological diseases, and pharmacogenomic research platforms. His work combines natural language processing, graph neural networks, and convolutional neural networks for predicting drug-protein interactions and identifying promising therapeutic compounds.

name: Samuel Cushman
title: Professor
summary: Samuel Cushman is a quantitative spatial and landscape ecologist who uses advanced statistical and computational methods to understand biodiversity, species populations, and ecosystem processes across landscape scales. His research integrates landscape genetics, habitat modeling, and connectivity analysis to address conservation challenges related to habitat fragmentation, climate change, and wildlife management.

name: Johan Dahlgren
title: Associate Professor
summary: Johan Dahlgren is a plant demographer studying how plants age and how environmental factors drive population dynamics. His research uses field data and statistical modeling to examine age-dependent demographic rates, senescence in perennial plants, and how climate and biotic factors influence plant survival, growth, and reproduction.

name: Owen R. Jones
title: Associate Professor
summary: Owen R. Jones is an Associate Professor at the University of Southern Denmark who investigates population dynamics and demographic patterns across animal species using matrix population models, comparative demography, and life history theory. His research focuses on how demographic strategies evolve across lineages, respond to environmental change, and what this means for species persistence, with particular interests in senescence/aging, phenological responses to climate change, and conservation applications.

name: Morgane Tidière
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Morgane Tidière studies sex differences in survival and aging patterns across vertebrate species using demographic data from the ZIMS database of captive animals in zoos and aquariums. Her research leverages population-level data to address evolutionary biology questions and inform conservation management practices.

name: Kristian Barrett
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Kristian Barrett is an Assistant Professor in Enzyme Bioinformatics at DTU who focuses on discovering, categorizing, and understanding carbohydrate-active enzymes, with particular emphasis on developing bioinformatics tools and protein sequence analysis methods for enzyme functional annotation.

name: Hans Christopher Bernstein
title: Professor
summary: Hans Christopher Bernstein is a professor specializing in genome-enabled bioengineering of microbes and microbial communities at the intersection of biotechnology and microbial ecology. His research encompasses synthetic biology, marine microbiomes, microalgal carbon capture and utilization, and the engineering of microbial consortia for sustainable bioprocess applications.

name: Kai Blin
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Kai Blin is a computational biologist focused on genome mining and bioinformatics for natural product discovery, particularly developing and maintaining antiSMASH and related tools for detecting and analyzing biosynthetic gene clusters in microbial genomes. His research spans secondary metabolite prediction, halogenated natural products, and community-driven biocuration of biosynthetic databases.

name: Alexander Kai Büll
title: Professor
summary: Alexander Kai Büll is a Professor of Protein Biophysics at DTU Bioengineering who studies mechanisms of protein assembly and aggregation, with primary focus on amyloid formation in neurodegenerative diseases like Parkinson's and Alzheimer's. His recent work extends to phase separation of intrinsically disordered proteins and application of biophysical techniques to understand plant protein properties for sustainable food.

name: Pep Charusanti
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Pep Charusanti is a Senior Researcher at DTU Bioengineering (within the Novo Nordisk Foundation Center for Biosustainability) who specializes in the discovery and development of new antibiotics from Streptomyces bacteria. He combines systems biology, molecular biology, and bioinformatics approaches to identify, characterize, and engineer secondary metabolite biosynthetic pathways in actinomycetes for drug discovery.

name: Irina Borodina
title: Professor
summary: Irina Borodina is a Professor of Yeast Metabolic Engineering at DTU Biosustain who develops genetic engineering methodologies and strain optimization approaches for yeast cell factories to sustainably produce chemicals and bioactive compounds from renewable feedstocks. Her research spans metabolic engineering of Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Yarrowia lipolytica for production of high-value chemicals, natural products, and insect pheromones for pest control applications.

name: Sanni-Leea Hellevi Aalto
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Sanni-Leea Aalto is a senior researcher at DTU Aqua studying microbiomes and microbial processes in aquaculture environments, particularly focusing on interactions between water, fish, and biofilms, as well as nitrification biofilters, denitrification, and hydrogen sulfide-producing microbes in recirculating aquaculture systems.

name: Kim Aarestrup
title: Professor
summary: Kim Aarestrup is a Professor at DTU Aqua specializing in freshwater fisheries ecology and fish migration dynamics, using telemetry techniques to track movement patterns of anadromous and catadromous fish species including salmon, trout, and eels in rivers and coastal environments.

name: Mathis von Ahnen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mathis von Ahnen develops and optimizes cost-effective end-of-pipe treatment technologies for removing nutrients and contaminants from aquaculture effluents, with a focus on denitrifying woodchip bioreactors and constructed wetlands for recirculating aquaculture systems.

name: Christoffer Moesgaard Albertsen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Christoffer Moesgaard Albertsen's research focuses on applied statistics and state-space modelling of marine systems, particularly fisheries stock assessment models and individual animal movement models. His main statistical interest is state-space models—both applications and computational methods.

name: Ken Haste Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Ken Haste Andersen is a theoretical marine ecologist who develops quantitative models combining physics and ecology to understand marine ecosystem structure and function across scales from bacteria to whales. His work focuses on size-structured population dynamics, fisheries sustainability, and evolutionary strategies in fish communities.

name: Niels Gerner Andersen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Niels Gerner Andersen develops mechanistic models of gastric evacuation in predatory fish to understand food intake rates. His work aims to improve fisheries management and ecosystem predictions by quantifying how much predatory fish consume under natural conditions.

name: Henrik Baktoft
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Henrik Baktoft is a senior researcher specializing in tracking and understanding fish behavior using high-resolution acoustic telemetry and positioning technologies, with particular focus on freshwater species and migration patterns in temperate lakes and rivers.

name: Francois Bastardie
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Francois Bastardie is a Senior Researcher at DTU Aqua specializing in ecosystem-based marine management through spatial fisheries modeling and impact assessment. He develops methods for mapping fishing activities, assessing bottom-gear impacts on seafloor habitats, and evaluates fisheries management scenarios using bio-economic simulations that integrate fisher behavior dynamics with marine ecosystem responses.

name: Jane Behrens
title: Professor
summary: Jane Behrens is a Senior Researcher at DTU Aqua who studies fish ecophysiology through experimental and field investigations of physiological and behavioral responses to environmental stressors, with a focus on commercially important species like Atlantic cod in the Baltic Sea and invasive fish species. Her research includes examining how fish adapt to hypoxia, temperature changes, parasitic infections, and other biotic and abiotic factors affecting growth, health, and population dynamics.

name: Dorte Bekkevold
title: Professor
summary: Dorte Bekkevold is a Senior Researcher at DTU Aqua specializing in population genetics and environmental DNA (eDNA) methods applied to marine fish stocks and fisheries management. Her work focuses on understanding genetic diversity, population structure, and migration dynamics in commercially important species like Atlantic herring and pelagic fishes, with applications to sustainable fisheries and conservation.

name: Casper Willestofte Berg
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Casper Willestofte Berg is a senior researcher at DTU Aqua specializing in statistical methods for fish stock assessment and population dynamics modeling, with particular focus on Baltic fish stocks and the impacts of fisheries on marine life, including bycatch in gillnets and fishing gear interactions.

name: Jesper Boje
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Jesper Boje is a senior researcher at DTU Aqua specializing in fisheries science and fish population dynamics. His research focuses on stock assessment and management of North Atlantic fish species, particularly Greenland halibut and flatfishes, integrating genetics, spatial monitoring, and fisheries data to inform sustainable management strategies.

name: Amber Adore Boot
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Amber Boot investigates how changes in ocean circulation systems, particularly AMOC weakening and tipping points, affect marine ecosystems and global climate. Her research combines climate modeling, biogeochemical processes, and ecosystem responses to understand ocean-climate interactions under future warming scenarios.

name: Niels Agerbirk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Niels Agerbirk is an Associate Professor specializing in the chemistry and biochemistry of natural products, particularly glucosinolates and other secondary metabolites in plants. His research combines analytical chemistry (HPLC-MS and NMR) with plant ecology to study how chemical diversity in plants evolves and mediates interactions with herbivorous insects, using the model plant Barbarea vulgaris.

name: Christian Andreasen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Andreasen is an Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen researching weed management, crop protection, and sustainable agricultural practices. His work encompasses weed biology, integrated crop protection strategies including physical and chemical control methods, and crop production in subtropical regions including quinoa cultivation.

name: Johan Andersen-Ranberg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Johan Andersen-Ranberg is an associate professor at the University of Copenhagen studying how to genetically engineer microalgae and plants to produce valuable compounds including carotenoids, terpenes, and fatty acids, with applications in sustainable biomanufacturing and biotechnology.

name: Frederik Bak
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Frederik Bak is an assistant professor in soil microbial ecology studying plant-microbe interactions and rhizosphere communities, particularly in wheat and maize crops. His research focuses on understanding how microbial communities assemble on roots, the role of seed bacteria, and uses metagenomics and amplicon sequencing to study subsurface bacterial ecology and agricultural applications.

name: Nanna Bjarnholt
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Nanna Bjarnholt is an Associate Professor of Plant Biochemistry at University of Copenhagen who pioneered spatial metabolomics technology, particularly mass spectrometry imaging (PlantMSI facility), to visualize metabolite distribution in plant tissues. Her research focuses on plant specialized metabolites including cyanogenic glucosides, their biosynthesis, defense mechanisms, and metabolic recycling pathways in crops like sorghum.

name: Marie Louise Bornø
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Marie Louise Bornø studies how agricultural management practices affect soil processes, with particular focus on biochar amendments and their effects on phosphorus dynamics, nutrient availability, and microbial communities in the rhizosphere. Her research integrates soil science and environmental microbiology to understand sustainable soil management and greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural systems.

name: Kristian Koefoed Brandt
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kristian Koefoed Brandt is an Associate Professor in microbial ecology at University of Copenhagen studying how environmental contaminants affect soil and aquatic microorganisms, with particular focus on antibiotic resistance dissemination, microbial community responses to pollutants (metals, pesticides, antibiotics), and ecotoxicological assessment methods.

name: Andreas Brændholt
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Andreas Brændholt investigates soil greenhouse gas fluxes (CO2 and methane) in agricultural and forest ecosystems, with emphasis on measurement techniques and processes controlling soil carbon dynamics under different land management and climate conditions.

name: Pablo D. Cárdenas
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Pablo D. Cárdenas studies how plants synthesize specialized metabolic compounds, particularly saponins and defensive metabolites, using integrated approaches combining molecular biology, genomics, transcriptomics, and metabolomics to enable the bioengineering of crops for future food security.

name: Alexander Byth Carstens
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Alexander Byth Carstens is an Assistant Professor researching bacteriophages as tools for controlling plant bacterial pathogens and managing plant microbiomes. His work involves isolating novel phages, characterizing their genomics and biology, and investigating their potential for biocontrol applications against plant pathogens like Dickeya species and phyllosphere bacteria.

name: Svend Christensen
title: Professor
summary: Svend Christensen is a professor in crop science and technologies who researches precision agriculture methods for sustainable farming, with expertise in weed management, plant phenotyping, remote sensing, and robotics for autonomous crop management. His current work focuses on the intersection of sustainable agricultural systems and biosolutions, including robotic intercropping systems and AI-driven crop monitoring.

name: Pedro Miguel Pereira Correia
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Pedro Miguel Pereira Correia is a postdoctoral researcher studying plant stress physiology and climate resilience, focusing on how crops respond to combined heat and drought stress, photosynthetic recovery mechanisms, and the physiological and metabolic basis of abiotic stress tolerance in crops like wheat and maize.

name: Sohini Deb
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Sohini Deb studies plant immune mechanisms against fungal and bacterial pathogens, focusing on how pathogen effector proteins manipulate plant endomembrane trafficking and cellular responses in rice and barley.

name: Dorte Bodin Dresbøll
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dorte Bodin Dresbøll is an associate professor in crop sciences focused on understanding deep-rooted crop systems, their anatomical characteristics, and capacity to extract water and nutrients from deep soil layers. Her research spans root physiology, rhizosphere microbiology, stomatal traits, and sustainable cropping systems including legume and intercropping innovations.

name: Brian Lund Fredensborg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Brian Lund Fredensborg studies the ecological and evolutionary relationships between parasites and their hosts, with a focus on parasite-host interactions with environmental stressors (e.g., pesticides) and parasite behavioral manipulation of hosts using helminths in arthropod systems.

name: Fernando Geu-Flores
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Fernando Geu-Flores is an Associate Professor specializing in elucidating and engineering complex plant biosynthetic pathways, particularly those producing bioactive alkaloids and other secondary metabolites with pharmaceutical and agricultural applications. His research focuses on natural product biosynthesis, transport mechanisms, and metabolic engineering to improve crop nutritional value and pest resistance.

name: Bhim Bahadur Ghaley
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bhim Bahadur Ghaley is an associate professor researching sustainable agricultural production systems, focusing on nutrient use efficiency, soil health, and agroecological practices including agroforestry, intercropping, and controlled-environment agriculture such as microgreen and vertical farming production.

name: Veronika Hansen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Veronika Hansen is an Assistant Professor focused on sustainable and circular crop production that minimizes climate and environmental impacts. Her research addresses soil fertility building, nutrient recycling, and crop productivity through cover crops and bio-based fertilizers.

name: Christian Bugge Henriksen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christian Bugge Henriksen leads the Climate and Food Security research group at University of Copenhagen, conducting research on sustainable food production systems including intercropping, regenerative agriculture, agroforestry, and urban farming. He applies crop modelling, life cycle analysis, and decision support systems to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote plant-based diets for climate change mitigation and food security.

name: Nina Molin Høyland-Kroghsbo
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nina Molin Høyland-Kroghsbo investigates how bacteria use quorum sensing (cell-cell communication) to detect and respond to environmental threats, particularly bacteriophage infections. Her work focuses on the molecular mechanisms of phage-bacterial interactions and bacterial defense systems like CRISPR-Cas, particularly in Pseudomonas aeruginosa.

name: Stine Kramer Jacobsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Stine Kramer Jacobsen studies agroecology and natural pest control mechanisms, focusing on predatory insects and mites as biological control agents in crops such as strawberries and apples. Her research uses methods including behavioral studies and molecular gut content analysis to understand trophic interactions between generalist predators and agricultural pests.

name: Signe Marie Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Signe Marie Jensen is an Associate Professor in Crop Sciences at PLEN, University of Copenhagen, specializing in statistical methods for analyzing agricultural phenotyping data and crop management. Her recent research focuses on applying data science and artificial intelligence to precision agriculture, including crop trait analysis, weed management, and crop yield prediction.

name: Per Moestrup Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Per Moestrup Jensen studies the ecology and epidemiology of zoonotic parasites and tick-borne diseases, with particular expertise in tick vectors, Borrelia and other tick-borne pathogens, and rodent-mediated parasite transmission in Nordic ecosystems.

name: Annette Bruun Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Annette Bruun Jensen is an Associate Professor whose research focuses on interactions between microorganisms and insects, with particular emphasis on insect diseases, fungal pathogens, and applications to both honey bee health and edible insect production systems.

name: Birgit Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Birgit Jensen studies biological control of plant diseases through isolation and characterization of beneficial microbes, with focus on fungal endophytes and their mechanisms for protecting crops from pathogens and abiotic stress. Her research combines microbial ecology, plant-pathogen interactions, and molecular mechanisms underlying biocontrol efficacy in agricultural systems.

name: Hans Jørgen Lyngs Jørgensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Hans Jørgen Lyngs Jørgensen is an associate professor focusing on plant pathology and microbiology, studying disease resistance mechanisms and fungal pathogen interactions in both temperate cereals and tropical crops using microscopy, molecular biology techniques, and biochemical assays.

name: Bodil Jørgensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bodil Jørgensen studies cell wall enzymes, tissue culture, and carbohydrates with a focus on plant glycobiology. Her research contributes to topics of cell wall and polysaccharide characterization.

name: Kirsten Jørgensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kirsten Jørgensen is an Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen studying plant metabolism and biotechnology, with particular focus on toxic secondary metabolites in crop plants (cyanogenic glycosides, almonds, cassava) and engineered starch properties in transgenic plants.

name: Johanna Krahmer
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Johanna Krahmer studies how light signals regulate plant growth, metabolism, and cellular processes through molecular mechanisms including phototropism, hypocotyl development, and proteomics-based analysis of protein modifications in Arabidopsis.

name: Conny Bruun Asmussen Lange
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor at the University of Copenhagen specializing in plant systematics, taxonomy, and molecular phylogenetics. She leads the Plant Systematics research group and focuses on plant evolution, identification, and classification using DNA-based methods, with particular expertise in Brassicaceae family species and crop genetic diversity.

name: Kristian Holst Laursen
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Kristian Holst Laursen leads the Plant Nutrients and Food Quality research group at University of Copenhagen, focusing on bridging plant and food science using advanced analytical chemistry. His work investigates nutrient content, bioavailability, and food authenticity in plant-based foods using ICP-MS, stable isotope analysis, and chemometric approaches.

name: Tomas Laursen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tomas Laursen studies the structure and organization of plant metabolons—enzymatic complexes embedded in endoplasmic reticulum membranes that produce secondary metabolites and defense compounds. His research combines advanced biophysical techniques including single-molecule microscopy, cryo-EM, and neutron reflectometry to understand how plants organize biosynthetic enzymes and the role of lipids in these dynamic complexes.

name: Antoine Lecocq
title: Associate Professor
summary: Antoine Lecocq researches the health and disease of insects mass-produced for food and feed, particularly house crickets, mealworms, and black soldier flies. His work involves developing bioassays to assess pathogen susceptibility, identifying and diagnosing pathogens in production systems, and screening beneficial bacteria to improve insect health.

name: Feiyan Liang
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Feiyan Liang is an Assistant Professor working on elucidating natural product biosynthesis pathways and engineering microorganisms for sustainable pharmaceutical production. Their recent breakthrough involves engineering yeast to produce Taxol (paclitaxel), a widely used chemotherapy drug, through fermentation rather than chemical synthesis, with applications to cost reduction and environmental sustainability.

name: Grmay Hailu Lilay
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Grmay Hailu Lilay studies plant zinc nutrition and homeostasis, particularly focusing on how plants sense and regulate zinc uptake. His research identifies transcription factors like bZIP19 and bZIP23 that function as zinc sensors, with applications toward developing zinc-enriched crops to address global zinc deficiency affecting billions of people.

name: Guangbin Luo
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Guangbin Luo is an Assistant Professor working on the de novo domestication of perennial grain species to develop crops for future agriculture in response to climate change. His research combines plant genome editing, wheat genetics, and genetic engineering to improve crop traits including seed storage protein synthesis, starch accumulation, and plant regeneration efficiency.

name: Marin Rose Lysák
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Marin Rose Lysák is an assistant professor working on food system sustainability, focusing on reducing greenhouse gas emissions through dietary changes, sustainable procurement in public institutions, and transforming European food systems toward climate-friendly and plant-based approaches.

name: Henrik Vlk Lütken
title: Associate Professor
summary: Henrik Lütken's main research focuses on molecular breeding of ornamentals, applying specific genes affecting plant growth control in economically important plants like Kalanchoë and Poinsettia. He also develops non-GMO plant transformation platforms using Agrobacterium rhizogenes in plants like Campanula and Kalanchoë.

name: Jakob Magid
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jakob Magid is an Associate Professor studying soil-plant-nutrient interactions in agroecosystems, with emphasis on carbon and phosphorus cycling, organic matter turnover, and sustainable nutrient management through waste recycling and organic farming practices.

name: Karel Anton Miettinen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Karel Miettinen is an assistant professor at the University of Copenhagen studying plant specialized metabolism, particularly terpenoid and alkaloid biosynthesis pathways. His research focuses on understanding and engineering plant secondary metabolite production using molecular biology, heterologous expression systems, and yeast-based biosensors for pharmaceutical compound discovery.

name: Dorette Sophie Müller-Stöver
title: Associate Professor
summary: Müller-Stöver is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen researching interactions between soil, microorganisms, and plants, with a focus on soil fertility management through organic waste products, biochar applications, and nutrient recovery from biowaste. Her work emphasizes phosphorus cycling, soil microbial ecology in the rhizosphere, and sustainable agriculture practices.

name: Mette Haubjerg Nicolaisen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mette Haubjerg Nicolaisen is a molecular microbial ecologist studying the assembly and function of soil microbial communities, with a focus on how microbes interact with plant roots, fungal hyphae, and nematodes. Her applied research develops microcosm platforms and microbial inoculants to promote plant growth and enhance crop resilience.

name: Nikoline Juul Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nikoline Juul Nielsen is an environmental analytical chemist specializing in chromatographic and mass spectrometric methods for analyzing complex biological and environmental samples. Her research focuses on developing advanced analytical techniques for environmental chemical fingerprinting, non-targeted screening approaches, food safety analysis, and identifying contaminants and pollutants in water and soil.

name: Tue Kjærgaard Nielsen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Tue Kjærgaard Nielsen is a molecular microbiologist specializing in third-generation sequencing (Nanopore) and bioinformatics, with research focused on how mobile genetic elements affect microbial evolution and their role in degrading environmental pollutants including PFAS, pesticides, and antibiotic resistance mechanisms.

name: Lise Charlotte Marie Noack
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Lise Noack is an Assistant Professor studying how membrane lipids and sterols regulate cellulose synthesis and protein dynamics at the plant plasma membrane. Her research focuses on understanding the role of signaling lipids in controlling cellulose synthase complex trafficking and plant cell surface organization.

name: Rocio Ochoa Fernandez
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rocio Ochoa Fernandez develops synthetic biology tools to control plant gene expression through light, including the PULSE optogenetic system that enables light-regulated transgene expression in plants. Her current research investigates how plants store secondary metabolites in biocondensates while preventing cellular toxicity.

name: Mike Ogden
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mike Ogden is an Assistant Professor studying how nitrogen regulates plant cell wall synthesis and root growth, with research interests in plant responses to nutrient stress and pest attack. His work focuses on understanding molecular pathways and metabolic processes involved in plant adaptation to stressful conditions.

name: Morgane Ourry
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Morgane Ourry studies multiple organismal interactions between plants, insects, and microorganisms, focusing on the molecular mechanisms underlying these interactions and how belowground herbivory influences rhizosphere and root microbial communities. She is currently involved in the H2020 Excalibur project investigating soil biodiversity dynamics and synergistic effects of prebiotic and probiotic approaches in horticulture.

name: Chandana Pandey
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Chandana Pandey is an Assistant Professor studying plant physiology, stress responses, and plant-microbe interactions. Her research focuses on understanding how plants respond to biotic stresses (pathogen infection), abiotic stresses (temperature, heavy metals, UV-B radiation), and beneficial bacteria interactions, using molecular and phenotyping approaches.

name: Carsten Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Carsten Pedersen's main research area is within plant-microbe interactions with focus on pathogen effector genomics and biology, using barley and the barley powdery mildew fungus (Blumeria hordei) as a model pathosystem, while also employing Arabidopsis and its compatible powdery mildew fungi. He has also been involved in research on wild Crocus species and indoor cultivation of Crocus sativus under various light spectra conditions.

name: Daniel Pergament Persson
title: Associate Professor
summary: Daniel Pergament Persson studies plant mineral nutrition and homeostasis, with particular focus on how plants acquire, transport, and regulate essential micronutrients like zinc. He develops analytical methods for measuring element distribution in plant tissues and investigates the molecular mechanisms underlying nutrient sensing and deficiency responses.

name: Rikke Poulsen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Rikke Poulsen is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Plant and Environmental Sciences (PLEN) at University of Copenhagen who studies how environmental chemicals, particularly endocrine disruptors and thyroid hormone system disruptors, affect organisms at multiple biological levels. She combines biochemical and chemical tools including hormone quantification, metabolomics, transcriptomics, and mass spectrometry imaging to assess toxicological impacts in non-mammalian model organisms such as frogs and fish.

name: Daniel Poveda Huertes
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Assistant professor working on synthetic biology approaches to engineer algae as photosynthetic biofactories, with focus on improving transformation technologies in stramenopile species like Nannochloropsis and Phaeodactylum. Previously studied mitochondrial protein biogenesis and stress responses in yeast.

name: Nelly Sophie Raymond
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Nelly Sophie Raymond is a soil and plant nutrition scientist who studies how to improve soil fertility and crop productivity sustainably, with a primary focus on phosphorus cycling in soils. Her research encompasses characterizing recycled fertilizers from waste streams, using microbial inoculants and phosphate-solubilizing fungi to enhance crop phosphorus availability, and investigating soil microbial communities and soil chemistry in agricultural systems.

name: Leise Riber
title: Associate Professor
summary: Leise Riber studies molecular microbiology and bacterial genetics, with a primary focus on the biology and dynamics of mobile genetic elements including plasmids and bacteriophages, particularly their interactions with bacterial hosts and the molecular mechanisms of phage-bacteria defense strategies.

name: Monica Rojas Triana
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Monica Rojas-Triana studies how plants sense and respond to nutrient deficiency, particularly phosphate starvation, focusing on signaling mechanisms, protein ubiquitination, and systemic communication between plant organs. Her work includes investigating mobile RNAs that transport nutrients and coordinate responses across different tissues in Arabidopsis.

name: Eva Rosenqvist
title: Associate Professor
summary: Eva Rosenqvist is an Associate Professor of Crop Sciences at University of Copenhagen who studies plant physiological responses to environmental stress through chlorophyll fluorescence measurements, with research focusing on heat, drought, and light stress tolerance in crops like tomato and wheat, as well as greenhouse production optimization.

name: Lene Sigsgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lene Sigsgaard is a researcher in entomology and agriculture who focuses on applied ecological studies of predation aimed at developing sustainable production systems which promote natural regulation and biological control.

name: Bjarne W. Strobel
title: Associate Professor
summary: Bjarne W. Strobel is an associate professor at the University of Copenhagen researching environmental contaminants including PFAS, pesticides, and natural plant toxins in soil and water systems, with focus on remediation strategies, contaminant transport mechanisms, and biochar/biological treatment approaches.

name: Simon Fiil Svane
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Simon Fiil Svane develops high-throughput imaging systems and computational methods for automated root phenotyping in crops. His research combines multispectral camera technology, image analysis algorithms, and genomic prediction to study deep root traits and yield-related characteristics in barley and wheat under field conditions.

name: Azeem Tariq
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Azeem Tariq studies soil and plant nutrient cycling, atmospheric greenhouse gas emissions, and soil carbon sequestration in response to crop and nutrient management practices. His research focuses on understanding mechanisms of GHG production from agricultural soils and developing mitigation strategies through field measurements and biogeochemical modeling.

name: Frederik Grønbæk Tidemand
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Frederik Grønbæk Tidemand is a postdoctoral researcher studying the structure and function of membrane proteins using biophysical methods, with recent focus on plant signaling peptides and their receptors. His work combines structural biology approaches like small-angle scattering and neutron diffraction to understand membrane protein organization, ion transport mechanisms, and plant peptide-mediated signaling pathways.

name: Selina Kornelia Tisler
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Selina Tisler is an Assistant Professor in Analytical Chemistry at University of Copenhagen who develops advanced analytical methods for detecting emerging contaminants and pollutants in water systems. Her research focuses on nontarget screening using mass spectrometry to identify unknown toxic compounds in wastewater effluents and studying chemical migration from consumer products like reusable plastic bottles into drinking water.

name: Dominique Jeanette Tobler
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dominique Tobler is an environmental materials chemist studying biogeochemical processes at mineral-fluid-microbe interfaces, with emphasis on iron mineral chemistry, biomineralization, and the use of synthetic iron phases for groundwater pollutant remediation. Her research employs synchrotron-based spectroscopy and microscopy to observe these processes at nano- to micron-scales in real time.

name: Torben Bo Toldam-Andersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Toldam-Andersen is a pomologist studying fruit and berry physiology, quality, and processing. His research focuses on carbon physiology, fruit development, fruit composition (sugars, acids, color), and cultivation practices—particularly in apples, berries, and cool-climate viticulture in Denmark.

name: Giorgio Tomasi
title: Associate Professor
summary: Giorgio Tomasi is an Associate Professor in Environmental Chemistry and Physics who specializes in developing chemometric and statistical methods for environmental and analytical chemistry, with applications to detecting and characterizing pollutants in water and sediments using advanced chromatography and spectroscopy techniques.

name: Xenia Trier
title: Associate Professor
summary: Xenia Trier is an associate professor of environmental analytical chemistry studying per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) and other environmental contaminants. Her research focuses on developing analytical methods using liquid chromatography coupled to accurate mass spectrometry, including targeted, suspect, and non-targeted screening approaches combined with chemometrics, with applications to food contact materials and risk governance of environmental chemicals.

name: Mai Duy Luu Trinh
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mai Duy Luu Trinh is an Assistant Professor working on plant photosynthesis regulation, chloroplast physiology, and crop improvement using genetic technologies. His current focus is enhancing climate-resilient crops like quinoa through molecular breeding and genome editing to address food security challenges.

name: Frederik van der Bom
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Frederik van der Bom studies plant-soil interactions and crop productivity, with a focus on how root architecture and nutrient management (particularly phosphorus) influence crop performance under diverse soil and environmental conditions. His research integrates agronomy, soil science, and plant breeding to develop resource-efficient crops and resilient farming systems.

name: Deyang Xu
title: Associate Professor
summary: Deyang Xu studies the molecular mechanisms underlying the distribution and transport of plant specialized metabolites, particularly glucosinolates, and how plants use chemical signals to communicate with other organisms. His research integrates plant molecular biology, metabolite transport engineering, and the biological significance of plant chemistry for applications ranging from crop improvement to biotechnology.

name: Shuai Zheng
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Shuai Zheng is an Assistant Professor studying plant cell wall biology, glycobiology, and protein interactions. His research focuses on how transcription factors regulate plant developmental processes like vascular bundle formation and cell wall synthesis, employing molecular and imaging techniques to understand cellular mechanisms.

name: Henrik Hjarvard de Fine Licht
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: De Fine Licht studies the evolutionary consequences of species interactions between insects and fungi, focusing on genetic and phenotypic mechanisms underlying adaptation. His research primarily uses insect-microbial systems, including entomopathogenic fungi and symbiotic relationships in fungus-farming ants and beetles, combining genomic and transcriptomic approaches with functional phenotypic analysis.

name: Marian Ørgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Marian Ørgaard is a botanist who studies plant taxonomy and evolution, with a primary focus on the genus Barbarea (winter-cress). His research examines plant defense mechanisms, including glucosinolate and saponin production, herbivore resistance polymorphisms, and the evolutionary divergence of plant lineages through molecular phylogenetics and cytogenetic analysis.

name: Witold Piotr Kot
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Witold Kot studies bacteriophages and their interactions with microbial hosts using genomic and metagenomic approaches, with focus on DNA modifications in phage genomes and applications in agriculture and therapeutic interventions.

name: Bruno Trevenzoli Favero
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Assistant Professor specializing in plant genetic transformation using Rhizobium/Agrobacterium rhizogenes and CRISPR-Cas9 systems, focusing on engineering stress tolerance and improving crop and ornamental plant traits through molecular characterization of rol-genes and ORFs.

name: Xizi Wang
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Xizi Wang is an Assistant Professor studying crop physiology at the University of Copenhagen, with a focus on how wheat and other crops respond to environmental stresses including drought and elevated atmospheric CO2 concentrations. Their research examines photosynthetic responses, water-use efficiency, and long-term acclimation mechanisms across multiple plant generations.

name: Guillermo Sergio Moreno Pescador
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Moreno Pescador develops thermoplasmonic and biophysical methods to study cellular membrane dynamics, particularly focusing on membrane lipids, sterol function in plants, and using plasmonic nanoparticles for nanoscale thermal manipulation of cells and membranes.

name: Amir Yehudayoff
title: Professor
summary: Amir Yehudayoff is a Professor at DIKU, University of Copenhagen, working on the mathematical foundations of machine learning and theoretical computer science. His recent research investigates connections between compression, stability, privacy, online learning, and replicability in machine learning algorithms.

name: Barry Brown
title: Professor
summary: Barry Brown's work focuses on foreseeing how new technologies will actually come to be used and the problems they may create, documenting opportunities and challenges around technologies such as music sharing, digital tourism, mobile phone use, smartwatches, digital maps, video streaming, wikis, smart speakers, IoT hacking, ride sharing, e-scooters and self-driving cars. His recent research investigates the social mechanics of how robots navigate human crowds—how they signal intent, manage right-of-way, and fit into the social fabric of the

name: Christina Lioma
title: Professor
summary: Christina Lioma is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science (DIKU) at the University of Copenhagen who leads the Machine Learning research section. Her research focuses on artificial intelligence, information retrieval, natural language processing, and recommender systems, with notable work on improving how machines understand text, detecting misinformation and fake news, and applying machine learning to real-world problems like music streaming and fact-checking.

name: Erik Bjørnager Dam
title: Professor
summary: Erik Dam is a professor of machine learning at DIKU (University of Copenhagen) focused on deep learning methods for medical image analysis, particularly volumetric 3D segmentation tasks involving MRI and CT imaging. His work spans automated segmentation architectures, osteoarthritis imaging, and developing efficient neural network systems for clinical applications.

name: Ingemar Johansson Cox
title: Professor
summary: Ingemar Cox is a Professor of Machine Learning at University of Copenhagen (DIKU) whose research spans digital epidemiology, computer vision, image retrieval, and multimedia. His recent work focuses on using web search activity and online data to track and forecast infectious diseases and detect health anomalies through digital signals.

name: Irina Alex Shklovski
title: Professor
summary: Irina Shklovski is an interdisciplinary professor working across human-computer interaction, communication, and science & technology studies. Her research focuses on integrating ethics, responsibility, and fairness into technology development, with particular attention to privacy concerns, data protection, creepy technologies, and how to address the powerlessness people experience with massive personal data collection.

name: Jakob Nordström
title: Professor
summary: Jakob Nordström is a professor of theoretical computer science at DIKU working on computational complexity theory and combinatorial optimization. His research focuses on proving fundamental hardness results for optimization problems, developing improved algorithms, and harnessing complexity theory to certify that algorithms produce correct results through proof logging and certifying techniques.

name: Jon Sporring
title: Professor
summary: Jon Sporring is a Professor in Image Analysis, Computational Modelling and Geometry at DIKU who specializes in theoretical image processing, medical image analysis, and mathematical approaches to analyzing microscopy and synchrotron images. His recent work focuses on scale-space theory, stochastic geometry, electron microscope image analysis, and deep learning methods for feature extraction.

name: Kasper Hornbæk
title: Professor
summary: Kasper Hornbæk is a professor at DIKU who leads the Human-Centred Computing research group, focusing on understanding and improving how people interact with computer systems through empirical investigation and innovative interface design. His research spans body-based interactions, haptics, virtual reality, and the methodological foundations of HCI, including how to measure and evaluate usability and user experience.

name: Mads Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Mads Nielsen is a Professor of Computer Science whose research focuses on medical image analysis and computer vision, with particular emphasis on developing machine learning and deep learning methods for early cancer detection (especially breast cancer) and neuroimaging analysis. His work spans pattern recognition, image segmentation, and AI applications in medical imaging and diagnosis.

name: Morten Misfeldt
title: Professor
summary: Morten Misfeldt is a professor studying the intersection of digital technology, mathematics education, and learning science. His research focuses on how digital tools influence mathematical work processes and teaching, quantitative ethnography methods for analyzing student collaboration, and the implementation of technology-enhanced learning environments in science and mathematics education.

name: Pernille Bjørn
title: Professor
summary: Pernille Bjørn is a Professor of Computer Supported Cooperative Work (CSCW) at DIKU who investigates collaborative work practices and designs technologies to support cooperation. Her research spans healthcare systems, global distributed work, tech entrepreneurship in the Global South, diversity and neurodiversity in computing, and increasingly intersects with artistic and design-based research methods including Research through Art.

name: Philippe Bonnet
title: Professor
summary: Philippe Bonnet is an experimental computer scientist specializing in database systems, storage system software, and data-intensive computing. His recent work focuses on co-designed SSDs, computational storage, and storage systems supporting extreme-scale science.

name: Robert Glück
title: Professor
summary: Robert Glück is a professor of computer science whose research focuses on reversible computing, program generation, and metaprogramming. His work includes automatic program transformation techniques, program inversion, and the design of reversible programming languages that can execute computations in both forward and backward directions.

name: Robert Jenssen
title: Professor
summary: Robert Jenssen develops machine learning methods with emphasis on information-theoretic approaches, kernel methods, and deep learning for image analysis and multimodal learning. His research spans medical imaging, marine monitoring, remote sensing, and explainable artificial intelligence.

name: Serge Belongie
title: Professor
summary: Serge Belongie is a professor of computer science at University of Copenhagen and director of the Pioneer Centre for AI, conducting research in computer vision and machine learning with applications to biodiversity studies, augmented reality, and social media misinformation detection.

name: Stefan Sommer
title: Professor
summary: Stefan Sommer is a professor at DIKU working on statistical methods for analyzing geometric and non-linear data, with applications to shape analysis, medical image registration, and computational anatomy. His research focuses on extending statistical techniques to manifold-valued data using differential geometry and stochastic methods.

name: Stephen Alstrup
title: Professor
summary: Stephen Alstrup is a professor in algorithms and complexity at University of Copenhagen's Department of Computer Science who conducts fundamental research in algorithms, data structures, and graph theory. His work spans from theoretical algorithmic breakthroughs in areas like dynamic trees and distance labeling to applied machine learning projects in customer service systems, educational analytics, and information retrieval.

name: Sune Darkner
title: Professor
summary: Sune Darkner is a Professor at DIKU specializing in medical image analysis and computational geometry, focusing on image registration, deep learning-based segmentation, and machine learning approaches to medical imaging problems. His recent work spans diffeomorphic image registration, physics-informed neural networks, and automated medical image analysis for applications including neuroimaging and organ segmentation.

name: Thomas Philip Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Philip Jensen is a Professor of Programming Languages and Theory of Computation at DIKU focusing on semantics-based program analysis and software security, particularly through static analysis techniques, information flow analysis, and compiler verification methods.

name: Wouter Boomsma
title: Professor
summary: Wouter Boomsma is a Professor of Machine Learning at University of Copenhagen who develops computational methods for analyzing molecular structures, particularly proteins. His research spans machine learning approaches to protein engineering, mutation prediction, protein dynamics, and structural bioinformatics applications in biology and pharmaceutical research.

name: Yongluan Zhou
title: Professor
summary: Yongluan Zhou is a professor of computer science at the University of Copenhagen leading the Data Management Systems Lab. His research focuses on database systems and distributed systems, particularly scalable event-driven architectures, microservices data management, and consistency challenges in large-scale distributed systems.

name: Andrzej Filinski
title: Associate Professor
summary: Andrzej Filinski is an Associate Professor at DIKU specializing in programming language theory, particularly formal semantics, computational effects, monads, and control flow abstractions. His research focuses on semantic foundations for functional programming languages and logical frameworks for formalizing program behavior.

name: Boris Düdder
title: Associate Professor
summary: Boris Düdder is an Associate Professor at DIKU focusing on formal methods and programming languages for trustworthy distributed systems, with emphasis on automated program generation for adaptive systems with high-reliability guarantees. His research bridges formal computer science foundations with industrial applications including blockchain, secure data ecosystems, trustworthy AI, and smart contract design.

name: Bulat Ibragimov
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Bulat Ibragimov is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen working on applying machine learning and medical image analysis to cancer treatment planning, diagnosis, and outcome prediction. His research focuses on AI solutions for radiotherapy planning, toxicity prediction in radiation oncology, automated medical image segmentation, and understanding radiologists' decision-making through eye tracking.

name: Cosmin Eugen Oancea
title: Associate Professor
summary: Oancea is an Associate Professor working on programming language design, optimizing compilers, and automatic parallelization techniques. He is the co-architect of Futhark, a purely functional GPU-programming language with nested parallelism that achieves competitive performance with hand-written code.

name: Daniel Lee Ashbrook
title: Associate Professor
summary: Daniel Ashbrook is an Associate Professor in Human-Centered Computing who researches novel interaction techniques for personal fabrication technologies like 3D printers and laser cutters. His work focuses on making digital fabrication more accessible and interactive, while historically concentrating on wearable and mobile computing interfaces that minimize user distraction from the physical world.

name: Fabian Christian Gieseke
title: Associate Professor
summary: Fabian Gieseke develops machine learning methods for large-scale environmental monitoring, with recent focus on using deep learning and remote sensing data (satellite imagery and LiDAR) to estimate forest structure properties like canopy height and above-ground biomass. His research bridges machine learning foundations (optimization, GPU acceleration, kernel methods) with practical earth observation applications.

name: Francois Lauze
title: Associate Professor
summary: François Lauze is an Associate Professor who specializes in mathematical foundations of image analysis, using variational methods and differential geometry to solve computer vision and medical imaging problems including photometric stereo, refractive structure-from-motion, tomographic reconstruction, and diffusion-weighted imaging segmentation.

name: Fritz Henglein
title: Professor
summary: Fritz Henglein is a Professor at DIKU working on algorithmic, semantic and logical aspects of programming languages, with a focus on type systems, type-based program analysis, functional programming, and applications to enterprise systems. His recent research includes domain-specific languages, high-performance computing, smart contracts, blockchain technology, and reversible computation.

name: James Emil Avery
title: Associate Professor
summary: James Emil Avery works on computational methods spanning quantum chemistry (electronic structure calculations using Sturmian basis sets), mathematical structures (graph theory and topology of fullerenes and polyhedral molecules), and programming language theory (automatic program analysis and formal verification). His research bridges computational physics, pure mathematics, and computer science.

name: Jens Petersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jens Petersen is an Associate Professor specializing in medical image analysis, particularly focusing on segmentation and statistical analysis of tubular and branching structures such as airways and carotid arteries using discrete graph-based methods and optimal surface techniques.

name: Joanna Emilia Bergström
title: Associate Professor
summary: Joanna Bergström is a human-computer interaction researcher who focuses on virtual reality design and interaction techniques, particularly how users learn to control virtual bodies and map physical movements to virtual actions based on neuroscience and motor learning principles.

name: Maria Maistro
title: Associate Professor
summary: Maria Maistro is an associate professor at DIKU (University of Copenhagen) who conducts research in information retrieval, focusing on evaluation methodologies, reproducibility, click log analysis, and machine learning applications to search and recommendation systems.

name: Melanie Ganz-Benjaminsen
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Melanie Ganz-Benjaminsen develops medical image processing and machine learning methods for neuroimaging analysis (MRI/PET data), with applications to psychiatric diagnosis and radiotherapy. Her work spans image preprocessing, segmentation, registration, and AI algorithms for clinical decision-making in mental health and cancer treatment.

name: Michael Kastoryano
title: Associate Professor
summary: Michael Kastoryano is an Associate Professor at DIKU whose primary research focuses on quantum algorithms and tensor networks with applications to physics simulation, machine learning, finance, and engineering. His recent work includes developing quantum algorithms for simulating thermal properties of materials and fault-tolerant quantum computing architectures with photonic systems.

name: Mikkel Abrahamsen
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Mikkel Abrahamsen is an associate professor working on discrete and computational geometry, focusing on algorithmic complexity of packing, covering, and partitioning problems with geometric shapes, particularly polygons and unit squares.

name: Naja Holten Møller
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Naja Holten Møller is an expert on Computer-Supported Cooperative Work (CSCW) in complex, professional work domains, with research focused on responsibility and ethics through stakeholder participation in technology use and design. She investigates ethnographically how data-driven technologies transform work and explores how to balance human values while designing technologies that automate tasks and introduce algorithms in decision-making.

name: Omry Ross
title: Associate Professor
summary: Omry Ross researches the application of blockchain and distributed ledger technology in financial services, with focus on trade processing, settlement, derivatives, compliance, and decentralized finance systems. His work bridges computer science and fintech, exploring how blockchain can improve post-trade infrastructure and financial governance.

name: Oswin Krause
title: Associate Professor
summary: Oswin Krause is a machine learning researcher at the University of Copenhagen working on evolution strategies for optimization problems, reinforcement learning applications, deep learning methods, and medical image analysis. His recent work extends to quantum device simulation and parameter learning for quantum systems.

name: Srikanth Srinivasan
title: Associate Professor
summary: Srikanth Srinivasan is a theoretical computer scientist specializing in computational complexity theory, with particular focus on circuit complexity, pseudorandom objects, and algebraic complexity. His recent work investigates algebraic circuits, threshold circuits, and Boolean functions with applications to lower bounds and proof systems.

name: Tariq Osman Andersen
title: Associate Professor (promotion track)
summary: Tariq Osman Andersen researches how to design digital health systems through participatory approaches involving patients, clinicians, and stakeholders. His work focuses on patient-centered eHealth platforms, remote cardiac monitoring, patient-generated data interpretation, and increasingly on AI applications in healthcare and emergency services.

name: Tijs Slaats
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tijs Slaats is an Associate Professor at DIKU who researches declarative approaches to business process management, with a focus on the Dynamic Condition Response (DCR) Graphs formal model. His work spans process mining, workflow modeling, case management systems, and smart contracts, including both theoretical analysis and practical industrial applications.

name: Torben Ægidius Mogensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mogensen researches automatic program analysis and transformation techniques, particularly partial evaluation and program semi-inversion, alongside reversible computation models, compiler design for functional languages, and domain-specific language development.

name: Tuukka Ruotsalo
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tuukka Ruotsalo develops cognitive models that integrate machine learning with human-computer interaction, using brain signals (EEG), physiological measurements, and behavioral data to build systems that learn user preferences and intentions. His work focuses on creating personalized AI systems that can decode human cognition for applications in information retrieval, recommender systems, and brain-computer interfaces.

name: Amartya Sanyal
