summary: Niels Johannesen is a Professor of Public Economics at the University of Copenhagen and Oxford University whose research focuses primarily on tax evasion, tax avoidance, and the use of tax havens by firms and wealthy households. His work examines international taxation, multinational firm tax strategies, financial sector regulation, and the effects of tax policies on household behavior and inequality.

name: Nikolaj Arpe Harmon
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nikolaj Arpe Harmon is a labor and political economist who studies job search behavior, immigration effects on politics, public sector selection and honesty, and peer effects in legislative voting using econometric methods. His research combines administrative labor market data with experimental and natural experiments to understand labor market dynamics, political economy, and corruption.

name: Pablo Selaya
title: Associate Professor
summary: Pablo Selaya is an Associate Professor of Economics at the University of Copenhagen who researches comparative economic and political development, with particular focus on how institutions, culture, and historical factors shape long-run economic outcomes. His work examines topics including infrastructure persistence, the cultural origins of gender-based violence, foreign aid effectiveness, and the long-term health effects of historical migration patterns.

name: Paolo Falco
title: Associate Professor
summary: Paolo Falco uses experimental methods to study human behavior in labor markets, with a focus on understanding how to improve labor market functioning and remove obstacles for disadvantaged workers in developing countries. His research examines job search frictions, employer biases, worker cooperation, and employment outcomes through field experiments and randomized controlled trials.

name: Peter Birch Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Birch Sørensen is an environmental and public economist who researches climate policy, environmental taxation, and green national accounting. His recent work focuses on optimal unilateral climate policy, emissions trading systems, renewable energy transitions, natural resource valuation, and the measurement of environmental impacts on national income.

name: Peter Kjær Kruse-Andersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Peter Kjær Kruse-Andersen is an Associate Professor specializing in economic growth and environmental economics, with research focusing on how environmental policies and climate change affect long-run growth through directed innovation, carbon leakage in open economies, and optimal design of climate policies including carbon taxation and energy taxes.

name: Peter Norman Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Norman Sørensen is a Professor of Finance at the University of Copenhagen who develops economic theory to study how individuals make decisions under asymmetric information. His research focuses on herding behavior in financial markets, reputation-building among experts, information aggregation in prediction markets, and how asset prices respond to private and public information.

name: Rasmus Søndergaard Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Rasmus Søndergaard Pedersen specializes in financial econometrics and econometric theory, with a focus on time series methods for handling heavy-tailed distributions and financial volatility models like GARCH. His research develops robust inference techniques including bootstrap methods for parameter boundary problems and tests that work without requiring finite moments.

name: Robert Mahlstedt
title: Associate Professor
summary: Robert Mahlstedt is an Associate Professor of Economics at the University of Copenhagen who researches labor economics and behavioral economics, focusing on how psychological motives and behavioral concepts influence job search behavior of unemployed workers and their subsequent labor market outcomes. His work includes evaluating active labor market programs, regional labor market mobility, and unemployment dynamics using applied microeconomic methods.

name: Stefan Voigt
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Stefan Voigt researches financial markets using econometric and computational methods, with primary focus on market microstructure, asset pricing, and the economic implications of blockchain technology in financial systems. His work spans portfolio optimization under transaction costs, high-frequency trading dynamics, and big data applications in finance.

name: Steffen Altmann
title: Associate Professor
summary: Steffen Altmann studies behavioral economics and labor economics, with a focus on applying behavioral insights to labor market policies and organizational settings. His research involves experimental and field-based approaches to understand how cognitive limitations, defaults, nudges, and incentives influence worker behavior and economic decision-making.

name: Søren Leth-Petersen
title: Professor
summary: Søren Leth-Petersen is an applied economist studying household finance, consumption, and savings behavior, with particular focus on how savings decisions relate to housing, mortgages, house prices, and income expectations. His research combines administrative data with surveys and econometric methods to understand individual financial decisions and their distributional consequences.

name: Søren Hove Ravn
title: Associate Professor
summary: Søren Hove Ravn studies macroeconomics with focus on monetary policy, fiscal policy, and interactions between financial markets and the macroeconomy using macroeconomic modeling and econometric methods. His recent work examines fiscal multipliers in small open economies with heterogeneous households, house price dynamics and productivity effects of government spending, and credit cycles.

name: Thomas Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Thomas Jensen is an economic theorist specializing in applied game theory and political economy, with research focusing on electoral competition, strategic ambiguity in political messaging, candidate signaling, and security economics including terrorism dynamics.

name: Thomas Markussen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Markussen studies how groups implement institutions to solve collective action problems through laboratory experiments, with particular focus on punishment mechanisms and social dilemmas. He also investigates political economy and institutions in developing countries, especially Vietnam, examining relationships between political connections, property rights, and cooperation.

name: Thomas Høgholm Jørgensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Thomas Høgholm Jørgensen is a computational economist who develops and estimates dynamic economic models of family behavior, focusing on how fertility, children, labor supply, and retirement decisions interact over the life cycle. His research combines microeconomic theory with structural estimation techniques and uses Danish register data to study household economics and the effects of tax and welfare policy.

name: Torben Heien Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Torben Heien Nielsen is an economist studying how health, labor markets, and inequalities interact. His research examines physician careers, family responses to health shocks, health inequalities, and the long-run effects of early career outcomes on gender differences in earnings and occupational choices.

name: Rebecca Adler-Nissen
title: Professor
summary: Rebecca Adler-Nissen studies international relations through the lens of political sociology and practice theory, examining how diplomats and EU member states negotiate sovereignty, integration, and identity. Her research combines anthropological fieldwork and computational methods to analyze digital technologies in diplomacy, European opt-outs, and emerging issues like digital disinformation and AI governance.

name: Jonathan Luke Austin
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jonathan Luke Austin is a political sociologist studying global political violence, examining both its ontologies (through perpetrator interviews and case studies of torture) and prevention strategies. His research integrates design theory, aesthetics, and emerging technologies with international security problems, pioneering an 'International Political Design' approach that applies material-aesthetic and ergonomic thinking to humanitarian challenges including war and forced displacement.

name: Anders Berg-Sørensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Associate Professor in Political Theory interested in how the relationship between religion and politics is contested in European public discourse and political thought, with particular focus on the political doctrine of secularism and democratic governance principles in post-war European institutions.

name: Michele Merrill Betsill
title: Professor
summary: Michele Betsill is a Professor of Global Environmental Politics whose research examines how non-state and sub-national actors participate in transnational governance initiatives addressing climate change, marine conservation, and natural resource extraction. Her pioneering work on cities and climate change established new approaches to understanding climate politics as simultaneously local and global phenomena, with significant focus on NGO diplomacy and the mechanisms through which non-governmental organizations exercise agency in global environmental governance.

name: Christian Bueger
title: Professor
summary: Christian Bueger is Professor of International Relations focusing on global ocean politics and maritime security, studying political responses to maritime insecurity and the knowledge, resources, and technologies required to govern the oceans. He draws on and advances international practice theory and praxiography as his primary methodological approach.

name: Dean Cooper-Cunningham
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Dean Cooper-Cunningham is an Assistant Professor of International Relations who intersects feminist and queer theory, critical security studies, and visual politics. His research examines how sexuality and gender operate in international politics and foreign policy, particularly how marginalized groups use visual and bodily forms of activism to resist state violence and challenge security discourses.

name: Peter Dahler-Larsen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Dahler-Larsen is a professor specializing in evaluation research, evaluation systems, and performance management. His work examines how evaluation shapes organizational and political systems, explores the constitutive effects of evaluative practices, and investigates the institutionalization of evaluation across Europe using qualitative methods.

name: Peter Thisted Dinesen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Thisted Dinesen is a professor of political science at University of Copenhagen studying how social and political attitudes are formed, with primary focus on generalized social trust, the effects of immigration and ethnic diversity on trust and civic participation, and how violent events like terrorism shape political behavior and public opinion.

name: Gregory Eady
title: Associate Professor
summary: Gregory Eady is an Associate Professor of Political Science at the University of Copenhagen who studies the intersection of political behavior, public opinion, social media, and quantitative methods. His research investigates how social media platforms shape political attitudes and behavior, including topics such as foreign influence campaigns, online discourse quality, ideological polarization, and the effects of political content exposure on voting and public opinion.

name: Kristin Anabel Eggeling
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Kristin Anabel Eggeling is an Assistant Professor studying how digital technologies transform diplomatic practice and international relations. Her research combines ethnographic fieldwork with international relations theory to examine diplomacy, global tech governance, digital sovereignty, and identity politics through constructivist and interpretive approaches.

name: Mads Andreas Elkjær
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: His research focuses on the political and democratic consequences of economic inequality in advanced democracies, with broad interests in political representation, comparative politics, and political economy. He engages with questions of how rising economic inequality affects the distribution of political power and influence in advanced democracies.

name: Anders Esmark
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anders Esmark studies how public governance has been transformed through policy paradigms like New Public Management and network governance, with particular focus on tensions between technocratic approaches and democratic processes, as well as how political and media framing shapes welfare and social policy debates.

name: Yevgeniy Golovchenko
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Golovchenko studies the flow of political information across borders, focusing on disinformation, propaganda, and censorship on social media platforms. He applies computational methods to analyze how state actors and civil society disseminate competing narratives on Twitter and YouTube, particularly in contexts of international conflict.

name: Sara Hagemann
title: Professor
summary: Sara Hagemann is a Professor of European Politics and Public Policy at University of Copenhagen who studies political processes, representation, and transparency in European Union institutions, particularly the Council of Ministers. Her research examines government responsiveness to public opinion, voting behavior in EU legislative bodies, and the relationship between transparency and democratic accountability.

name: Kasper Møller Hansen
title: Professor
summary: Kasper Møller Hansen is a professor of political science at the University of Copenhagen specializing in quantitative research on democracy and elections. His work focuses on electoral behavior, using field experiments and national survey data to study voter turnout, campaign effects, and deliberative democratic participation in Denmark.

name: Lene Hansen
title: Professor
summary: Lene Hansen is a leading scholar in critical security studies who uses poststructuralist, feminist, and constructivist approaches to understand how discourse, images, and identity shape international security and foreign policy.

name: Jacob Gerner Hariri
title: Professor
summary: Jacob Gerner Hariri is a comparative politics professor studying processes of state formation, democracy and democratization, and political and economic development over the long run. His recent work examines arms technology diffusion, institutional persistence through colonization, welfare state preferences, and methodological issues in political science survey research.

name: Kevin Jon Heller
title: Professor
summary: Kevin Jon Heller is a Professor of International Law and Security at the University of Copenhagen's Centre for Military Studies who specializes in international criminal law, humanitarian law, and the legal dimensions of military operations. His research focuses on war crimes tribunals, international criminal prosecution, and the legal frameworks governing armed conflict and emerging military technologies.

name: Silje Synnøve Lyder Hermansen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
topics: Court of Justice of the European Union
summary: She researches judicial and legislative behavior among representatives at the EU-level with a special interest for the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) and the European Parliament (EP). She applies primarily quantitative methods with extensive experience in automated data collection and Bayesian estimation strategies.

name: Frederik Hjorth
title: Associate Professor
summary: Frederik Hjorth is an Associate Professor studying political behavior with a focus on how citizens and mainstream parties respond to the rise of right-wing populist parties, and how citizens form attitudes toward immigration and ethnic diversity. He emphasizes computational and text-based methods, including analysis of parliamentary speech and social media data.

name: Katja Lindskov Jacobsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jacobsen is an Associate Professor studying security and intervention through a Science & Technology Studies lens, with particular focus on how humanitarian technologies (especially biometrics) and maritime security interventions shape global distributions of security/insecurity, with geographic emphasis on Africa.

name: Wiebke Marie Junk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Wiebke Marie Junk is an Associate Professor studying how interest groups, firms, and expert actors influence political decision-making in Europe and the EU. Her research focuses on lobbying strategies, coalition formation, and patterns of political access across multiple countries, with recent work examining crisis-driven advocacy during the COVID-19 pandemic.

name: Karina Kosiara-Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kosiara-Pedersen studies political parties and elections, focusing on party membership, organizational development, and candidate recruitment in comparative perspective. Her research examines representation patterns, electoral campaigns, gender dynamics in politics, and the relationship between parties and their members and activists.

name: Peter Kurrild-Klitgaard
title: Professor
summary: Peter Kurrild-Klitgaard is a professor of political theory and comparative politics at the University of Copenhagen who specializes in constitutional political economy, electoral systems, and political economy. He has for many years pioneered the application of rational choice models and public choice theory to the study of political institutions and their economic effects.

name: Henrik Larsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Henrik Larsen is an Associate Professor and Jean Monnet Chair in International Relations who studies European and Danish foreign policy, with particular focus on how small EU member states balance national and European interests. His research employs discourse analysis and performativity approaches to examine foreign policy substance, EU normative power, and issues including Greenland's autonomy and Arctic governance.

name: Tobias Liebetrau
title: Associate Professor
summary: Liebetrau is a political scientist examining cybersecurity, digital technology, and infrastructure as strategic and political phenomena. His research focuses on how large tech companies function as international political actors, tensions between defense and offense in cyber contexts, and the governance of critical digital and maritime infrastructures.

name: Martin Marcussen
title: Professor
summary: Martin Marcussen is a professor of political science specializing in diplomacy and foreign policy analysis, with particular expertise in how small states navigate European integration, city-level diplomatic actors, and the governance of international institutions like central banks.

name: Dorte Sindbjerg Martinsen
title: Professor
summary: Dorte Sindbjerg Martinsen is a professor who researches EU integration and Europeanization, focusing particularly on how welfare policies develop through interactions between legal institutions (especially the European Court of Justice) and political decision-making processes. Her work examines implementation, enforcement, and compliance with EU policies across health, social security, and free movement domains.

name: Jens Ladefoged Mortensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jens Ladefoged Mortensen is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen who studies international political economy with a focus on EU trade politics, bilateral trade agreements, and the intersection of global trade with development and environmental governance.

name: Maria Mälksoo
title: Professor
summary: Maria Mälksoo is a Professor of International Relations specializing in critical security studies, memory politics, and political anthropology. Her research examines how ritual action shapes deterrence credibility in international politics, particularly regarding NATO's eastern flank and Russia, and how states use memory laws and historical narratives to construct national identity and ontological security.

name: Peter Nedergaard
title: Professor
summary: Peter Nedergaard is a professor of political science specializing in comparative politics and the political economy of European integration, particularly analyzing EU institutions, agricultural policy, market integration, and Nordic cooperation through rational choice, social constructivism, and integration theories.

name: Asmus Leth Olsen
title: Professor
summary: Asmus Leth Olsen studies how citizens form inferences about public services and how performance data shape their evaluations, with a focus on honesty, discrimination, and cognitive biases. He advances public administration through experimental methods and behavioral approaches, integrating insights from psychology.

name: Mogens Jin Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Pedersen is a political scientist studying public administration with emphasis on public employee motivation, bureaucratic behavior, and behavioral decision-making in public organizations. His research employs experimental designs and survey methodology to examine how cognitive biases, discrimination, and incentives affect public sector performance.

name: Lene Holm Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Lene Holm Pedersen is a professor of political science and public management who conducts research on steering, motivation, and performance in the public sector, with particular focus on how institutional constraints and individual motivations affect political participation and leadership. Her research spans politician compensation, local democracy, and the relationship between citizens' trust and their attitudes toward public officials.

name: Signe Pihl-Thingvad
title: Head of Department
summary: Signe Pihl-Thingvad is a researcher focused on organizational psychology, public management, and institutional governance. Her work examines leadership styles, employee innovation, workplace environment quality, and how governance reforms in public institutions affect organizational behavior and academic practice.

name: Carolin Hjort Rapp
title: Associate Professor
summary: Carolin Hjort Rapp is an Associate Professor at Copenhagen's Department of Political Science who researches the intersection of political psychology and comparative politics, focusing on how national identity and group belonging shape attitudes toward minorities, immigrants, and democracy across different countries.

name: Mikkel Vedby Rasmussen
title: Professor
summary: Mikkel Vedby Rasmussen is a distinguished scholar in international relations and defence studies who has made significant contributions to strategic studies, defence policy, and international security. His research focuses on crisis, armed conflict, risk analysis, wicked problems and change leadership, NATO, and Danish security and defence policy.

name: Anne Rasmussen
title: Professor
summary: Anne Rasmussen is a professor studying how interest groups, public opinion, and political representation interact in policymaking. Her research uses experimental and quantitative methods to examine lobbying, digital democracy, and the responsiveness of elected representatives to different constituencies.

name: Karsten Ronit
title: Associate Professor
summary: Karsten Ronit studies organized interests, particularly private organizations and associations operating in global politics. His work examines how business associations, consumer organizations, and other intermediary institutions influence policy-making, with special attention to industry governance, business self-regulation, and the use of evolutionary biology as an analytical framework for understanding organizational diversity in politics.

name: Christian F. Rostbøll
title: Professor
summary: Christian F. Rostbøll is a political theorist who examines the philosophical foundations of democracy, focusing on how societies recognize citizens and manage political disagreement. His recent work analyzes populism as a struggle for recognition and explores the tension between populism and democratic ideals of respect and compromise.

name: Adam Scharpf
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Adam Scharpf is a comparative politics scholar who studies how political regimes, particularly autocracies, produce loyalty and allegiance through their security apparatuses and repressive mechanisms. His research examines the organizational behavior of dictatorial institutions like secret police and militaries, the role of career incentives in facilitating state violence, and international strategies autocrats use to improve their image abroad.

name: Emily Flore St Denny
title: Associate Professor
summary: Emily Flore St Denny investigates how gender, health, and social inequalities are addressed through comparative public policy across France and the devolved UK. Her research examines preventive policy implementation, gender mainstreaming approaches, and develops feminist methods for evaluating policy effectiveness in reducing inequalities.

name: Marlene Wind
title: Professor
summary: Marlene Wind specializes in the law, politics, and policy of the European Union, with particular focus on the interplay between judicial systems, sovereignty, and European integration. Her research examines how national courts interact with EU institutions, constitutional questions in EU member states, and contemporary challenges to democratic values and rule of law across Europe.

name: Anders Wivel
title: Professor
summary: Anders Wivel is a Professor of International Relations whose primary research focus is how small states overcome power asymmetry and vulnerability in international relations. His work combines neoclassical realism theory with empirical analyses of foreign policy, international organizations, and peaceful change in an international system dominated by power politics.

name: Ole Wæver
title: Professor
summary: Ole Wæver is internationally known for coining the concept of 'securitization' within security theory and as a main figure in developing the 'Copenhagen School' in security studies. His most recent writings have applied securitization theory to religion and to climate change.

name: Maja Zehfuss
title: Professor
summary: Maja Zehfuss researches the politics of making migrants and hierarchies of political subjectivity in contemporary global politics. Her work examines migration, temporality, memory, war, and ethics through critical international relations theory.

name: Alice el-Wakil
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Alice el-Wakil is a political theorist who examines whether innovative and traditional political institutions can translate democratic principles into practice. Her research focuses on the democratic potential of referendums, initiatives, and citizen participation mechanisms, addressing core questions about accountability, representation, and deliberation in democratic systems.

name: Adéla Plechatá
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Adéla Plechatá investigates how immersive virtual reality and extended reality technologies can promote prosocial behavior, particularly in environmental and health communication contexts. Her research spans VR-based interventions for pro-environmental behavioral change (dietary choices, climate action), health communication applications, and cognitive rehabilitation for psychiatric patients.

name: Anders Petersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anders Petersen is an Associate Professor in quantitative methods who develops mathematical and computational tools to investigate visual attention and short-term memory using eye tracking, EEG, and pupillometry, with a focus on the Theory of Visual Attention framework and temporal dynamics of attention. He also conducts research on cognitive control, task-switching flexibility, and attentional deficits in clinical populations.

name: Anne Ranning
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anne Ranning is a clinical psychologist who studies intergenerational transmission of mental illness and the impact of parental serious mental illness on children's outcomes. She conducts nationwide register-based cohort studies and leads randomized controlled trials of family-based preventive interventions, particularly the Family Talk Intervention, to mitigate risk factors for children of parents with mental illness and suicidal behavior.

name: Asmus Vogel
title: Associate Professor
summary: Asmus Vogel is a clinical neuropsychologist researching cognitive deficits in dementia diseases, particularly Alzheimer's disease and mild cognitive impairment. He develops and validates neuropsychological tests for use in memory clinics and studies cognitive and personality changes across various neurodegenerative conditions including Huntington's disease.

name: Birgitte Fagerlund
title: Professor
summary: Birgitte Fagerlund is a Professor of Clinical Neuropsychology at University of Copenhagen who studies cognitive deficits and neurobiological markers in schizophrenia, particularly first-episode antipsychotic-naïve patients. Her research combines neuropsychological assessment with neuroimaging (white matter, functional connectivity) and electrophysiology (EEG) to understand cognitive and functional outcomes across the psychosis spectrum and related conditions including ADHD and neurodevelopmental disorders.

name: Bo Bach
title: Professor
summary: Bo Bach is a clinical psychology professor at the University of Copenhagen who specializes in assessment, classification, and treatment of personality disorders and psychopathology. His major focus is operationalizing the ICD-11 personality disorder framework and the DSM-5 Alternative Model, including differential diagnosis with complex PTSD and autism spectrum conditions.

name: Brady Wagoner
title: Professor
summary: Brady Wagoner is a cultural psychologist studying how people construct meaning through memory, social narratives, and cultural processes. His research examines collective memory formation, memorial interpretation, social change, and conspiracy beliefs using dialogical and qualitative methods within a culturally sensitive psychological framework.

name: Charlotte Bjerre Meilstrup
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Charlotte Bjerre Meilstrup is a postdoctoral researcher studying public mental health promotion, with focus on developing and evaluating population-wide interventions to promote mental wellbeing in children, adolescents, and students. She manages the ABCs of Mental Health program and works on translating mental health research into practical school and university initiatives.

name: Dea Siggaard Stenbæk
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dr. Stenbæk is an Associate Professor specializing in neuropsychopharmacological effects of psilocybin and the serotonin system. Her research combines experimental pharmacology with clinical applications, investigating how serotonin receptor signaling (particularly 5-HT2AR) relates to consciousness, personality, and neurocognitive function, with a focus on psychedelic-assisted therapy for psychiatric and palliative conditions.

name: Dora Kampis
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Dora Kampis studies how social cognitive abilities develop in infants and young children, including theory of mind, perspective-taking, and altercentric reasoning. She uses methods like eye-tracking, EEG neuroimaging, and behavioral tasks to investigate the cognitive mechanisms underlying human social nature and how these abilities emerge and develop across early childhood.

name: Emanuela Yeung
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Emanuela Yeung is an assistant professor studying how infants and young children develop social understanding, self-awareness, and executive function. Her research uses eye-tracking and behavioral tasks to investigate the emergence of perspective-taking abilities, self-concept, and memory biases in early development.

name: Guido Makransky
title: Professor
summary: Makransky investigates how people learn in immersive environments (virtual/augmented reality and AI tools) by combining laboratory research with classroom experiments, drawing on cognitive psychology to develop evidence-based theories and instructional methods that enhance learning outcomes and educational practices.

name: Hana Malá Rytter
title: Associate Professor
summary: Hana Malá Rytter is an Associate Professor of cognitive neuroscience and neuropsychology studying brain organization and reorganization following brain injury using animal models and clinical research. Her work focuses on how cognitive functions like spatial memory, executive functioning, and behavioral flexibility recover after brain injury through pharmacological interventions, enriched environments, and exercise, with particular attention to prefrontal cortex and hippocampal function.

name: Ida Egmose Pedersen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Ida Egmose Pedersen studies early parent-child interactions, infant attachment, and parental mentalization, with a focus on developing and evaluating early interventions to support parenting competence and child socio-emotional development in families at risk, particularly those affected by postpartum depression.

name: Ingo Zettler
title: Professor
summary: Ingo Zettler is a professor of personality and social behavior at the University of Copenhagen who studies the Dark Factor of Personality (D), a common core underlying aversive traits like narcissism and psychopathy. His research examines dishonesty, personality assessment, behavioral economics, organizational behavior, and how personality influences individual differences in ethical conduct and social outcomes.

name: Jason William Burton
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Jason Burton studies the computational mechanisms of human reasoning and decision-making, examining how digital technologies affect cognitive processes and how information environments can be redesigned to promote collective intelligence.

name: Jesper Dammeyer
title: Professor
summary: Jesper Dammeyer is a professor of psychology specializing in developmental and health psychology, with particular focus on the social, communicative, and cognitive development of people with sensory loss—including deafness, blindness, and deafblindness. His research examines communication systems, language acquisition, and psychosocial wellbeing across the lifespan in individuals with disability.

name: Jochen Eberhard Gebauer
title: Professor
summary: Gebauer conducts cross-cultural social and personality psychology research examining how cultural norms shape self-esteem, personality expression, and well-being. His work integrates Big Five personality traits with cultural context, investigating phenomena like communal narcissism, person-culture fit effects, and regional personality variation across nations.

name: Johanne Smith-Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Johanne Smith-Nielsen is an associate professor and licensed clinical psychologist focusing on perinatal and infant mental health, with particular emphasis on early detection of postpartum depression and interventions to promote secure attachment and healthy caregiver-child relationships in both family and childcare settings.

name: Karen-Inge Karstoft
title: Professor
summary: Karstoft's main research interests are within clinical and health psychology, specifically examining how individuals react differently to trauma and how posttraumatic stress disorder symptoms develop over time in trauma-exposed individuals, with a focus on applying machine learning methods for predicting mental health problems following trauma exposure and studying the complexity of psychopathology.

name: Katarina Begus
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Katarina Begus is an Assistant Professor studying cognitive development, curiosity, and learning mechanisms in infants during their first and second years of life. Her research combines behavioral methods with neuroimaging techniques (EEG, fNIRS) to understand how infants actively seek information, form social preferences, and develop causal reasoning.

name: Katrine Røhder
title: Associate Professor
summary: Katrine Røhder investigates early parenting and parent-infant interactions, particularly among vulnerable populations including women with severe mental illness and psychosocial risk factors. Her research uses attachment theory and developmental psychopathology frameworks to examine caregiving representations, risk mechanisms, and perinatal interventions that support healthy family relationships.

name: Katrine Isabella Wendelboe
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Katrine Isabella Wendelboe is a postdoctoral researcher studying how parents' capacity to reflect on their child's mental states (parental mentalizing) relates to infant development, attachment patterns, and postpartum mental health. Her work examines both mothers and fathers, with particular focus on early intervention programs and the quality of caregiver-child interactions in the postpartum period.

name: Laila Øksnebjerg
title: Associate Professor
summary: Laila Øksnebjerg is a neuropsychologist studying dementia care, focusing on the design and evaluation of assistive technologies for people with dementia, psychosocial interventions, caregiver outcomes, and the involvement of people with dementia in research and care design.

name: Lau Lilleholt Harpviken
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Lau Lilleholt Harpviken is a behavioral economist and psychologist studying how personality traits, particularly pro- and antisocial characteristics, influence economic decisions and ethical behavior. His research spans judgment and decision-making, behavioral ethics, and social data science, with recent work examining phenomena like pandemic fatigue, health behavior compliance, and the relationship between economic scarcity and moral behavior.

name: Line Nielsen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Line Nielsen is a postdoctoral researcher at the Department of Psychology, University of Copenhagen, focusing on mental health promotion, particularly among children and adolescents. Her work emphasizes translating research into practical interventions while addressing social inequalities in mental health, including projects like the ABCs of mental health initiative and the Optur school-based intervention.

name: Louise Birkedal Glenthøj
title: Associate Professor
summary: Louise Birkedal Glenthøj is an associate professor and psychologist who leads the VIRTU Research Group at Mental Health Center Copenhagen. Her research focuses on advancing clinical assessment and psychotherapeutic treatment of psychiatric disorders, with particular emphasis on virtual reality-based therapy, cognitive deficits assessment, and large-scale clinical trials across psychosis, autism, and eating disorders.

name: Madeleine Chapman
title: Associate Professor
summary: Madeleine Chapman studies the social psychology and experiences of Deaf and hard of hearing populations, particularly adolescents, examining cultural identity, social participation, and well-being. Her work combines qualitative and survey-based approaches to understand minority identity, disability experiences, and social integration across diverse populations.

name: Mark Schram Christensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mark Schram Christensen is an Associate Professor at University of Copenhagen's Department of Psychology who researches cognitive control of movements, focusing on how people perceive and experience agency over their own actions. His work employs neurophysiological methods including TMS, EEG, and fMRI to investigate the neural mechanisms underlying motor control, movement perception, and sense of agency.

name: Martin Vestergaard Gøtzsche
title: Associate Professor
summary: Martin Vestergaard Gøtzsche is a research psychologist focusing on child and adolescent psychiatry, with particular expertise in autism interventions (including parent-mediated PACT therapy), personality disorders, and emotion dysregulation in developmental populations.

name: Mette Skovgaard Væver
title: Professor
summary: Mette Skovgaard Væver is a professor of clinical child psychology specializing in early childhood (0-6 years) mental health, attachment, and parenting. She directs the Center for Early Intervention and Family Studies, conducting applied research on infant mental health screening, preventive interventions, and parent-child interaction patterns using both laboratory-based and community settings.

name: Milan Obaidi
title: Associate Professor
summary: Milan Obaidi is an associate professor studying the social and psychological mechanisms underlying violent extremism, intergroup conflict, and radicalization. His research examines how factors like threat perception, relative deprivation, group identity, and conspiracy beliefs interact to drive both Islamist and right-wing extremism, using experimental, longitudinal, and comparative quantitative and qualitative methods.

name: Monika Anna Walczak
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Monika Anna Walczak is a clinical psychologist investigating anxiety and depression disorders in children and adolescents, with particular focus on treatment outcomes and efficacy of cognitive behavioral therapy and metacognitive therapy approaches. Her research examines factors like parental involvement, attachment patterns, and predictors of treatment response in young people with internalizing disorders.

name: Niccolò Fiorentino Polipo
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Polipo is a psychoanalytic researcher and clinician integrating philosophy and psychology, focusing on three axes: epistemology (clinical reasoning skills for therapists), ethics (depth-psychological virtues for character development), and culture (psychoanalytic analysis of sociocultural phenomena), relying particularly on Jungian and Bionian ideas.

name: Nicoline Hemager
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nicoline Hemager is an associate professor studying neurocognitive, social-cognitive, and psychosocial outcomes in children born to parents with schizophrenia or bipolar disorder, with a focus on early risk factors, family-based preventive interventions, and longitudinal development.

name: Oliver James Hulme
title: Associate Professor
summary: Oliver James Hulme is a computational neuroscientist studying how the brain processes reward and makes decisions. His work combines fMRI neuroimaging, Bayesian modeling, and economic theory to investigate reward signaling mechanisms, risk preferences, and the neural basis of decision-making.

name: Paul Maurice Conway
title: Associate Professor
summary: Paul Maurice Conway is an Associate Professor studying occupational health psychology, focusing on workplace stressors like bullying and their effects on employee mental health, well-being, and work ability using prospective cohort and Danish register-based epidemiological methods.

name: Pia Ingold
title: Professor
summary: Pia Ingold is a Professor of Work and Organizational Psychology at the University of Copenhagen who specializes in personnel selection, assessment methods, and workplace behavior. Her research focuses on the validity and effectiveness of selection instruments like assessment centers, interviews, and personality measures, as well as impression management and behavioral prediction in organizational contexts.

name: Randi Starrfelt
title: Professor
summary: Randi Starrfelt is a professor of neuropsychology at the University of Copenhagen who studies cognitive neuropsychological deficits following acquired brain damage, with particular emphasis on visual disorders including impairments in face recognition, reading, and word recognition. Her research uses experimental psychology, psychophysics, and brain imaging methods to investigate how posterior brain damage affects visual processing and to develop better assessment and rehabilitation approaches for these conditions.

name: Ro Julia Robotham
title: Associate Professor
summary: Robotham is an Associate Professor in cognitive and clinical neuropsychology who conducts research on how the brain processes visual information and how to assess visual perceptual deficits following brain injury and developmental conditions. She develops neuropsychological assessment methods for acquired brain injury, stroke, and cerebral palsy.

name: Sara Kerstine Kaya Nielsen
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Sara Nielsen investigates psychological factors predicting treatment response in anxiety disorders and OCD, particularly examining emotion regulation, attachment style, and attentional control as predictors of cognitive behavioral therapy and acceptance and commitment therapy outcomes in clinical settings.

name: Sebastian Simonsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sebastian Simonsen is an associate professor focused on psychotherapy and psychiatry from a personality perspective. He specializes in mentalization-based treatment approaches for personality disorders, particularly borderline and avoidant personality disorders, with research on how personality functioning relates to treatment outcomes.

name: Simo Køppe
title: Professor
summary: Simo Køppe is a professor focusing on the pre-verbal development of the psyche, historical development of the subject, and philosophy of science with emphasis on psychoanalytic subject models. His research examines motor intentionality in infants, early childhood exploration and play, and the theoretical foundations of psychological science.

name: Sonja Breinholst
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sonja Breinholst is an Associate Professor and head of the Center for Anxiety at the University of Copenhagen who studies childhood and adolescent anxiety disorders, with particular focus on the roles of parental behavior, parent-child attachment, and parental involvement in anxiety development and treatment outcomes. Her research employs cognitive-behavioral and metacognitive therapeutic approaches to understand how anxiety develops in children and how to effectively treat it through evidence-based interventions.

name: Sophia Armand
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Sophia Armand investigates the role of brain serotonin in psychological processes including cognition, mood, and consciousness, with a particular focus on how serotonergic psychedelics like psilocybin affect the human mind and produce lasting positive psychological changes. She combines neuroimaging, computational modeling, and behavioral assessment to examine serotonin's involvement in cognitive-affective biases, threat perception, and learning mechanisms.

name: Sophie Merrild Juul
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Sophie Merrild Juul is an Assistant Professor at the University of Copenhagen conducting research on psychotherapy interventions for psychiatric disorders, with expertise in clinical trial methodology and systematic reviews. Her work focuses on evaluating the effectiveness of different psychotherapy approaches, particularly mentalization-based therapy for borderline personality disorder, and examining methodological rigor in psychological intervention trials.

name: Stephen Fitzgerald Austin
title: Associate Professor
summary: Stephen Fitzgerald Austin is an Associate Professor in the Department of Psychology at University of Copenhagen specializing in early intervention and recovery processes in psychosis, particularly first-episode psychosis. His research integrates metacognitive approaches, trauma assessment, patient-reported outcomes, and technology-enhanced psychiatric treatments.

name: Steven Blurton
title: Associate Professor
summary: Steven Blurton develops mathematical and computational models to predict response times and accuracy in visual attention and decision-making tasks, using sequential sampling models and diffusion models to understand how humans process visual and multisensory information.

name: Stig Poulsen
title: Professor
summary: Stig Poulsen is a Professor of Psychotherapy at the University of Copenhagen who conducts empirical psychotherapy research using both quantitative and qualitative methods. His research focuses on comparing psychotherapy approaches (psychoanalytic, cognitive-behavioral, transdiagnostic) for psychiatric conditions including eating disorders, anxiety, and depression, with emphasis on client experiences and practice-based outcome measurement.

name: Sune Bo Hansen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sune Bo Hansen is an associate professor working in personality and personality disorders, with specific focus on mentalizing capacities and theory of mind. His research examines assessment and treatment of personality pathology, including borderline personality disorder in adolescents, and the role of mentalizing in psychotherapy interventions and relationships with other psychiatric conditions.

name: Séamus Anthony Power
title: Associate Professor
summary: Séamus Power studies how people psychologically experience and respond to economic inequality, migration, and social change using field social psychology and mixed methods. His research examines conditions under which citizens accept inequality versus mobilizing for protest, and explores cultural pluralism in Western democracies.

name: Søren Kyllingsbæk
title: Professor
summary: Søren Kyllingsbæk is a Professor of Cognitive Psychology whose research focuses on experimental and mathematical modeling of visual cognition, particularly visual perception, attention, and working memory. He developed the Neural Theory of Visual Attention (NTVA) and studies intentional object-oriented action through psychophysical experiments and computational modeling.

name: Thomas Habekost
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Habekost is a Professor of Cognitive Neuropsychology at the University of Copenhagen who specializes in visual and attentional processes using experimental and neuroimaging methods. His research primarily focuses on developing and applying the mathematical Theory of Visual Attention (TVA) model to assess attentional deficits in neurological, neuropsychiatric, and developmental disorders including brain damage, ADHD, and aging.

name: Thomas Morton
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Morton is a social psychologist studying how group memberships, social identity, and collective action processes shape responses to social change, ethnic diversity, and environmental challenges. His research employs qualitative methods including interviews and discourse analysis to examine identity dynamics in public spaces, urban integration policies, and mass gatherings.

name: Thomas Rune Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Thomas Rune Nielsen is an associate professor and clinical neuropsychologist at the Danish Dementia Research Centre who specializes in developing and validating cross-cultural neuropsychological assessments for diverse populations and addressing barriers to dementia diagnosis and care in minority ethnic communities across Europe.

name: Thor Grünbaum
title: Professor
summary: Thor Grünbaum is a Professor of Philosophy of Psychology at University of Copenhagen who integrates philosophical and cognitive science perspectives to study how people control their actions, experience agency, and form intentions. His research focuses on the philosophical and empirical foundations of consciousness, motor control, memory for intended actions, and the relationship between cognition and bodily experience.

name: Tone Roald
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tone Roald investigates aesthetic experiences and how people are affected by art, atmospheres, and beauty using phenomenological psychology methods. Her research explores the embodied, pre-reflective dimensions of consciousness in experiences with visual art, architectural spaces, and music, with particular focus on how these experiences shape the self.

name: Torben Bechmann Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Torben Bechmann Jensen is a social psychologist studying youth development, marginalized populations, and social wellbeing in contemporary contexts. His current work focuses on information bubbles (through the interdisciplinary CIBS center) and dignity in everyday life, while his broader research examines youth transitions, mental health, migration, health behaviors, and qualitative research methodology.

name: Victoria Helen Southgate
title: Professor
summary: Victoria Southgate is a developmental cognitive neuroscientist studying how self-representation emerges in infancy and how this milestone transforms social cognition. She uses neuroimaging methods (fNIRS, EEG) and behavioral observation to investigate action understanding, perspective-taking, mimicry, and parent-infant interaction in infants and toddlers.

name: Xuan Li
title: Associate Professor
summary: Xuan Li studies fatherhood, parent-child relationships, and children and adolescents' socioemotional and gender development with a focus on contemporary China and East Asia. Her research examines how fathers express emotions during interactions, the effects of parental acceptance on child outcomes, and how cultural contexts shape parenting behaviors and family relationships.

name: Kristian Almstrup
title: Professor
summary: Kristian Almstrup studies how genetic and epigenetic variations affect male reproductive function, with specific focus on testicular gene expression, spermatogenesis, and small RNAs (piRNAs and miRNAs) as biomarkers. His group investigates genetic causes of fertility disorders and testicular cancers, while also examining DNA methylation patterns during germ cell development and pubertal onset using advanced molecular techniques.

name: Simon Bekker-Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Simon Bekker-Jensen is a Professor of Cellular Stress Responses studying how cells sense and respond to stress through MAP kinase signaling pathways, with research spanning DNA damage responses, translational control mechanisms, centrosome homeostasis, and their connections to cellular aging.

name: Henrik Clausen
title: Professor
summary: Henrik Clausen is a professor at KU ICMM and head of Copenhagen Center for Glycomics, developing novel genetic and technological approaches to overcome barriers in glycomics research. His work focuses on discovering and engineering human glycans, mapping O-glycoproteomes, identifying cancer biomarkers, and transforming glycosciences into a mainstream omics field through innovative platforms like GlycoDisplay and GlycoCRISPR.

name: Eva Ran Hoffmann
title: Professor
summary: Eva Hoffmann studies how DNA damage response and cell cycle proteins govern genetic changes in human germline cells, particularly examining chromosome stability, meiotic recombination, and aneuploidy in oocytes to understand reproductive aging and fertility.

name: Ian Hickson
title: Professor
summary: Ian Hickson is a Professor and Director of the Center for Chromosome Stability at the University of Copenhagen who studies how chromosomal instability impacts human disease, with particular focus on defective genome maintenance genes that lead to cancer, neurodegeneration, and infertility. His research examines DNA replication stress, common fragile sites, and the molecular mechanisms underlying genetic disorders like Bloom's syndrome.

name: Marja Jäättelä
title: Professor
summary: Marja Jäättelä is a professor studying how lysosomes and autophagy regulate cancer cell death and progression. Her research focuses on lysosomal membrane permeability, lipid metabolism in lysosomes, and how these pathways can be targeted for cancer therapy.

name: Lars Allan Larsen
title: Professor
summary: Lars Allan Larsen studies genetic and molecular mechanisms of human cardiac development and congenital heart disease using genomics, functional approaches, and model organisms, with additional focus on brain and eye development ciliopathies and signaling pathways controlling embryonic development.

name: Jørgen Olsen
title: Professor
summary: Jørgen Olsen studies morphogenesis and differentiation of the gastrointestinal mucosa with a focus on bioinformatics interpretation of transcriptome and metabolome alterations during mucosal inflammation. His research translates basic mouse models of intestinal inflammation into applied studies of inflammatory bowel diseases, including development of rectal insulin as a treatment approach.

name: Ole William Petersen
title: Professor
summary: Ole William Petersen studies human breast epithelial stem cell biology and tumor microenvironment, with expertise in cell culture and humanized mouse models for breast morphogenesis. His research focuses on understanding stem cell hierarchies in normal breast tissue and breast cancer to identify cells of origin in different cancer subtypes.

name: Peter E. Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Peter E. Nielsen is a professor at University of Copenhagen who develops peptide nucleic acids (PNA), synthetic DNA mimics, for therapeutic applications including antisense antibiotics against resistant bacteria, treatments for muscular dystrophy and cystic fibrosis, and gene targeting strategies. His research encompasses the chemical biology, medicinal chemistry, and cellular/in vivo delivery of PNA-based biopharmaceuticals.

name: Lene Juel Rasmussen
title: Professor
summary: Lene Juel Rasmussen is a professor who studies the molecular mechanisms connecting hallmarks of aging and their contributions to age-related disorders. Her group investigates how DNA damage signaling and replication stress regulate mitochondrial function, and explores interactions between genomic instability, telomere attrition, epigenetic alterations, and other aging hallmarks to identify pharmaceutical targets for improving health during aging.

name: Jesper Q. Svejstrup
title: Professor
summary: Svejstrup studies the interface between transcription and other DNA-related processes, such as DNA repair, replication, and recombination. His laboratory focuses on the mechanisms of transcription with particular emphasis on transcript elongation in human cells, using biochemical, proteomic, genomic, and cell biological approaches.

name: Niels Tommerup
title: Professor
summary: Niels Tommerup is a Professor of Medical Genetics studying how balanced chromosomal rearrangements map to disease genes, chromosomal topological domains, and genetic mechanisms including germline chromothripsis and X-inactivation, while also investigating sex differential responses to viral infections and meiosis in wild animals.

name: Hans H. Wandall
title: Professor
summary: Hans H. Wandall investigates how glycans impinge on cells during tissue development, host-pathogen interactions with emphasis on viral infections, and how to exploit this for early detection and treatment of disease. He uses gene editing, organotypic tissue models, and sophisticated mass spectrometry to decipher glycan functions, with genetically engineered cells and organotypic tissue models providing evidence of the importance of glycans in tissue differentiation, cancer, and host-pathogen interactions.

name: Jeremy Turnbull
title: Professor
summary: Jeremy Turnbull is a professor specializing in molecular glycobiology with focus on the structure, function and therapeutic applications of heparan sulfates and glycosaminoglycans. His research spans glycomics approaches, structure-activity relationships, and drug discovery for cancer, neurodegenerative diseases, and spinal cord injury.

name: Ying Liu
title: Professor
summary: Ying Liu is a Professor of Cancer Genetics and Biology who studies how cells counteract replication stress and why certain genomic regions are susceptible to DNA damage, with a focus on understanding mechanisms that maintain genome stability during DNA replication and their implications for cancer and aging.

name: Alicia Lundby
title: Professor
summary: Alicia Lundby is a Professor of Cardiac Proteomics at the University of Copenhagen who uses high-resolution proteomics and multi-modal data integration to identify molecular mechanisms and protein drivers of cardiac diseases. Her research focuses on understanding protein regulation, signaling pathways, and cellular alterations in heart tissue to develop novel therapeutic targets for cardiac pathologies.

name: Andreas Kjær
title: Professor
summary: Andreas Kjær is a professor and head of the Cluster for Molecular Imaging at the University of Copenhagen, specializing in translational molecular imaging using PET and PET/MRI technologies and the development of targeted radionuclide therapies (theranostics) for precision medicine applications in cancer, cardiovascular diseases, and inflammatory conditions.

name: Bente Merete Stallknecht
title: Professor
summary: Stallknecht is a Professor in Health-Enhancing Physical Activity at University of Copenhagen who develops and tests physical activity interventions to prevent inactivity-related diseases like obesity, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Her research uses physiological methods to investigate exercise effects on metabolism and includes clinical trials on diverse populations including pregnant women, menopausal women, and obese individuals.

name: Clare Louise Hawkins
title: Professor
summary: Clare Hawkins studies how oxidative stress and inflammation drive atherosclerosis development, with a focus on neutrophil-derived products, extracellular traps, and reactive oxidants produced by myeloperoxidase. Her research examines oxidative modifications in immune cells and vascular dysfunction to develop therapeutic approaches for chronic inflammatory diseases.

name: Dominik Karl Linz
title: Professor
summary: Dominik Linz is a clinician-scientist who investigates the pathophysiology and mechanisms underlying atrial fibrillation, with particular focus on the interaction between atrial arrhythmias and sleep-disordered breathing, and the development of mechanism-driven treatment and management approaches including catheter ablation techniques and risk factor modification strategies.

name: Flemming Dela
title: Professor
summary: Flemming Dela is a professor of pathophysiology at University of Copenhagen who leads the Xlab research group, focusing on human integrative physiology in health and disease. His research examines how physical training influences metabolic processes, particularly insulin sensitivity, mitochondrial function, and metabolic adaptations in diabetes and aging.

name: Gerrit van Hall
title: Professor
summary: Gerrit van Hall is a Professor of Endocrinology and Metabolism at University of Copenhagen who investigates human metabolic responses to exercise, diet, and disease using advanced tracer techniques and metabolomics. His research spans muscle protein turnover, glucose and lipid metabolism, and the metabolic mechanisms underlying diabetes and obesity.

name: Joshua Mark Brickman
title: Professor
summary: Joshua Brickman studies how transcription factors regulate cell fate decisions in embryonic stem cells and early embryos, with particular focus on understanding the molecular mechanisms of lineage specification, pluripotency, and the transcriptional basis for endoderm priming and commitment.

name: Jens Juul Holst
title: Professor
summary: Jens Juul Holst is a professor studying translational metabolic physiology, focusing on appetite regulation and hormones that regulate metabolism, particularly incretin hormones and glucagon in the context of type 2 diabetes and obesity. His research group investigates the pathophysiology and potential therapeutic targeting of gut-derived hormones in metabolic disease.

name: Jørn Wulff Helge
title: Professor
summary: Jørn Wulff Helge is a professor of exercise physiology who studies how physical activity and inactivity affect human health across the lifespan, with particular focus on aging, skeletal muscle, obesity, and metabolic disorders including type 2 diabetes.

name: Kim Bak Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Kim Bak Jensen is a Professor at the Novo Nordisk Foundation Center for Stem Cell Medicine (reNEW) who studies the molecular mechanisms governing stem cell fate specification and tissue homeostasis, particularly in epithelial tissues. His research combines mouse models and clinical specimens to understand how extracellular signals and gene regulatory networks control tissue maturation, regeneration, and cellular plasticity, with the long-term goal of translating findings into regenerative therapies.

name: Michael J. Davies
title: Professor
summary: Michael Davies is a Professor at University of Copenhagen studying protein oxidation and damage caused by reactive species (radicals, oxidants) and their role in cardiovascular disease. His research employs LC-MS proteomics, EPR spectroscopy, and mechanistic approaches to understand and quantify protein modifications with emphasis on disease biomarkers and antioxidant development.

name: Mette Rosenkilde
title: Professor
summary: Mette Rosenkilde is a professor specializing in the structure and function of seven transmembrane G protein-coupled receptors (7TM GPCRs), with particular focus on incretin hormones and metabolic regulatory receptors in the immune and endocrine systems. Her research uses molecular pharmacology approaches to understand receptor activation mechanisms, develop novel compounds, and explore therapeutic applications for metabolic diseases including diabetes and obesity.

name: Nils Billestrup
title: Professor
summary: Nils Billestrup studies beta cell biology and function, particularly how nutrients, incretins, and gut microbial metabolites regulate insulin secretion and beta cell proliferation. His research combines cell culture and animal models with high-throughput techniques like mass spectrometry and genomics to investigate molecular mechanisms of diabetes pathogenesis and potential protective interventions.

name: Signe Sørensen Torekov
title: Professor
summary: Signe Sørensen Torekov is a Professor of Clinical and Translational Metabolism at the University of Copenhagen who leads research investigations on obesity prevention and treatment, focusing on the psychological and biological mechanisms underlying weight loss maintenance. Her group conducts clinical trials examining obesity medications (particularly GLP-1 receptor agonists), exercise interventions, metabolic hormones, genetic factors in obesity and diabetes, and the links between cardiovascular and metabolic disease.

name: Thomas Jespersen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Jespersen is a Professor at University of Copenhagen studying how cardiac fat composition and distribution affect heart function, particularly in response to diet-induced weight loss versus medication-induced weight loss. His research explores the molecular and physiological mechanisms of cardiac metabolism and arrhythmias in cardiometabolic diseases.

name: Thomas Mandrup-Poulsen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Mandrup-Poulsen is a pioneering immuno-endocrinologist who studies inflammatory and metabolic mechanisms of beta-cell destruction in diabetes. His research employs molecular, cellular, animal, and clinical approaches to understand how pro-inflammatory cytokines, oxidative stress, and metabolic dysfunction impair pancreatic islet function and viability.

name: Tor Biering-Sørensen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Tor Biering-Sørensen is a cardiologist who develops and applies advanced imaging methods—particularly echocardiography and ultrasound—to detect early signs of heart disease in asymptomatic individuals and predict cardiovascular risk. He leads pragmatic randomized clinical trials using Danish national health registries to evaluate preventive interventions, including vaccine effectiveness studies.

name: Simon Albrecht
title: Associate Professor
summary: Simon Albrecht is an associate professor studying exoplanet detection, characterization, and atmospheric composition using high-resolution spectroscopy and radial velocity methods. His research focuses on understanding planetary system architectures, stellar-planetary interactions, and the use of advanced spectrographs to detect molecules in exoplanet atmospheres.

name: Mie Andersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mie Andersen develops computational models and machine learning methods to understand and predict catalytic properties of materials at the atomic scale. Her research spans applications from CO2 conversion on metal/oxide surfaces to catalytic processes on dust grains in interstellar space, relevant for studying the origin of life.

name: Jan Joachim Arlt
title: Professor
summary: Jan Joachim Arlt is a Professor of Experimental Quantum Physics at Aarhus University who investigates fundamental quantum mechanical behavior in atomic gases at extremely low temperatures. His research focuses on ultracold quantum gases, quantum impurities (polarons) in many-body systems, and the use of cold atoms to understand complex quantum phenomena with applications to quantum technologies.

name: Peter Balling
title: Professor
summary: Peter Balling is an experimental physicist who studies light-matter interactions using ultrashort laser pulses combined with time-resolved experiments and material dynamics modeling. His research spans femtosecond laser processing, nonlinear optical phenomena in materials, terahertz radiation generation, and applications in medical physics including three-dimensional radiation dosimetry.

name: Georg Bruun
title: Professor
summary: Georg Bruun is a theoretical physicist specializing in quantum many-body physics, particularly the properties of impurities (polarons) in ultracold atomic gases and quantum degenerate systems including Bose-Einstein condensates and Fermi gases. His research uses quantum field theory and many-body methods to study exotic quantum phenomena in dipolar atoms, molecular gases, and strongly correlated quantum systems.

name: Yong Chen
title: Professor
summary: Yong P. Chen is an experimental physicist who works on diverse quantum materials including graphene, two-dimensional materials, topological insulators, and cold atoms, developing new measurement techniques and exploring applications in quantum information, nanotechnology, and sensing devices.

name: Aurelien Romain Dantan
title: Associate Professor
summary: Aurelien Dantan is an Associate Professor of experimental opto- and nanomechanical physics at Aarhus University working on optomechanical systems that exploit light-matter interactions in high-quality mechanical nanoresonators for quantum technology, sensing, and photonics applications.

name: Michael Drewsen
title: Professor
summary: Michael Drewsen leads the Ion Trap Group at Aarhus University, focusing on manipulating and studying trapped ions and molecular ions using laser cooling and spectroscopy techniques. His research explores quantum state control, ion-neutral interactions, and applications to quantum information processing and precision measurements.

name: Dmitri Fedorov
title: Associate Professor
summary: Dmitri Fedorov is an associate professor specializing in nuclear and low-energy particle physics, focusing particularly on quantum few-body systems, nucleons, and mesons. His research involves theoretical studies of bound states, wave functions, and the structure and reactions of quantum systems with few particles.

name: Hans Otto Uldall Fynbo
title: Professor
summary: Hans Otto Uldall Fynbo is an experimental nuclear physicist studying nuclear astrophysics and the structure of light nuclei, with focus on beta-decay spectroscopy, stellar nucleosynthesis processes, and nuclear resonance states relevant to cosmic fusion reactions.

name: Frank Grundahl
title: Associate Professor
summary: Frank Grundahl's research is directed towards accurate determinations of basic stellar parameters, such as mass, radius and age from photometric and spectroscopic observations, primarily conducted using the Stellar Observations Network Group (SONG) telescopes. His work combines asteroseismology with studies of stellar evolution, metallicity, and stellar mass, with red-giant branch research incorporating elements of solar-like oscillations.

name: Bjørk Hammer
title: Professor
summary: Bjørk Hammer's research focuses on the structure and properties of materials' surfaces using quantum mechanical methods for calculating atomic interactions and machine learning methods for accelerating computations.

name: Jeffrey S. Hangst
title: Professor
summary: Jeffrey Hangst is an experimental particle physicist and founder of the ALPHA collaboration at CERN who specializes in producing, trapping, and studying antihydrogen atoms through precision spectroscopy and gravity measurements to test fundamental symmetries between matter and antimatter.

name: Steen Hannestad
title: Professor
summary: Steen Hannestad is a professor of theoretical astrophysics specializing in the intersection of particle physics and cosmology. His research focuses on using precision cosmological observations to constrain neutrino properties, dark matter scenarios, and early universe physics through theoretical modeling and data analysis.

name: Philip Hofmann
title: Professor
summary: Philip Hofmann is an experimental condensed matter physicist who uses synchrotron radiation-based spectroscopy techniques to study electronic structure and electron dynamics in quantum materials. He primarily investigates surfaces of semimetals, topological insulators, and novel 2D materials including graphene and transition metal dichalcogenides.

name: Liv Hornekær
title: Professor
summary: Liv Hornekær investigates the electronic and chemical properties of carbonaceous surfaces at the atomic level, focusing on graphene and nanoparticles for electronics and coatings. She also leads research on astrochemistry, studying how interstellar dust grains catalyze the formation of complex molecules including the building blocks of life.

name: Sergio Ioppolo
title: Associate Professor
summary: Sergio Ioppolo is an astrochemist conducting laboratory experiments to understand how molecules form in space, specifically using ultrahigh vacuum systems and advanced spectroscopy techniques to study the physical and chemical processes in interstellar ice analogs. His research focuses on radiation-driven chemistry, complex organic molecule formation, and molecular processes on cosmic dust grain surfaces relevant to star and planet formation.

name: Brian Julsgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Brian Julsgaard studies light-matter interactions across quantum and nanoscale systems, with current focus on semiconductors and their optical properties. His research explores silicon nanocrystals, rare-earth ions in crystals, GeSn laser materials, and electron dynamics in wide-bandgap materials using time-resolved spectroscopy and photonic techniques.

name: Christoffer Karoff
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christoffer Karoff is an interdisciplinary researcher studying stellar magnetic activity cycles and their effects on acoustic modes in stars, as well as developing atmospheric monitoring techniques for greenhouse gas emissions using inverse modelling, remote sensing, and atmospheric dispersion models.

name: Hans Kjeldsen
title: Professor
summary: Hans Kjeldsen is a Professor of Astrophysics at Aarhus University specializing in asteroseismology—the study of stellar oscillations—to determine precise physical parameters of stars and characterize exoplanet-hosting systems. His research uses data from major space missions including Kepler and TESS to advance understanding of stellar evolution, internal structure, and the properties of planets orbiting other stars.

name: Trolle René Linderoth
title: Associate Professor
summary: Linderoth investigates molecular self-assembly and nanostructure formation on metal surfaces using scanning tunneling microscopy, with focus on understanding fundamental interactions driving molecular organization, including hydrogen bonding, metal-organic coordination, and chiral recognition phenomena.

name: Lars Bojer Madsen
title: Professor
summary: Lars Bojer Madsen is a theoretical physicist specializing in strong-field and attosecond physics, studying light-matter interactions on ultrafast timescales. His research focuses on high-order harmonic generation, ionization processes, and time-dependent many-electron phenomena in atoms, molecules, and solid-state systems driven by intense femtosecond and attosecond laser pulses.

name: Jill Miwa
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jill Miwa is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University who engineers and characterizes quantum materials using advanced spectroscopy techniques, particularly angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) and scanning tunneling microscopy. Her research focuses on delta-layers (atomically thin buried interfaces), 2D materials, and developing quantum electronic devices with novel electronic properties.

name: Anne E. B. Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anne E. B. Nielsen is a theoretical physicist studying quantum many-body systems, with particular focus on topological effects, anyons in fractional quantum Hall systems, and non-thermal phenomena like quantum many-body scars. Her research explores how to create and control anyons for potential quantum computing applications and investigates deviations from thermalization in strongly correlated quantum systems.

name: Steen Brøndsted Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Professor Nielsen studies the intrinsic photophysics of isolated molecular ions in vacuum using advanced fluorescence spectroscopy techniques, including two custom-built instruments (LUNA and LUNA2). His research focuses on understanding how molecular chromophores behave when completely isolated, with applications to understanding biological fluorescence in proteins and natural bioluminescence.

name: Jesper Olsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jesper Olsen is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University who specializes in radiocarbon dating and accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) applications to paleoclimate and paleoenvironmental research. His work focuses on using lake sediments, marine records, and stable isotopes to reconstruct long-term climate variability, particularly in Arctic and Greenland regions, and to develop improved radiocarbon dating methods for archaeological and climate science applications.

name: Henrik B. Pedersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Henrik B. Pedersen conducts experimental research on spectroscopy, dynamics, and reactions of atomic and molecular systems using accelerators, ion traps, and lasers, with specialized facilities (XRING-lab and SAPHIRA-lab) for studying atmospherically-relevant reactions and cooled molecular ions, plus educational research on non-linear mechanics.

name: Karsten Riisager
title: Associate Professor
summary: Karsten Riisager is a nuclear physicist specializing in the study of exotic and unstable nuclei, particularly their beta decay properties and structural characteristics. His research leverages radioactive ion beam facilities, especially the ISOLDE experiment at CERN, to investigate exotic nuclear systems including halo nuclei.

name: Maximilian Stritzinger
title: Associate Professor
summary: Maximilian Stritzinger studies transient stellar explosions, particularly supernovae associated with massive star deaths and white dwarf thermonuclear explosions.
