His research focuses on obtaining early observations of supernovae near explosion to understand explosion physics, identify progenitors, and use Type Ia supernovae as cosmological distance indicators.

name: Thomas Tram
title: Associate Professor
summary: Thomas Tram is a theoretical cosmologist who develops numerical tools and tests new cosmological models by calculating their effects on the cosmic microwave background and large-scale structure of the universe. His research focuses on neutrino physics, dark matter, and the relativistic effects in structure formation, with major contributions to codes like CLASS and LASAGNA.

name: Ulrik Ingerslev Uggerhøj
title: Head of Department
summary: Uggerhøj conducts experimental physics research primarily focused on strong field effects and radiation emission in extreme electromagnetic fields comparable to those on magnetar surfaces. His work involves experiments with ultrarelativistic particles and positron beams, primarily conducted at CERN's particle physics laboratory.

name: Søren Ulstrup
title: Associate Professor
summary: Søren Ulstrup is an experimental physicist who studies electronic properties of two-dimensional quantum materials and heterostructures using micro-focused angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (nanoARPES) at nanometer and femtosecond scales. His research focuses on visualizing and controlling electronic states in exotic materials like twisted graphene systems and transition metal dichalcogenides to understand and engineer quantum phenomena.

name: Simon Elliot Wall
title: Associate Professor
summary: Simon Elliot Wall uses femtosecond pulses of light across the entire electromagnetic spectrum (THz to hard X-rays) to manipulate and measure properties of quantum materials. His research focuses on understanding ultrafast dynamics and light-induced phase transitions in correlated materials, exploiting advanced techniques like X-ray free-electron lasers and time-resolved spectroscopy.

name: Nikolaj Thomas Zinner
title: Professor
summary: Nikolaj Thomas Zinner is a theoretical physicist working on quantum many-body systems, quantum control techniques, and quantum technologies. His research spans from few-body physics and strongly interacting ultracold atoms to quantum thermodynamics, quantum computing architectures (including superconducting qubits), and quantum control methods.

name: Merete Bilde
title: Professor
summary: Merete Bilde is a professor of atmospheric physical chemistry who studies how aerosols form, grow, and interact with water in the atmosphere. Her research combines experimental studies using an atmospheric simulation chamber (AURA) with computational modeling to understand aerosol formation from various sources including volatile organic compounds, sea spray, and microplastics, with applications to climate and cloud formation.

name: Henrik Birkedal
title: Professor
summary: Henrik Birkedal researches the structure and properties of biological materials, particularly bone and biominerals, using advanced X-ray and synchrotron imaging techniques. He also synthesizes bioinspired materials including mussel-inspired coacervates and studies crystal growth in confined environments.

name: Victoria Birkedal
title: Associate Professor
summary: Victoria Birkedal studies the structure and dynamics of nucleic acids and proteins using single-molecule fluorescence spectroscopy and FRET microscopy techniques. Her research focuses on understanding conformational changes in DNA and RNA structures, particularly G-quadruplexes and telomeres, and designing DNA-based nanostructures for nanotechnology applications.

name: Martin Bremholm
title: Associate Professor
summary: Martin Bremholm is an associate professor who specializes in high-pressure materials synthesis and crystallography to study novel compounds and their structure-property relationships. His research focuses on solid-state chemistry, crystal growth, phase transitions, topological materials, and electronic/magnetic properties, with significant work on in situ X-ray diffraction studies of synthesis processes.

name: Mogens Christensen
title: Professor
summary: Mogens Christensen studies magnetic materials across multiple length scales, focusing on designing efficient permanent magnets without rare earth elements through structural characterization and synthesis of nanocrystalline magnetic compounds. His research employs advanced diffraction techniques and in-situ methods to understand how crystallographic structure and nanoscale properties govern magnetic behavior.

name: Ove Christiansen
title: Professor
summary: Ove Christiansen is a theoretical chemist who develops and applies coupled cluster electronic structure methods and focuses on vibrational structure theory. His research combines advanced computational methods for describing molecular vibrations and electronic properties, including development of vibrational coupled cluster theory and applications to quantum computing for chemistry.

name: Kim Daasbjerg
title: Professor
summary: Kim Daasbjerg develops electrochemical methods to convert carbon dioxide into valuable chemicals, combining expertise in surface modification, polymer brushes, and graphene materials. His group focuses on designing electrode interfaces using polymer brush technology and theoretical modeling to improve CO2 reduction reaction selectivity and catalytic performance.

name: Anna Louise Duncan
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Anna Louise Duncan is a computational biophysicist who develops and applies molecular simulation methods to understand biological membranes and membrane proteins. Her research focuses on multiscale simulation approaches, including coarse-grained dynamics, to model membrane structure and protein-lipid interactions at different scales.

name: Jonas Elm
title: Associate Professor
summary: Jonas Elm is a computational chemist who studies atmospheric molecular cluster formation and new particle formation processes using quantum chemical methods. His research focuses on how sulfuric acid, bases, and organic compounds interact to form atmospheric nanoparticles that can grow into aerosols and cloud condensation nuclei.

name: Marianne Glasius
title: Associate Professor
summary: Marianne Glasius develops and applies advanced analytical methods to detect molecular tracers of chemical processes in atmospheric aerosol formation and hydrothermal liquefaction of biomass for biofuel development. Her research addresses air pollution, climate change, and alternative fuel development using mass spectrometry and chemical analysis to investigate organic compounds across diverse environmental systems.

name: Maarten Goesten
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Maarten Goesten is a theoretical chemist developing chemical blueprints for designing new superconductors using group theory and solid-state theory. His research combines computational methods with materials design, focusing on quantum materials for energy applications including superconductivity, topological properties, and exotic quantum states in metal-organic frameworks.

name: Kurt Vesterager Gothelf
title: Professor
summary: Kurt Gothelf develops functional nanostructures and therapeutic applications through DNA nanotechnology and organic chemistry. His research focuses on designing DNA-based scaffolds for drug delivery, biosensing, and programmable protein engineering, with applications in molecular electronics and biomedicines.

name: Nicole Maria Hauser
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Nicole Hauser works at the interface of organic synthesis and biocatalysis, focusing on using enzymes as catalysts to make pharmaceutical drug synthesis more efficient and sustainable. Her research spans from transition metal catalysis to understanding how microorganisms produce bioactive molecules.

name: Bo Brummerstedt Iversen
title: Professor
summary: Bo Brummerstedt Iversen is a materials chemist who uses advanced structural characterization techniques (synchrotron and neutron scattering) to understand how material properties relate to atomic structure, with applications to energy materials including thermoelectrics, catalysts, and biomass conversion.

name: Frank Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Frank Jensen is a computational chemist specializing in density functional theory, basis sets, and quantum chemical methods for predicting molecular structures and properties. His research also includes excited-state dynamics and photochemical processes in aqueous systems.

name: Henrik Helligsø Jensen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Henrik Helligsø Jensen is a bioorganic chemist specializing in carbohydrate and glycoside synthesis, particularly thioglycosides for oligosaccharide production and glycosylation chemistry with catalytic methods. His research encompasses organic synthesis methodology, fluorescent molecules, and bioactive carbohydrate derivatives for medicinal applications.

name: Torben René Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Torben René Jensen is a Professor of Inorganic Chemistry at Aarhus University specializing in the synthesis and characterization of inorganic energy materials, particularly complex metal hydrides and borohydrides for hydrogen storage and solid-state batteries. His research uses advanced techniques like synchrotron X-ray diffraction and synchrotron radiation to design novel materials for energy storage applications and gas separation.

name: Karl Anker Jørgensen
title: Professor
summary: Karl Anker Jørgensen is a leading researcher in asymmetric organocatalysis, focusing on developing chiral catalysts to control the formation of stereoisomers in organic synthesis. His work involves designing organocatalysts (particularly proline-derived) to enable highly selective cycloaddition reactions and enantioselective transformations for applications in pharmaceutical and fine chemical synthesis.

name: Mads Ry Vogel Jørgensen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Mads Ry Vogel Jørgensen is a materials researcher who develops and operates the DanMAX synchrotron beamline at MAX IV Laboratory, using intense X-ray beams for powder diffraction and total scattering to examine atomic structures of functional materials. His personal research focuses on piezoelectric and ferroelectric materials, employing advanced crystallographic methods including pair distribution function analysis to understand structure-property relationships.

name: Fabian Mahrt
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Fabian Mahrt is an experimental atmospheric scientist who studies how aerosol particle properties determine their ability to nucleate clouds and affect climate. His research focuses on ice nucleation, secondary organic aerosol phase behavior, and the chemical and physical properties of atmospheric particles using laboratory instrumentation.

name: Niels Christian Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Niels Christian Nielsen develops advanced magnetic resonance methods including NMR, DNP, EPR, and MRI for studying molecular structures and dynamics at atomic resolution. His research spans protein aggregation, amyloid fibrils, soft materials, membrane proteins, and materials chemistry using specialized pulse sequences and computational techniques.

name: Jan Skov Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Jan Skov Pedersen studies how molecules interact and self-assemble into higher-order structures using scattering techniques, with applications in understanding protein aggregation, complex surfactant systems, and designing responsive drug delivery systems.

name: Thomas Poulsen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Poulsen leads the Chemical Biology group at Aarhus University, using organic chemistry to synthesize complex bioactive molecules and study their biological effects through cell-based and biochemical approaches. His research focuses on developing electrophilic reactive groups and covalent tools for chemical biology applications, including natural product synthesis, ionophore chemistry, and proteomic target identification.

name: Dorthe Ravnsbæk
title: Professor
summary: Dorthe Ravnsbæk develops functional electrode materials for sustainable rechargeable batteries, focusing on understanding how nano- and atomic-scale structures of battery materials affect their electrochemical performance using operando X-ray scattering and spectroscopy methods.

name: Alonso Rosas-Hernández
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: He obtained his doctoral degree at the Leibniz Institute for Catalysis (LIKAT) in Germany focusing on molecular catalysts for the photoconversion of carbon dioxide into fuels and carried out a postdoctoral stay at the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) developing novel electrocatalytic systems for the valorization of carbon dioxide. His group aims to develop novel hybrid catalytic architectures for highly selective and efficient electrochemical conversion of methane to methanol under ambient conditions.

name: Jørgen Skibsted
title: Professor
summary: Jørgen Skibsted is a professor specializing in solid-state NMR spectroscopy applications for inorganic materials research, with primary focus on exploring structure and reactivity of cement-based materials to develop sustainable cement production and studying heterogeneous catalysts, zeolites, glasses, and hydrogen storage materials.

name: Troels Skrydstrup
title: Professor
summary: Skrydstrup's research develops catalytic transformations using gaseous building blocks like carbon monoxide, ethylene, and hydrogen cyanide to construct organic compounds through transition metal-catalyzed carbon-carbon and carbon-heteroatom bond formation. His group also focuses on CO₂ activation chemistry to exploit carbon dioxide as a valuable reagent for producing high-value chemicals.

name: Henrik Stapelfeldt
title: Professor
summary: Henrik Stapelfeldt studies the manipulation and control of molecules using short laser pulses, with a primary focus on aligning and imaging molecules embedded in superfluid helium nanodroplets using femtosecond spectroscopy and ultrafast imaging techniques.

name: Thibault Viennet
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Thibault Viennet uses solution NMR spectroscopy and complementary biophysical methods to study how phosphorylation and other post-translational modifications regulate protein function, particularly in intrinsically disordered regions of kinases and transcription factors. His research integrates structural biology, protein chemistry, and molecular dynamics to understand disease mechanisms and protein regulation at the atomic scale.

name: Thomas Vosegaard
title: Head of Department
summary: Thomas Vosegaard is a Professor at Aarhus University specializing in solid-state nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy to study membrane protein structure, dynamics, protein-lipid interactions, and cellular lipidomics. His work also includes developing advanced NMR methodologies and characterizing insoluble proteins and single-atom catalysts.

name: Shuai Wei
title: Associate Professor
summary: Shuai Wei studies novel amorphous materials (phase-change materials and metallic glasses) for technological applications including nonvolatile memory devices and structural materials. He uses synchrotron X-ray and neutron scattering techniques to investigate the atomic-scale structure and properties of amorphous solids and supercooled liquids.

name: Tobias Weidner
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tobias Weidner studies molecular structure, orientation, and dynamics at interfaces using spectroscopic methods, with particular focus on understanding how proteins behave at surfaces and applying this knowledge to design biomimetic materials and prevent biofouling.

name: Michael Westberg
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Michael Westberg is a biophysical chemist who designs light- and ligand-controlled protein tools by combining computational and experimental methods to understand and control interactions between proteins, small molecules, and light. His research develops novel protein-based tools for studying cell signaling dynamics and biological processes, with applications in chemical and synthetic biology.

name: Alexander Zelikin
title: Professor
summary: Alexander Zelikin develops advanced polymer-based drug delivery systems and biomaterials to address complex biomedical challenges, particularly focusing on antiviral therapies, site-specific drug delivery via implantable systems, and synthetic cell engineering with enzyme-catalyzed drug activation.

name: Diego Abalos
title: Professor
summary: Diego Abalos is a Professor at Aarhus University's Department of Agroecology who specializes in soil biogeochemistry and environmental impacts of agriculture. His research focuses on reducing greenhouse gas emissions (particularly N2O) and improving nutrient cycling through optimized crop and grassland management strategies, including plant trait selection, cover crop combinations, and soil organic matter dynamics.

name: Mathias Neumann Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Andersen is an agronomist specializing in plant physiology and crop production, with primary research focused on water balance in agro-ecosystems and how water availability affects plant physiology and crop yields. His work addresses irrigation efficiency, drought stress mechanisms, and sustainable water resource management in agriculture, with applications including remote sensing, biochar amendments, and deficit irrigation techniques.

name: Davide Cammarano
title: Professor
summary: Davide Cammarano is a professor of environmental crop science who specializes in precision agriculture, crop simulation modeling, and climate change impacts on agriculture. His recent research integrates sensing technologies and field-scale measurements to understand spatial and temporal variability in nitrogen management, with focus on reducing nitrogen leaching while maintaining crop productivity.

name: Iris Vogeler Cronin
title: Professor
summary: Iris Vogeler Cronin is a Professor of Soil Fertility at Aarhus University who specializes in using biophysical modelling and field experiments to understand nitrogen cycling, nutrient transport, and soil processes in agricultural systems, with a focus on reducing nitrate leaching through optimized crop rotations, cover crops, and sustainable farming practices.

name: Tommy Dalgaard
title: Professor
summary: Tommy Dalgaard is a professor specializing in sustainable agricultural systems, with focus on nitrogen management, greenhouse gas emissions, and environmental performance of farming. His research integrates life cycle assessment, ecosystem services, and landscape-scale sustainability to address agricultural environmental challenges and develop climate-smart farming practices.

name: Lis Wollesen de Jonge
title: Professor
summary: Lis Wollesen de Jonge is a Professor of Soil Physics at Aarhus University specializing in soil physicochemical processes, particularly soil water dynamics, transport properties, and the development of spectroscopic methods for soil characterization. Her research encompasses soil structure, colloid transport, macropore flow, and the application of visible near-infrared spectroscopy for rapid soil property prediction.

name: Lars Elsgaard
title: Professor
summary: Lars Elsgaard is a Professor of microbial ecology at Aarhus University studying how agricultural and soil management practices affect greenhouse gas emissions and carbon sequestration. His research focuses on soil microbial processes controlling CO₂, CH₄, and N₂O emissions, soil organic carbon storage, biochar applications, and sustainable practices like peatland restoration and nitrogen management in agricultural systems.

name: Jørgen Eriksen
title: Professor
summary: Jørgen Eriksen is Head of the Soil Fertility Section at Aarhus University's Department of Agroecology, focusing on soil nutrient dynamics, carbon cycling, and grassland productivity. His research investigates how plant diversity, soil management systems, and nutrient turnover affect sustainable agricultural production and environmental quality.

name: Mo Bahram
title: Professor
summary: Mo Bahram is a microbial ecologist studying soil microbiome structure, function, and diversity across ecosystems using molecular and metagenomic approaches. His research focuses on understanding how microbial communities—particularly fungi and bacteria—drive ecosystem functioning and nutrient cycling in soils globally.

name: Birte Boelt
title: Professor
summary: Birte Boelt is a professor specializing in seed science, production, and technology, with expertise in developing innovative non-destructive methods (NIR spectroscopy and multispectral imaging) for seed quality assessment and viability testing. Her research focuses on optimizing seed yield in forage grasses and understanding plant-environment interactions to improve sustainable agricultural practices.

name: Henrik Brinch-Pedersen
title: Professor
summary: Henrik Brinch-Pedersen is a professor heading the Crop Genetics and Biotechnology section at Aarhus University, focusing on improving cereals through genetic transformation and gene editing methods. His research emphasizes developing transgenic wheat and barley with enhanced nutrient content, particularly phytase expression for improved mineral bioavailability and phosphate management.

name: René Gislum
title: Associate Professor
summary: René Gislum is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University who specializes in sensor-based crop monitoring and nutrient management, particularly in grass seed crops. His research combines remote sensing technology, chemometrics, and machine learning to optimize nitrogen supply and assess crop physiological traits, with applications in precision agriculture and sustainable crop production.

name: Mogens Støvring Hovmøller
title: Professor
summary: Mogens Støvring Hovmøller is a professor of plant pathology focusing on pathogenic fungi affecting agricultural crops, particularly rust fungi on cereals and grasses. He leads the Global Rust Reference Center at Aarhus University and conducts research on disease epidemiology, population biology, evolutionary dynamics of crop pathogens, and resistance breeding strategies with global food security implications.

name: Christopher James Barnes
title: Associate Professor
summary: Christopher James Barnes is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University working on the co-evolution between plants and their microbiomes, particularly in crop systems. His research combines molecular microbiology, genomics, and plant physiology to understand how plants select and shape their associated microbial communities, with applications toward developing more sustainable agriculture.

name: Kim Hebelstrup
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kim Hebelstrup is a molecular biologist and crop biotechnologist specializing in genetic modification and selective breeding of staple crops, particularly focusing on developing high-amylose varieties of cereals and potatoes for enhanced nutritional and industrial applications. His research includes de novo domestication of wild crop species and precision breeding to improve crop traits such as starch composition and disease resistance.

name: Isaac Kwesi Abuley
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Isaac Kwesi Abuley is a plant pathologist specializing in sustainable disease management for arable crops, with primary focus on potato diseases including late blight and early blight. His research spans disease epidemiology, pathogen characterization, fungicide resistance, disease forecasting, integrated pest management, and cultural/physiological approaches to reduce fungicide dependence.

name: Daniel Buchvaldt Amby
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Daniel Buchvaldt Amby is a plant pathologist at Aarhus University who focuses on understanding microbial pathogens and their interactions with agricultural crops, particularly minor crops like quinoa and strawberry, using molecular and morphological diagnostic methods.

name: Benjamin Fuchs
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Benjamin Fuchs is a plant ecologist specializing in chemical ecology of crop interactions with microbes and insects, using LC-MS and GC-MS analysis to identify phytochemical drivers that improve sustainable crop production and reduce pesticide dependency. His research focuses on grass-endophyte symbioses, plant defense mechanisms, and how soil pesticide residues disrupt plant-insect signaling in agricultural ecosystems.

name: Jawameer Hama
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Jawameer Hama studies plant secondary metabolites and their roles in ecological interactions, particularly how plants defend against soil pests like root-knot nematodes. His research focuses on chemical defense mechanisms in plants and their sustainable applications to reduce pesticide dependency in agriculture.

name: Tobias Hanak
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Tobias Hanak develops genome editing approaches to re-domesticate crop wild relatives, particularly barley, using CRISPR technologies to create climate-resilient crop varieties for improved food security.

name: Christian Dold
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Christian Dold is a soil fertility researcher specializing in measuring and modeling greenhouse gas emissions (particularly nitrous oxide) from agricultural systems, with focus on soil-atmosphere interactions, crop productivity monitoring, and developing field measurement infrastructure to support sustainable agriculture and climate change mitigation.

name: Kirsten Lønne Enggrob
title: Tenure Track Assistant Professor
summary: Kirsten Lønne Enggrob uses advanced isotopic methods (SIP and CSIA) to study how plants, microbes, and soil interact in nutrient cycling systems. Her research focuses on improving agricultural sustainability in Northern Europe through understanding soil organic nitrogen dynamics, legume-based cropping systems, and how grassland biodiversity affects carbon and nitrogen transformations.

name: Klaus Butterbach-Bahl
title: Centre Director
summary: Klaus Butterbach-Bahl is a biogeochemist who studies greenhouse gas emissions from agricultural systems, focusing on nitrous oxide and methane fluxes from soils, and develops modeling approaches and mitigation strategies for sustainable agriculture and climate impact reduction.

name: Amélie Marie Beucher
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Amélie Marie Beucher is an Assistant Professor in Soil Physics and Hydropedology who specializes in digital soil mapping using machine learning and deep learning methods. Her research focuses on mapping soil properties and classes (particularly acid sulfate soils and peatlands), predicting soil hydrological characteristics, and developing explainable AI techniques for soil classification and carbon sequestration assessment.

name: Mohit Masta
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mohit Masta is an assistant professor at Aarhus University focused on understanding nitrous oxide (N2O) production and consumption mechanisms in agricultural soils using stable isotope analysis, functional gene analysis, and high-resolution gas emission measurements to develop strategies for climate change mitigation.

name: Claus Rasmussen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Claus Rasmussen is an entomologist studying the ecology and evolution of bees (particularly Hymenoptera), focusing on understanding and sustaining biological diversity through pollinator ecology, crop pollination, and biodiversity conservation in agricultural landscapes. His research emphasizes ecological relationships shaped by evolutionary history, functional traits, and applies phylogenetics and network theory to address insect-plant interaction questions.

name: Christopher John Topping
title: Professor
summary: Christopher John Topping is a professor who develops and applies simulation models for understanding agricultural ecosystems and environmental impacts of pesticides, with particular focus on pollinator health, beneficial organisms, and wildlife in agricultural landscapes. He leads the Social-Ecological Systems Simulation centre and maintains the widely-used Animal Landscape and Man Simulation System (ALMaSS) platform.

name: Christoffer Green-Pedersen
title: Head of Department
summary: Christoffer Green-Pedersen is a professor of political science at Aarhus University who specializes in how political parties compete through agenda-setting and how this affects policy decisions on issues like immigration, welfare reform, and morality policies. He founded the Danish Policy Agendas Project and is a co-initiator of the Comparative Agendas Project, focusing on party politics and policy dynamics in comparative perspective, particularly in Scandinavian and Western European contexts.

name: Lene Aarøe
title: Professor
summary: Lene Aarøe is a political psychology researcher who combines political science with psychology, biology, neuroscience, and communication methods to understand how citizens form political opinions. Her work uses laboratory experiments, physiological data, and social media analysis to investigate how biological factors, psychological biases, and narrative exemplars shape democratic attitudes and resilience to political misinformation.

name: Laurits Florang Aarslew
title: Assistant Professor
summary: An Assistant Professor specializing in public opinion and its role in shaping regime dynamics. His research examines the consequences of electoral malpractice for public opinion and investigates methodological issues such as social desirability biases in survey instruments capturing democratic attitudes.

name: Nanna Vestergaard Ahrensberg
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Ahrensberg researches how citizens with immigrant backgrounds interact with public administration, particularly schools, and how education and integration policies affect civic outcomes and political participation. Her work includes field experiments testing implementation of evidence-based interventions and qualitative studies on political engagement among immigrant and refugee populations.

name: David Delfs Erbo Andersen
title: Associate Professor
summary: David Delfs Erbo Andersen studies the causes of democratization and democratic stability, examining how state capacity and bureaucratic quality—particularly meritocratic recruitment and impartial administration—affect democratic resilience and prevent regime breakdown. He specializes in comparative historical-institutional analysis with focus on Scandinavian political development.

name: Jens Peter Andersen
title: Senior Researcher
summary: Jens Peter Andersen analyzes sociological trends and inequalities in the scientific system using bibliometric methods, social network analysis, and natural language processing to examine gender differences and elite formation in academia.

name: Lotte Bøgh Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Lotte Bøgh Andersen investigates leadership, motivation, and performance of public employees and managers, focusing on how economic incentives, professional norms, and leadership strategies affect behavior in public organizations. She leads the LEAP project, a large-scale field experiment testing the causal effects of leadership training on organizational performance.

name: Simon Calmar Andersen
title: Professor
summary: Simon Calmar Andersen is a professor at Aarhus University who examines the effects of education policies and interventions through randomized field experiments, studying topics including instruction time, teacher resources, parental engagement, performance management, and how organizational contexts affect both student outcomes and public sector performance.

name: Andreas Bengtson
title: Associate Professor
summary: Andreas Bengtson is an Associate Professor in political philosophy at Aarhus University whose research focuses on democracy and relational egalitarianism, with particular attention to questions of justice, equality in political relationships, and policy areas including affirmative action, paternalism, and the role of wealth in democratic institutions.

name: Anne Skorkjær Binderkrantz
title: Professor
summary: Anne Skorkjær Binderkrantz is a professor of comparative politics whose research centers on the political role of interest groups, particularly how organized interests gain access and influence across different political arenas—including parliaments, bureaucracies, media, and regulatory bodies. She has conducted extensive cross-national research examining interest group strategies, media attention patterns, and corporatist structures in democratic systems.

name: Martin Bisgaard
title: Associate Professor
summary: Martin Bisgaard's research sits at the intersection of political behavior, political psychology, and political representation, focusing on the electoral link between citizens and politicians. His current project deals with how politicians think about voter behavior.

name: Carter Walter Bloch
title: Centre Director
summary: Carter Bloch is Centre Director of the Danish Centre for Studies in Research and Research Policy at Aarhus University. His research focuses on research evaluation, the effects of research funding on academic careers and research outputs, the relationship between research and innovation, science policy, and the economic analysis of R&D systems.

name: Jens Blom-Hansen
title: Professor
summary: Jens Blom-Hansen studies EU political-administrative systems, focusing on the European Commission's role in policy-making, intergovernmental relations, and the mechanisms through which EU delegated powers are controlled through committees and inter-institutional procedures.

name: Troels Bøggild
title: Associate Professor
summary: Troels Bøggild is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University who studies political behavior, opinion formation, and political trust. His research focuses on how citizens' psychological biases shape political attitudes, including the causes of affective polarization, the spread of political distrust through social networks, and how political incivility affects democratic satisfaction.

name: Morten Brænder
title: Associate Professor
summary: Morten Brænder studies why soldiers serve and how military deployment affects their motivation, leadership values, and engagement. His research integrates military sociology with investigations into the relationship between religion and politics, examining soldier motivation, professional military development, and the role of religious justification in military service.

name: Love Aksel Christensen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Love Aksel Christensen is an assistant professor of political science conducting research on behavioral political economy with a focus on how uncertainty and persuasion influence political attitudes and behavior, combining experimental methods with theoretical reasoning.

name: Peter Munk Christiansen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Munk Christiansen studies how interest groups influence political decision-making, particularly through their access to administrative, parliamentary, and media channels, and examines the organization of political advice and elite networks in Scandinavian democracies.

name: Derek Beach
title: Professor
summary: Derek Beach develops process tracing methodology for studying causal mechanisms in social science research and policy evaluation. His substantive research focuses on European integration, including how the EU manages crises, voting behavior in EU referendums, and international negotiations within EU institutions.

name: Daniel Finke
title: Professor
summary: Daniel Finke is a professor of comparative politics at Aarhus University who studies decision-making in the European Union and international organizations, with particular focus on how agenda-setting, voting behavior, and parliamentary scrutiny mechanisms shape legislative outcomes in the EU.

name: Marion Kathe Godman
title: Associate Professor
summary: Marion Kathe Godman is a philosopher working on the ontology, epistemology, and normative implications of human kinds—how people are scientifically categorized—with particular focus on gender, ethnicity, and race. Her research bridges philosophy of biology and political philosophy to examine how historical and cultural reproduction shapes group membership, discrimination, and the moral basis for group rights like affirmative action and minority protections.

name: Johan Gøtzsche-Astrup
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Johan Gøtzsche-Astrup studies how activism, protests, and riots are ascribed meaning within liberal democracies using political and cultural sociological approaches. His research examines democratic truth-telling practices, the boundary between legitimate protest and riots, and how the public interprets contentious action.

name: Daniel Sandvej Eriksen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Daniel Sandvej Eriksen studies how political parties set agendas and strategically communicate through his Issue Initiation Model. His research analyzes millions of social media posts, news articles, and parliamentary questions from Denmark and the UK to understand how parties can proactively initiate and elevate policy issues to influence media and other political actors.

name: Nicholas Haas
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nicholas Haas investigates causes, consequences, and solutions to intergroup inequality, polarization, and conflict, with particular focus on how group identity shapes treatment of marginalized populations. He employs experimental and statistical methods combined with fieldwork and behavioral insights to examine topics like political ideology in developing countries, conflict resolution, and mechanisms of social cohesion.

name: Aske Halling
title: Assistant Professor
summary: His research focuses on administrative burdens, discrimination, and street-level bureaucracy. He is interested in administrative burdens, citizen-state interactions, and policy feedbacks.

name: Christian Bøtcher Jacobsen
title: Professor
summary: Christian Bøtcher Jacobsen is a professor of political science researching public leadership and administration, with particular focus on the health sector. His work examines employee motivation, leadership behaviors, organizational performance, and how public managers can effectively influence employee engagement and well-being through quantitative methods and large-scale field experiments.

name: Mads Leth Jakobsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Mads Leth Jakobsen studies public leadership, governance, and organizational dynamics in complex public sector settings, with particular focus on how leadership approaches, performance systems, and work conditions affect employee behavior and organizational outcomes in public organizations like hospitals.

name: Morten Jakobsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Morten Jakobsen studies citizens' relationships with the public sector, examining how public service organization influences citizen participation, political engagement, and policy outcomes through randomized field experiments and empirical research.

name: Carsten Jensen
title: Professor
summary: Carsten Jensen is a professor studying comparative politics in Western democracies, focusing on how political parties shape welfare state policies, the electoral consequences of policy reforms, and the relationship between politics and inequality across advanced democracies.

name: Lars Vinther Johannsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lars Vinther Johannsen is an associate professor at Aarhus University who studies political development, with particular emphasis on corruption, state capacity, democratization, and populism in post-communist countries, especially the Baltic states and Slovenia.

name: Simon Tobias Schulz Karg
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Simon Karg is an assistant professor at Aarhus University whose research examines online political hostility and prosocial responses on social media using computational methods and experimental psychology. His work investigates moral behavior, bystander interventions in online contexts, and how people respond to political hostility on social platforms.

name: Florian Keppeler
title: Associate Professor
summary: Florian Keppeler is an Associate Professor at Aarhus University who researches public administration, policy, and human resource management in the public sector, with particular focus on recruitment, selection, and retention of public employees. He conducts large-scale field experiments examining how artificial intelligence, employer branding, and behavioral interventions affect public sector hiring and policy outcomes.

name: Anne Mette Kjær
title: Professor
summary: Anne Mette Kjær is a professor of political science at Aarhus University who has researched governance and political economy in East Africa for 25 years, with particular focus on Uganda. Her research examines how political institutions and power dynamics shape policy implementation across sectors including land, agriculture, education, and food security.

name: Anne Mette Hjortskov Kjeldsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Anne Mette Kjeldsen is an associate professor researching public administration and leadership, with a focus on how public service motivation, job satisfaction, and distributed leadership affect employees in healthcare, education, and social services. Her work uses field experiments and surveys to examine leadership training effectiveness and organizational change in public sector organizations.

name: Tonny Brems Knudsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Tonny Brems Knudsen studies international relations with a focus on humanitarian intervention, international organizations like the UN and ICC, and how primary and secondary institutions shape world order and great power management in an evolving multipolar world.

name: Karoline Larsen Kolstad
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Karoline Larsen Kolstad studies how bureaucratic workload and organizational factors affect public service delivery and discriminatory treatment of citizens. Her research uses register data from Danish administrative systems to examine real-world interactions between government agencies and citizens, particularly focusing on employment services and housing policy.

name: Ane Edslev Konradsen
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Ane Edslev Konradsen researches political economy and state-society relations in Sub-Saharan Africa, focusing on taxation, protest, and conflict in informal economies using ethnographic methods. Currently, she investigates food system governance and security in the Uganda-Tanzania border region.

name: Suthan Krishnarajan
title: Associate Professor
summary: Krishnarajan studies why citizens in Western democracies support undemocratic leaders and accept undemocratic behavior, using experimental methods including surveys across multiple countries. His current research focuses on how democratic advertisements can strengthen citizens' democratic values and commitment to democratic principles.

name: Mathias Kruse
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mathias Kruse is an assistant professor studying group relations in diverse societies through the lens of immigration. His research examines how exposure to in- and outgroup members during childhood affects attitudes and behavior, investigating causal mechanisms of identity-based biases and outcomes like well-being and discrimination.

name: Lars Thorup Larsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lars Thorup Larsen studies comparative health policy, professional authority, and citizens' resistance to knowledge authorities. His research spans political sociology, examining how populist attitudes affect expert acceptance, morality politics across Western nations, and the historical development of health policy ideas.

name: Martin Vinæs Larsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Martin Vinæs Larsen studies the political causes and consequences of geographic inequality in housing prices, focusing on how housing markets influence electoral behavior, support for populist parties, and opposition to urban development. His research explores the politics of housing affordability, local land-use policy, and the political power of homeowners versus renters in shaping policy outcomes.

name: Carsten Bagge Laustsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Carsten Bagge Laustsen is a political sociologist who studies the intersection of politics, religion, violence, and culture. His research employs poststructuralist theory to examine contemporary political phenomena including terrorism, ideology, biopolitics, and the relationship between cultural expressions and political violence.

name: Lasse Laustsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Lasse Laustsen investigates how evolutionary psychology and cognitive biases shape political behavior, particularly voters' candidate preferences and discriminatory attitudes. He integrates social, cognitive, and evolutionary psychology to study voter responses to leader traits, facial appearance, political emotions, and redistribution attitudes using experimental methods.

name: Lasse Egendal Leipziger
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Leipziger is an Assistant Professor studying the relationships between democracy, inequality, and ethnic politics. His research uses cross-national statistical methods, comparative case studies, and experimental designs to examine how democratic institutions affect socioeconomic inequalities between ethnic groups and how ethnic conflicts influence regime change.

name: Lasse Lindekilde
title: Professor
summary: Lasse Lindekilde is a political science professor studying political violence, radicalization, and online hostility. His current research focuses on how bystanders react to online political aggression and how pro-social reactions can mitigate its harmful effects through behavioral experiments and intervention campaigns.

name: Kasper Lippert-Rasmussen
title: Professor
summary: Kasper Lippert-Rasmussen is a political philosopher who primarily investigates discrimination—what it is and what makes it wrong—using experimental methods. His broader research encompasses relational egalitarianism, luck egalitarianism, the ethics of blame, and applied questions like affirmative action and healthcare justice.

name: Clara Siboni Lund
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Clara Siboni Lund researches professional development leadership in public organizations, examining how managers can align organizational goals with professional norms while maintaining motivation and performance of public professionals. Her work encompasses leadership practices in healthcare and social services, technology adoption in public administration, and the tension between managerial and professional logics.

name: Jørgen Møller
title: Professor
summary: Jørgen Møller is a professor of political science at Aarhus University whose research focuses on democratic theory, democratization processes, and state formation in Europe using comparative-historical methods. His work examines regime transitions, democratic resilience, the development of parliamentary institutions, and the historical roots of state governance through the study of medieval religious and political institutions.

name: Peter Bjerre Mortensen
title: Professor
summary: Peter Bjerre Mortensen is a professor who studies policy stability and change, focusing on how political parties compete over the political agenda and the relationship between political leadership and public administration, particularly in Denmark's central government.

name: Clara Neupert-Wentz
title: Associate Professor
summary: Clara Neupert-Wentz studies how political institutions—particularly traditional authorities and customary systems—affect conflict and violence in Africa. Her research examines the relationship between gender practices like polygyny, institutional arrangements, and intergroup conflict, using georeferenced data and expert surveys to understand mechanisms of communal violence and peace.

name: Vibeke Lehmann Nielsen
title: Professor
summary: Vibeke Lehmann Nielsen studies how street-level bureaucrats implement public policy and how citizens respond to interactions with public authorities, with recent interests in regulatory compliance, administrative burden, and human-AI collaboration in public sector decision-making.

name: Tore Vincents Olsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Olsen studies how liberal democracies defend themselves against populist political forces, examining legal, political, cultural and economic mechanisms deployed by various actors including authorities, civil society, and supranational organizations. His research also covers political theory related to toleration, recognition, multiculturalism, and citizenship, with applications to immigration and integration policy.

name: Thomas Olesen
title: Professor
summary: Thomas Olesen is a professor of political sociology who studies the role of journalists, whistleblowers, and activists in democratic societies, with particular focus on social movements, global solidarity networks, and the politics of disclosure and transparency.

name: Poul Aaes Nielsen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Nielsen studies how public organizations use data and performance measurements to facilitate organizational learning and decision-making. His research examines how performance information affects public managers, employees, elected politicians, and organizational outcomes through experimental and behavioral approaches.

name: Viki Lyngby Hvid
title: Associate Professor
summary: Viki Lyngby Hvid is an associate professor who researches discrimination and paternalism within moral, political, and experimental philosophy, using empirical vignette-based experiments to examine how people judge the moral wrongness of discriminatory acts and policies.

name: Martin Bækgaard
title: Professor
summary: Martin Bækgaard researches the intersection of public administration and political science, with particular focus on administrative burden—the psychological and practical costs citizens face when interacting with government—and how politicians and bureaucrats make decisions regarding policy implementation and service delivery.

name: William Kjærgaard Egendal
title: Assistant Professor
summary: William Kjærgaard Egendal studies EU political processes, particularly focusing on negotiations and the role of institutional actors and informal networks. His work employs political ethnography to examine how EU officials and member state representatives navigate complex policy negotiations during crises, such as fishing quota negotiations during COVID-19 and Brexit.

name: Mads Fuglsang Hove
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Mads Fuglsang Hove is an Assistant Professor of Political Science at Aarhus University who researches politics on social media, particularly examining how political campaigns use microtargeting on platforms like Facebook and Instagram to communicate with voters, and studying broader patterns of political polarization and misinformation in digital environments.

name: Kristian Frederiksen
title: Associate Professor
summary: Kristian Frederiksen studies why citizens support undemocratic politicians and how political institutions shape this support, with research focusing on democratic erosion and democratic backsliding across multiple countries using experimental methods.

name: Niels Nyholt
title: Assistant Professor
summary: Niels Nyholt studies how local geographic contexts and local political decisions shape voter behavior and electoral outcomes. His research examines topics including voters' preferences for local candidates, electoral responses to school and hospital closures, and opposition to housing development projects, using surveys, experiments, and quasi-experimental methods.
